You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_55.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 55: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[55]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_55.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 55: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""U11 (I-deepfix-001) — clinical evidence-type query expansion (T1/T2 RECALL weight).
2	
3	Clinical runs starve high-tier evidence (T1 randomized controlled trials /
4	systematic reviews / meta-analyses, T2 clinical practice guidelines) because the
5	sub-queries fired at the search backends (Serper / S2 / OpenAlex / Europe PMC) are
6	plain natural language with NO evidence-type targeting. The candidate pool is then
7	dominated by generic web pages and the primary literature ranks below the fold.
8	
9	This module ADDS a small, bounded set of evidence-type-targeted VARIANTS of the
10	anchor query (e.g. ``"<q> randomized controlled trial"``,
11	``"<q> systematic review meta-analysis"``, ``"<q> clinical practice guideline"``)
12	so the high-tier literature SURFACES in the search results.
13	
14	CLAUDE.md §-1.3 (WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER): expansion is purely ADDITIVE. It appends
15	discovery queries; it NEVER drops, caps, thins, or filters a source, and it never
16	targets a breadth NUMBER. The original NL queries still fire unchanged, and every
17	added candidate flows through the SAME fetch -> tier -> strict_verify / NLI /
18	provenance chokepoint as every other source. The faithfulness engine is untouched.
19	
20	Deterministic, no LLM, no network — a pure string transform (reproducible).
21	
22	All knobs are env vars (LAW VI): ``PG_EVIDENCE_TYPE_QUERY_EXPANSION`` (kill switch,
23	default OFF => byte-identical) and ``PG_EVIDENCE_TYPE_QUERY_TERMS`` (comma list of
24	evidence-type qualifiers, overrides the clinical defaults).
25	"""
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import logging
29	import os
30	from typing import Iterable
31	
32	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.evidence_type_query_expansion")
33	
34	# The high-tier clinical evidence types the expansion targets, in GRADE order
35	# (RCT / systematic review + meta-analysis / practice guideline). These qualifiers
36	# are appended to the anchor query so the search engines rank the primary
37	# literature that carries them above generic web.
38	_DEFAULT_CLINICAL_EVIDENCE_TYPE_TERMS: tuple[str, ...] = (
39	    "randomized controlled trial",
40	    "systematic review meta-analysis",
41	    "clinical practice guideline",
42	)
43	
44	
45	def evidence_type_query_expansion_enabled() -> bool:
46	    """True iff the U11 evidence-type expansion flag is ON.
47	
48	    Default OFF: unset / empty => no expansion and the effective query list is
49	    byte-identical to before this wiring.
50	    """
51	    return os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_TYPE_QUERY_EXPANSION", "").strip().lower() in {
52	        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
53	    }
54	
55	
56	def _evidence_type_terms() -> tuple[str, ...]:
57	    """Parse ``PG_EVIDENCE_TYPE_QUERY_TERMS`` into the qualifier tuple.
58	
59	    Format: ``randomized controlled trial,systematic review,practice guideline``.
60	    Unset / empty / all-blank => the clinical defaults. Malformed (all-blank
61	    entries) never crash: blanks are skipped and the defaults backstop an empty
62	    result (fail-safe, LAW II).
63	    """
64	    raw = os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_TYPE_QUERY_TERMS", "").strip()
65	    if not raw:
66	        return _DEFAULT_CLINICAL_EVIDENCE_TYPE_TERMS
67	    terms = tuple(t.strip() for t in raw.split(",") if t.strip())
68	    return terms or _DEFAULT_CLINICAL_EVIDENCE_TYPE_TERMS
69	
70	
71	def expand_evidence_type_queries(
72	    base_queries: Iterable[str],
73	    *,
74	    clinical: bool,
75	    enabled: bool | None = None,
76	    terms: Iterable[str] | None = None,
77	) -> list[str]:
78	    """Return ``base_queries`` UNCHANGED, plus evidence-type-targeted variants.
79	
80	    A bounded set of variants is appended for the ANCHOR query (the first
81	    non-empty base query) — one per high-tier evidence type — so the search
82	    backends surface RCTs / systematic reviews / meta-analyses / guidelines that
83	    a plain NL query buries. Expansion is anchor-only so the added query count is
84	    bounded (it does not multiply with the sub-query count) and each backend
85	    round-trip stays affordable.
86	
87	    Args:
88	        base_queries: the effective query list already compiled upstream.
89	        clinical: whether this is a clinical-domain run. Expansion fires ONLY
90	            when this is True — non-clinical runs are returned unchanged.
91	        enabled: kill-switch override (defaults to the env knob). When falsy the
92	            input is returned unchanged (byte-identical).
93	        terms: evidence-type qualifier override (defaults to the env knob).
94	
95	    Returns:
96	        A NEW list: the original queries in their original order, followed by the
97	        de-duplicated (case-insensitive) evidence-type variants. Never drops or
98	        reorders the originals (WEIGHT-not-filter, §-1.3).
99	    """
100	    out = [q for q in base_queries]
101	    if enabled is None:
102	        enabled = evidence_type_query_expansion_enabled()
103	    if not enabled or not clinical:
104	        return out
105	    if terms is None:
106	        terms = _evidence_type_terms()
107	
108	    anchor = ""
109	    for q in out:
110	        if isinstance(q, str) and q.strip():
111	            anchor = q.strip()
112	            break
113	    if not anchor:
114	        # No usable anchor text (empty / non-string queries) — nothing to expand.
115	        return out
116	
117	    seen = {q.strip().lower() for q in out if isinstance(q, str)}
118	    added = 0
119	    for term in terms:
120	        term = term.strip()
121	        if not term:
122	            continue
123	        variant = f"{anchor} {term}"
124	        key = variant.lower()
125	        if key in seen:
126	            continue
127	        seen.add(key)
128	        out.append(variant)
129	        added += 1
130	
131	    if added:
132	        logger.info(
133	            "[evidence_type_query_expansion] added %d clinical evidence-type "
134	            "sub-queries off anchor %r",
135	            added, anchor[:80],
136	        )
137	    return out
138

1	"""R1 (I-deepfix-001, #1344) — LLM expert-facet planner widens the query frontier.
2	
3	THE GAP (measured, drb_72 SWEEP_...1783055694): FS-Researcher issued only 7 of 35 budgeted
4	queries. The adaptive checklist critic stops at the first ``NONE``; each sub-topic yields exactly
5	ONE query; ``query_decomposer.decompose_question`` returns ``[]`` for a concise question. The
6	frontier is therefore built from the question's own words, not the ~53 expert facets a DRB-II task
7	actually spans. Recall starves before selection even runs.
8	
9	THE FIX: expand the question into a hierarchical FACET TREE (sub-topics, actors/stakeholders,
10	time-slices, geographies, mechanisms, opposing views) with ONE bounded LLM call, then multiply each
11	facet into several distinct facet-ANGLE queries (mechanism / stakeholder / counter-evidence /
12	temporal / geographic vantage points) by DETERMINISTIC templating. Every emitted query carries the
13	question's own subject keywords as a scope anchor, so a facet never generalises into its broad field
14	(the exact drb_72-v2 drift the sibling ``_scope_anchored`` guard in ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` was
15	built to stop). The deterministic split of the question stays as a FLOOR so a degenerate LLM reply
16	never shrinks the frontier below the legacy behaviour.
17	
18	DNA (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE): this is pure query-FRONTIER expansion. It adds on-topic queries
19	only; it DROPS ZERO sources; it touches no tiering / selection / consolidation / strict_verify / NLI
20	/ 4-role / provenance gate. ``PG_EXPERT_FACET_MAX_FACETS`` and the FS-Researcher ``_max_queries`` cap
21	are compute-SAFETY bounds (they cap cost — the opposite of the banned pattern, which forces a breadth
22	number UP); no forced cap / target / thinner / breadth-canary is introduced. Default OFF
23	(``PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER``) => the FS-Researcher path is byte-identical to today.
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import os
29	import re
30	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
31	from typing import Any, Callable
32	
33	# (prompt) -> text. The already-picked FS-Researcher / intent-frame policy model, injected by the
34	# caller (async client wrapped to sync) so this module never imports a live client at load time.
35	LlmFn = Callable[[str], str]
36	
37	# The facet-angle vantage points. Each is a short lens phrase spliced into the query alongside the
38	# facet name and the question's own subject keywords (the scope anchor). Deterministic — one bounded
39	# LLM call produces the facet tree; the angles multiply it at $0. The order is stable so the emitted
40	# frontier is reproducible.
41	_ANGLE_LENSES: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
42	    ("mechanism", "how it works mechanism drivers"),
43	    ("stakeholder", "who is affected stakeholders impact"),
44	    ("counter_evidence", "criticism limitations counter-evidence dissent"),
45	    ("temporal", "recent trends over time change"),
46	    ("geographic", "regional differences across countries"),
47	)
48	
49	
50	def expert_facet_enabled() -> bool:
51	    """True iff the R1 expert-facet planner is flag-enabled (default OFF = legacy FS-Researcher).
52	
53	    LAW VI env kill-switch. Default OFF keeps the production sweep byte-identical until the fresh
54	    paid run that validates the widened frontier; the tests exercise the ON path explicitly.
55	    """
56	    return os.getenv("PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True")
57	
58	
59	def _max_facets() -> int:
60	    """Max facets kept from the facet tree. A compute-safety CAP on cost (facets x angles x fetch),
61	    NOT a breadth target — it bounds the frontier UP-side, it never forces a number up (§-1.3)."""
62	    return max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_EXPERT_FACET_MAX_FACETS", "12")))
63	
64	
65	def _angles_per_facet() -> int:
66	    """How many facet-angle vantage queries to emit per facet (<= the number of defined lenses).
67	    Another compute-safety bound on cost, defaulting to the full lens set."""
68	    n = int(os.getenv("PG_EXPERT_FACET_ANGLES", str(len(_ANGLE_LENSES))))
69	    return max(1, min(n, len(_ANGLE_LENSES)))
70	
71	
72	# ── I-deepfix-001 Wave-2d (#1344) — TWO-SIDED DEBATE con-side retrieval guarantee ────────────────
73	#
74	# DeepTRACE One-Sided (#1) / Overconfident (#2): a DEBATE-framed question ("benefits and risks",
75	# "pros and cons", "positive vs negative") that renders only the majority side scores one-sided. The
76	# ``counter_evidence`` lens (``_ANGLE_LENSES[2]``) already sources the con angle for every facet, but a
77	# reduced ``PG_EXPERT_FACET_ANGLES`` budget (e.g. 2) would truncate it away. When ``PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE``
78	# is ON AND the question is debate-framed, GUARANTEE the ``counter_evidence`` lens is emitted regardless
79	# of the angle budget so the con side is actually retrieved. Pure ADDITIVE widener (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-
80	# FILTER): it adds on-topic con-angle queries, drops zero sources, caps nothing. Default OFF =>
81	# ``debate_active`` is False => ``_ANGLE_LENSES[:n_angles]`` exactly as today => byte-identical.
82	_COUNTER_EVIDENCE_LABEL = "counter_evidence"
83	
84	# Precision-first debate-framing detector. Each pattern is an EXPLICIT opposition pair (pro/con,
85	# benefits/risks, advantages/disadvantages, positive/negative, for/against, supporters/critics, ...)
86	# joined by ``and`` / ``or`` / ``vs`` / ``versus``, OR a standalone debate word (debate / controversy /
87	# contested / both-sides). A BARE ``A vs B`` comparison is DELIBERATELY excluded — a head-to-head
88	# comparison is not a pro/con debate; over-firing would emit a spurious asymmetry note on a plain
89	# comparison section (under-relax is safe, but a wrong note is noise), so only KNOWN opposition pairs
90	# license the ``vs`` form.
91	_DEBATE_CONNECTIVE = r"(?:and|or|vs\.?|versus)"
92	_DEBATE_PAIR_TERMS: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
93	    (r"pros?", r"cons?"),
94	    (r"benefits?", r"(?:risks?|drawbacks?|harms?|costs?|dangers?|downsides?)"),
95	    (r"(?:risks?|drawbacks?|harms?|dangers?|downsides?)", r"benefits?"),
96	    (r"advantages?", r"disadvantages?"),
97	    (r"disadvantages?", r"advantages?"),
98	    (r"strengths?", r"(?:weaknesses|limitations)"),
99	    (r"positives?", r"negatives?"),
100	    (r"positive", r"negative"),
101	    (r"upsides?", r"downsides?"),
102	    (r"for", r"against"),
103	    (r"(?:supporters?|proponents?)", r"(?:critics?|opponents?|detractors?|skeptics?|sceptics?)"),
104	    (r"praise", r"criticism"),
105	)
106	_DEBATE_STANDALONE: tuple[str, ...] = (
107	    r"debate[sd]?",
108	    r"controvers(?:y|ial|ies)",
109	    r"contested",
110	    r"both\s+sides",
111	    r"two\s+sides",
112	    r"either\s+side",
113	    r"divided\s+(?:opinion|views?|evidence)",
114	    r"case\s+for\s+and\s+(?:the\s+)?case\s+against",
115	)
116	_DEBATE_RE = re.compile(
117	    "|".join(
118	        [rf"\b{a}\s+{_DEBATE_CONNECTIVE}\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:potential\s+)?{b}\b" for a, b in _DEBATE_PAIR_TERMS]
119	        + [rf"\b{w}\b" for w in _DEBATE_STANDALONE]
120	    ),
121	    re.IGNORECASE,
122	)
123	
124	
125	def two_sided_debate_enabled() -> bool:
126	    """``PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE`` kill-switch (default OFF, LAW VI). OFF => the con-side retrieval guarantee
127	    and the composition-side disclosure are both no-ops => byte-identical."""
128	    return os.getenv("PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE", "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
129	
130	
131	def is_debate_question(text: str) -> bool:
132	    """True iff ``text`` (a question or a section title+focus) is DEBATE-framed — it asks for both sides
133	    of a disagreement. Precision-first: matches an explicit opposition pair joined by and/or/vs/versus,
134	    or a standalone debate word. Pure; the SHARED detector consumed by the retrieval-side con guarantee
135	    (here) and the composition-side debate-section detector (``multi_section_generator``)."""
136	    return bool(_DEBATE_RE.search(str(text or "")))
137	
138	
139	def _angle_lenses_for(n_angles: int, debate_active: bool) -> tuple[tuple[str, str], ...]:
140	    """The angle lenses to emit for a facet. Default = ``_ANGLE_LENSES[:n_angles]`` (byte-identical).
141	    When ``debate_active`` (``PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE`` ON + debate-framed question), the ``counter_evidence``
142	    lens is GUARANTEED present even if the budget would have truncated it — the con side must be
143	    retrieved for a debate query. Additive only; the base slice order is preserved."""
144	    lenses = list(_ANGLE_LENSES[:n_angles])
145	    if debate_active and not any(label == _COUNTER_EVIDENCE_LABEL for label, _ in lenses):
146	        ce = next((l for l in _ANGLE_LENSES if l[0] == _COUNTER_EVIDENCE_LABEL), None)
147	        if ce is not None:
148	            lenses.append(ce)
149	    return tuple(lenses)
150	
151	
152	@dataclass
153	class Facet:
154	    """One expert facet of the research question plus the angle queries derived from it."""
155	
156	    name: str
157	    queries: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
158	
159	
160	def _clean_facet_lines(text: str, cap: int) -> list[str]:
161	    """Parse an LLM facet-tree reply into clean facet names (strip numbering/bullets/labels).
162	
163	    Mirrors ``fs_researcher_query_gen._lines`` semantics (bullet/number strip, drop preamble lines,
164	    length floor) so facet parsing and the FS-Researcher TOC parse behave the same. Also strips a
165	    leading ``FACET:`` / ``ACTOR:`` / ``GEOGRAPHY:`` style dimension label the prompt may emit.
166	    """
167	    out: list[str] = []
168	    seen: set[str] = set()
169	    for raw in (text or "").splitlines():
170	        s = raw.strip()
171	        if not s:
172	            continue
173	        s = re.sub(r"^\s*(?:[-*]|\d+[.):])\s*", "", s).strip()
174	        # strip an optional leading dimension label ("FACET:", "Stakeholders -", ...)
175	        s = re.sub(r"^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z /_-]{2,20}\s*[:\-]\s+", "", s).strip().strip('"').strip()
176	        if len(s) <= 2:
177	            continue
178	        low = s.lower()
179	        if low.startswith(("here", "sure", "the following", "none", "facet tree")):
180	            continue
181	        if low in seen:
182	            continue
183	        seen.add(low)
184	        out.append(s)
185	        if len(out) >= cap:
186	            break
187	    return out
188	
189	
190	def _question_anchor(question: str) -> str:
191	    """The question's own subject keywords, used verbatim as a scope anchor on EVERY angle query.
192	
193	    Reuses ``query_decomposer.distill_keywords`` (in-scope retrieval util) so the anchor is the same
194	    distilled keyword phrase the keyword backends already use. Falls back to a whitespace-trimmed
195	    slice of the raw question when distillation yields nothing (never returns an empty anchor — an
196	    unanchored angle query is exactly the drb_72-v2 off-topic drift this fix must not re-create).
197	    """
198	    try:
199	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.query_decomposer import distill_keywords
200	        anchor = (distill_keywords(question, max_terms=8) or "").strip()
201	    except Exception:
202	        anchor = ""
203	    if anchor:
204	        return anchor
205	    # Fallback: the question's own leading content words (never empty for a real question).
206	    words = [w for w in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9\-]+", question or "") if len(w) > 2]
207	    return " ".join(words[:8]).strip() or (question or "").strip()
208	
209	
210	def _facet_angle_queries(
211	    facet_name: str, anchor: str, n_angles: int, debate_active: bool = False,
212	) -> list[str]:
213	    """Emit up to ``n_angles`` distinct facet-ANGLE queries for one facet, each scope-anchored.
214	
215	    Each query = ``{facet} {angle lens} {question anchor}`` — the facet supplies the sub-topic, the
216	    lens supplies the vantage point (mechanism / stakeholder / counter-evidence / temporal /
217	    geographic), and the anchor keeps it inside the question's subject so it cannot generalise into
218	    the facet's broad field. Duplicate collapse is case-insensitive.
219	
220	    Wave-2d: when ``debate_active`` the ``counter_evidence`` lens is guaranteed emitted (con-side
221	    retrieval for a debate query); ``debate_active=False`` (default / flag OFF) => byte-identical.
222	    """
223	    out: list[str] = []
224	    seen: set[str] = set()
225	    for label, lens in _angle_lenses_for(n_angles, debate_active):
226	        # facet first (primary subject), then the angle lens, then the scope anchor.
227	        q = f"{facet_name} {lens} {anchor}".strip()
228	        q = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", q)
229	        key = q.lower()
230	        if key in seen:
231	            continue
232	        seen.add(key)
233	        out.append(q)
234	    return out
235	
236	
237	def _deterministic_floor_facets(question: str) -> list[str]:
238	    """Facet FLOOR when the LLM returns no usable facet tree: the deterministic question split.
239	
240	    Uses ``query_decomposer.decompose_question`` (returns [] for a truly single-clause question) and
241	    falls back to the whole question. Guarantees the facet planner never emits FEWER seed facets than
242	    the legacy deterministic split — the frontier can only widen, never shrink (§-1.3 floor).
243	    """
244	    facets: list[str] = []
245	    try:
246	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.query_decomposer import decompose_question
247	        facets = list(decompose_question(question) or [])
248	    except Exception:
249	        facets = []
250	    if not facets and question and question.strip():
251	        facets = [question.strip()]
252	    return facets
253	
254	
255	def plan_expert_facets(question: str, llm: LlmFn, *, retrieval_policy: Any = None) -> list[Facet]:
256	    """Build the facet tree for ``question`` and expand each facet into angle queries.
257	
258	    ONE bounded LLM call elicits the facet tree across the taxonomy dimensions (sub-topics, actors /
259	    stakeholders, time-slices, geographies, mechanisms, opposing views); the angles are then
260	    multiplied deterministically at $0. When the LLM reply is unusable, the deterministic split of
261	    the question is used as the facet floor. Returns an ordered list of ``Facet`` (each with >=1
262	    scope-anchored angle query). Pure control flow over the injected ``llm`` — no network here.
263	
264	    ``retrieval_policy`` (spec item 3): the typed
265	    :class:`~planning.retrieval_projection.RetrievalPolicy`. ``None`` (default /
266	    PG_GATE OFF) => byte-identical. When present, each facet angle query is
267	    scope-anchored with the policy's HARD ``allowed_source_kinds`` +
268	    ``languages`` so the contract's source-type/language survive into the facet
269	    frontier (never as a filter — additive query text; exclusions stay negative
270	    and are NEVER appended here).
271	    """
272	    cap = _max_facets()
273	    n_angles = _angles_per_facet()
274	    anchor = _question_anchor(question)
275	    # Wave-2d (#1344): guarantee the counter_evidence angle for a debate-framed question when
276	    # PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE is ON. Default OFF => debate_active is False => byte-identical.
277	    debate_active = two_sided_debate_enabled() and is_debate_question(question)
278	
279	    facet_names: list[str] = []
280	    try:
281	        reply = llm(
282	            "You are an expert research planner. Decompose the RESEARCH QUESTION into its distinct "
283	            "expert facets so an exhaustive literature review can be planned. Cover these dimensions "
284	            "where they exist: sub-topics, actors/stakeholders, time-slices, geographies, mechanisms, "
285	            "and opposing/critical views. Every facet MUST stay within the scope of the question — "
286	            "carry its subject, domain and key entities; do NOT generalise a facet into its broad "
287	            "field. One facet per line, no numbering.\n\nRESEARCH QUESTION:\n" + (question or "")
288	        )
289	        facet_names = _clean_facet_lines(reply, cap)
290	    except Exception:
291	        facet_names = []
292	
293	    # I-qgen-001 (#1373): a validator/status message ("Temporal constraint violation:
294	    # The research question requires ...") is never a facet — screening here keeps it
295	    # out of the {facet} slot of the deterministic angle-query template. Shared
296	    # predicate with the scope validator; the question text is the subject-exemption
297	    # source; PG_QUERY_META_STATUS_SCREEN=0 kill-switch => byte-identical. The
298	    # deterministic FLOOR below still guarantees the frontier never shrinks to zero.
299	    if facet_names:
300	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.scope_query_validator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
301	            is_meta_status_clause,
302	            meta_status_screen_enabled,
303	        )
304	        if meta_status_screen_enabled():
305	            _screened = [f for f in facet_names if not is_meta_status_clause(f, question)]
306	            if len(_screened) != len(facet_names):
307	                import logging  # noqa: PLC0415
308	                logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.expert_facet_planner").info(
309	                    "[expert_facet_planner] I-qgen-001 (#1373) status-leak screen "
310	                    "dropped %d validator/status line(s) from the facet tree.",
311	                    len(facet_names) - len(_screened),
312	                )
313	            facet_names = _screened
314	
315	    # FLOOR: never fewer than the deterministic split; merge (dedup, keep LLM facets first).
316	    floor = _deterministic_floor_facets(question)
317	    seen = {f.lower() for f in facet_names}
318	    for f in floor:
319	        if f.lower() not in seen:
320	            facet_names.append(f)
321	            seen.add(f.lower())
322	        if len(facet_names) >= cap:
323	            break
324	    facet_names = facet_names[:cap]
325	
326	    facets: list[Facet] = []
327	    for name in facet_names:
328	        queries = _facet_angle_queries(name, anchor, n_angles, debate_active)
329	        if retrieval_policy is not None:
330	            queries = _policy_scope_anchor(queries, retrieval_policy)
331	        if queries:
332	            facets.append(Facet(name=name, queries=queries))
333	    return facets
334	
335	
336	def _policy_scope_anchor(queries: list[str], retrieval_policy: Any) -> list[str]:
337	    """ADD the policy's HARD source-type + language anchors into each facet angle
338	    query so the contract's scope survives into the facet frontier. Additive query
339	    TEXT only — never a filter, and exclusions are NEVER appended (they stay
340	    negative predicates). A policy without hard anchors returns the queries as-is
341	    (byte-identical)."""
342	    kinds = list(getattr(retrieval_policy, "allowed_source_kinds", []) or [])
343	    langs = [l for l in (getattr(retrieval_policy, "languages", []) or [])
344	             if str(l).strip().lower() not in ("en", "english")]
345	    suffix = " ".join([*kinds, *langs]).strip()
346	    if not suffix:
347	        return queries
348	    out: list[str] = []
349	    seen: set[str] = set()
350	    for q in queries:
351	        qs = (q or "").strip()
352	        if not qs:
353	            continue
354	        anchored = qs if suffix.lower() in qs.lower() else f"{qs} {suffix}"
355	        key = anchored.lower()
356	        if key not in seen:
357	            seen.add(key)
358	            out.append(anchored)
359	    return out
360	
361	
362	def facet_seed_queries(question: str, llm: LlmFn, *, retrieval_policy: Any = None) -> list[str]:
363	    """Flatten the facet tree into the ordered, de-duplicated seed-query frontier.
364	
365	    This is what the FS-Researcher planner consumes when the R1 flag is ON: a widened list of
366	    on-topic, angle-differentiated, scope-anchored queries. The FS-Researcher ``_max_queries`` cap
367	    (a compute-safety bound) still applies downstream, so this may return more than are ultimately
368	    issued — that is intentional headroom, never a forced count.
369	
370	    ``retrieval_policy`` (None => byte-identical) is forwarded to
371	    :func:`plan_expert_facets` so the contract's hard source-type/language anchor
372	    the flattened frontier.
373	    """
374	    out: list[str] = []
375	    seen: set[str] = set()
376	    for facet in plan_expert_facets(question, llm, retrieval_policy=retrieval_policy):
377	        for q in facet.queries:
378	            key = q.lower()
379	            if key in seen:
380	                continue
381	            seen.add(key)
382	            out.append(q)
383	    return out
384

1	"""R2 (I-deepfix-001, #1344) — per-task facet checklist drives the completeness/expansion loop.
2	
3	THE GAP: the production completeness/expansion loop (``nodes/completeness_checker.py``) only has a
4	CLINICAL checklist. On a general task (labor, finance, science) it reads a vacuous "0 of 0 covered"
5	(``completeness_state == "not_applicable"``) and fires NO gap-closing retrieval, so a tail facet
6	missed on retrieval pass one stays missed. Coverage never closes on general questions.
7	
8	THE FIX (retrieval-frontier half): derive a per-task facet checklist from R1's facet tree
9	(``expert_facet_planner``), measure which facets the ACCUMULATED corpus actually covers, and fire
10	targeted expansion queries for the UNCOVERED facets — repeating until the source yield stops growing
11	(a SATURATION stop keyed to new-distinct-source fraction, NOT a fixed count / breadth target). This
12	turns a vacuous general-task completeness check into a genuine coverage-closing loop keyed to the
13	task's own facets.
14	
15	SCOPE NOTE (parallel-safety): the group note pins this GROUP to ``retrieval/`` ONLY, so this half of
16	R2 lives at the FS-Researcher retrieval frontier (``fs_researcher_query_gen`` wires it in) and
17	``nodes/completeness_checker.py`` is left UNTOUCHED. The facet checklist is genuinely a
18	retrieval-frontier mechanism — it drives expansion RETRIEVAL. If the completeness NODE's own
19	``expand_queries`` path should also consume this checklist, that is a CORE/integration follow-up.
20	
21	DNA (§-1.3): coverage loop keyed to the task's own facets. It ADDS on-topic expansion queries only;
22	it DROPS ZERO sources; it touches no faithfulness gate. The saturation stop and the max-rounds bound
23	are compute-SAFETY bounds keyed to source YIELD — never a breadth number. Default OFF
24	(``PG_FACET_COMPLETENESS``) => the FS-Researcher path is byte-identical to today.
25	"""
26	
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import os
30	import re
31	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
32	from typing import Any, Callable
33	
34	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.expert_facet_planner import Facet
35	
36	# (research_question, **kw) -> LiveRetrievalResult (has ``.evidence_rows``). Injected — same contract
37	# as ``fs_researcher_query_gen.PerQueryRetrieveFn`` so the loop is unit-testable on a stub.
38	PerQueryRetrieveFn = Callable[..., Any]
39	
40	_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
41	    "the a an of to in on for and or but with without within into over under about across "
42	    "is are was were be been being as by at from this that these those it its their his her "
43	    "how what why when where which who whom whose than then also more most less least very "
44	    "impact effect role between among during recent trends over time change how it works "
45	    "mechanism drivers who affected stakeholders criticism limitations counter evidence dissent "
46	    "regional differences countries".split()
47	)
48	
49	
50	def facet_completeness_enabled() -> bool:
51	    """True iff the R2 facet-checklist expansion loop is flag-enabled (default OFF = legacy).
52	
53	    LAW VI env kill-switch. Default OFF keeps the FS-Researcher path byte-identical; the tests
54	    exercise the ON path explicitly.
55	    """
56	    return os.getenv("PG_FACET_COMPLETENESS", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True")
57	
58	
59	def _min_hits() -> int:
60	    """Min evidence rows matching a facet's keywords for the facet to count as covered (default 1)."""
61	    return max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_FACET_COVERAGE_MIN_HITS", "1")))
62	
63	
64	def _saturation_min_new_fraction() -> float:
65	    """Stop the expansion loop when a round's NEW distinct sources fall below this fraction of the
66	    corpus already gathered. A compute-safety SATURATION stop keyed to source YIELD — never a breadth
67	    count / target (§-1.3). Default 0.10 (a round adding <10% new sources is judged saturated)."""
68	    try:
69	        v = float(os.getenv("PG_FACET_SATURATION_MIN_NEW_FRACTION", "0.10"))
70	    except ValueError:
71	        v = 0.10
72	    return max(0.0, v)
73	
74	
75	def _max_expansion_rounds() -> int:
76	    """Hard compute-safety bound on expansion rounds (caps cost even if saturation never triggers).
77	    Not a breadth target — it bounds the loop UP-side. Default 4."""
78	    return max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_FACET_EXPANSION_MAX_ROUNDS", "4")))
79	
80	
81	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
82	    """Lower-cased content tokens (>=3 chars, stopword-filtered) of ``text``.
83	
84	    Prefers ``query_decomposer._content_tokens`` (in-scope retrieval util) for consistency with the
85	    rest of the frontier; falls back to a local tokenizer if that import is unavailable.
86	    """
87	    try:
88	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.query_decomposer import _content_tokens as _ct
89	        toks = {t for t in _ct(text or "") if len(t) >= 3 and t not in _STOPWORDS}
90	        if toks:
91	            return toks
92	    except Exception:
93	        pass
94	    return {
95	        t.lower()
96	        for t in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9\-]+", text or "")
97	        if len(t) >= 3 and t.lower() not in _STOPWORDS
98	    }
99	
100	
101	def _facet_keywords(facet: Facet) -> set[str]:
102	    """The facet's own subject keywords (from its NAME, not the generic angle-lens words).
103	
104	    A facet counts as covered only when the corpus carries its SUBJECT — the angle-lens words
105	    (mechanism / stakeholder / temporal / ...) are stripped by the stopword set so a generic word
106	    can never spuriously mark a facet covered.
107	    """
108	    return _content_tokens(facet.name)
109	
110	
111	def _row_text(row: dict) -> str:
112	    return " ".join(
113	        str(row.get(k) or "") for k in ("statement", "direct_quote", "title", "snippet")
114	    )
115	
116	
117	@dataclass
118	class FacetCoverage:
119	    """Coverage of one facet against the accumulated corpus."""
120	
121	    facet: Facet
122	    covered: bool
123	    hits: int
124	    matched_keywords: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
125	
126	
127	def measure_facet_coverage(
128	    facets: list[Facet],
129	    evidence_rows: list[dict],
130	    *,
131	    min_hits: int | None = None,
132	) -> list[FacetCoverage]:
133	    """Measure which facets the ACCUMULATED corpus covers.
134	
135	    A facet is covered iff at least ``min_hits`` evidence rows each contain at least one of the
136	    facet's SUBJECT keywords. Pure / no-network / no-LLM. This is the per-task facet checklist the
137	    plan calls for, replacing the vacuous clinical-only "0 of 0" on general tasks.
138	    """
139	    min_hits = min_hits or _min_hits()
140	    row_tokens = [_content_tokens(_row_text(r)) for r in (evidence_rows or [])]
141	    out: list[FacetCoverage] = []
142	    for facet in facets:
143	        kws = _facet_keywords(facet)
144	        if not kws:
145	            # A facet with no distinguishing subject token cannot be measured — treat as covered so
146	            # it never drives an unbounded expansion on an empty signal.
147	            out.append(FacetCoverage(facet=facet, covered=True, hits=0))
148	            continue
149	        hits = 0
150	        matched: set[str] = set()
151	        for toks in row_tokens:
152	            inter = kws & toks
153	            if inter:
154	                hits += 1
155	                matched |= inter
156	        out.append(
157	            FacetCoverage(
158	                facet=facet,
159	                covered=hits >= min_hits,
160	                hits=hits,
161	                matched_keywords=sorted(matched),
162	            )
163	        )
164	    return out
165	
166	
167	def uncovered_facets(coverage: list[FacetCoverage]) -> list[Facet]:
168	    """The facets not yet covered by the corpus (the expansion-loop work list)."""
169	    return [c.facet for c in coverage if not c.covered]
170	
171	
172	def _distinct_sources(rows: list[dict]) -> set[str]:
173	    out: set[str] = set()
174	    for r in rows or []:
175	        u = (r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip()
176	        if u:
177	            out.add(u)
178	    return out
179	
180	
181	@dataclass
182	class FacetExpansionResult:
183	    """Outcome of the facet-driven expansion loop."""
184	
185	    expansion_queries: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
186	    results: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
187	    rounds_run: int = 0
188	    stop_reason: str = ""
189	    coverage_trace: list[int] = field(default_factory=list)  # uncovered-facet count per round
190	
191	
192	def run_facet_expansion(
193	    facets: list[Facet],
194	    seed_evidence_rows: list[dict],
195	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
196	    *,
197	    retrieve_kwargs: dict | None = None,
198	    min_hits: int | None = None,
199	    saturation_min_new_fraction: float | None = None,
200	    max_rounds: int | None = None,
201	    max_queries: int | None = None,
202	    already_issued: set[str] | None = None,
203	    retrieval_deadline_passed: Callable[[], bool] | None = None,
204	) -> FacetExpansionResult:
205	    """Fire expansion queries for UNCOVERED facets until the source yield saturates.
206	
207	    Each round: (1) measure facet coverage against the accumulated corpus; (2) if every facet is
208	    covered, STOP (``all_covered``); (3) otherwise issue each uncovered facet's angle queries
209	    (skipping any already issued upstream) through the SAME production ``per_query_retrieve``,
210	    accumulate the new rows, and (4) STOP when the round's NEW distinct sources fall below
211	    ``saturation_min_new_fraction`` of the corpus so far (``yield_saturated``) or the compute-safety
212	    ``max_rounds`` bound is hit. Every new source flows through the UNCHANGED downstream
213	    (consolidation -> generation -> verify); this loop only decides WHICH queries to issue.
214	
215	    ``retrieval_deadline_passed`` (optional) is the shared per-question retrieval wall predicate; when
216	    it returns True the loop stops issuing new rounds (WALL-03 parity with the FS-Researcher loop).
217	    ``max_queries`` (optional) is a compute-safety budget cap on the TOTAL expansion queries issued
218	    here (so the caller's overall FS-Researcher query budget is honoured) — a cost bound, never a
219	    breadth target. Returns the queries issued, the per-query results, and a coverage trace. Drops
220	    ZERO sources.
221	    """
222	    retrieve_kwargs = dict(retrieve_kwargs or {})
223	    min_hits = min_hits or _min_hits()
224	    sat = _saturation_min_new_fraction() if saturation_min_new_fraction is None else saturation_min_new_fraction
225	    max_rounds = max_rounds or _max_expansion_rounds()
226	
227	    corpus: list[dict] = list(seed_evidence_rows or [])
228	    known_sources: set[str] = _distinct_sources(corpus)
229	    issued: set[str] = set(already_issued or set())
230	
231	    queries_out: list[str] = []
232	    results_out: list[Any] = []
233	    trace: list[int] = []
234	    stop_reason = "max_rounds"
235	
236	    for round_idx in range(max_rounds):
237	        if retrieval_deadline_passed is not None and retrieval_deadline_passed():
238	            stop_reason = "retrieval_wall"
239	            break
240	        coverage = measure_facet_coverage(facets, corpus, min_hits=min_hits)
241	        pending = uncovered_facets(coverage)
242	        trace.append(len(pending))
243	        if not pending:
244	            stop_reason = "all_covered"
245	            break
246	
247	        # Issue the uncovered facets' angle queries (dedup against what upstream already issued).
248	        round_new_sources: set[str] = set()
249	        issued_this_round = 0
250	        for facet in pending:
251	            if retrieval_deadline_passed is not None and retrieval_deadline_passed():
252	                stop_reason = "retrieval_wall"
253	                break
254	            if max_queries is not None and len(queries_out) >= max_queries:
255	                stop_reason = "query_budget"
256	                break
257	            for q in facet.queries:
258	                if max_queries is not None and len(queries_out) >= max_queries:
259	                    stop_reason = "query_budget"
260	                    break
261	                key = q.lower()
262	                if key in issued:
263	                    continue
264	                issued.add(key)
265	                queries_out.append(q)
266	                issued_this_round += 1
267	                result = per_query_retrieve(research_question=q, **retrieve_kwargs)
268	                results_out.append(result)
269	                new_rows = list(getattr(result, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
270	                corpus.extend(new_rows)
271	                for u in _distinct_sources(new_rows):
272	                    if u not in known_sources:
273	                        round_new_sources.add(u)
274	            if stop_reason in ("query_budget", "retrieval_wall"):
275	                break
276	        if stop_reason in ("query_budget", "retrieval_wall"):
277	            break
278	
279	        # No query left to issue for the uncovered facets -> frontier exhausted for them.
280	        if issued_this_round == 0:
281	            stop_reason = "frontier_exhausted"
282	            break
283	
284	        # SATURATION stop keyed to source YIELD (never a breadth count): if this round added fewer
285	        # than `sat` * (corpus-so-far) NEW distinct sources, the frontier is saturated.
286	        prior = max(1, len(known_sources))
287	        known_sources |= round_new_sources
288	        new_fraction = len(round_new_sources) / prior
289	        if new_fraction < sat:
290	            stop_reason = "yield_saturated"
291	            break
292	
293	    return FacetExpansionResult(
294	        expansion_queries=queries_out,
295	        results=results_out,
296	        rounds_run=len(trace),
297	        stop_reason=stop_reason,
298	        coverage_trace=trace,
299	    )
300

1	"""Rate-Limited Fetch Helper (Fix R4-#4).
2	
3	In v1, the pipeline was slow enough to naturally rate-limit itself. In v2,
4	asyncio.to_thread + concurrent fetching can fire 150 requests simultaneously,
5	instantly hitting HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) on Jina Reader, Serper, or S2.
6	
7	The existing analyzer.py uses asyncio.Semaphore(PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY=5) for
8	content fetching. This module provides a reusable rate-limited wrapper that
9	the v2 CRAG graph node should use for any URL fetching.
10	
11	Integration point: The CRAG graph node should use `rate_limited_fetch()`
12	instead of raw httpx/requests calls.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import asyncio
18	import logging
19	import os
20	import random
21	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
22	
23	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
24	
25	# Concurrency cap — matches existing PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY default
26	FETCH_CONCURRENCY = int(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY", "5"))
27	
28	# Retry config for 429/5xx responses
29	MAX_RETRIES = int(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_MAX_RETRIES", "3"))
30	RETRY_BASE_SECONDS = float(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_RETRY_BASE", "2.0"))
31	RETRY_MAX_SECONDS = float(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_RETRY_MAX", "30.0"))
32	
33	# Per-fetch timeout
34	FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = int(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_TIMEOUT", "30"))
35	
36	# Module-level semaphore (shared across all callers)
37	_semaphore: Optional[asyncio.Semaphore] = None
38	
39	
40	def _get_semaphore() -> asyncio.Semaphore:
41	    """Lazy-init semaphore (must be created inside an event loop)."""
42	    global _semaphore
43	    if _semaphore is None:
44	        _semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(FETCH_CONCURRENCY)
45	    return _semaphore
46	
47	
48	async def rate_limited_fetch(
49	    url: str,
50	    fetch_fn: Callable[..., Any],
51	    *args: Any,
52	    **kwargs: Any,
53	) -> Any:
54	    """Execute a fetch function with semaphore throttling and retry.
55	
56	    Fix R4-#4: Prevents 429 burst limit errors by:
57	    1. Semaphore: max PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY concurrent requests
58	    2. Retry: exponential backoff with jitter on 429/5xx
59	    3. Timeout: per-fetch timeout via asyncio.wait_for
60	
61	    Args:
62	        url: URL being fetched (for logging).
63	        fetch_fn: The actual fetch function (sync or async).
64	                  If sync, it will be wrapped in asyncio.to_thread.
65	        *args, **kwargs: Passed through to fetch_fn.
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        Whatever fetch_fn returns.
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        Exception: After MAX_RETRIES exhausted.
72	    """
73	    sem = _get_semaphore()
74	
75	    for attempt in range(1, MAX_RETRIES + 1):
76	        async with sem:
77	            try:
78	                # Run the fetch (with timeout)
79	                if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(fetch_fn):
80	                    result = await asyncio.wait_for(
81	                        fetch_fn(url, *args, **kwargs),
82	                        timeout=FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
83	                    )
84	                else:
85	                    result = await asyncio.wait_for(
86	                        asyncio.to_thread(fetch_fn, url, *args, **kwargs),
87	                        timeout=FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
88	                    )
89	                return result
90	
91	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
92	                logger.warning(
93	                    "Fetch timeout (%ds) for %s (attempt %d/%d)",
94	                    FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, url[:80], attempt, MAX_RETRIES,
95	                )
96	            except Exception as e:
97	                error_str = str(e).lower()
98	                is_rate_limit = "429" in error_str or "rate limit" in error_str
99	                is_server_error = any(
100	                    code in error_str for code in ("500", "502", "503", "504")
101	                )
102	
103	                if (is_rate_limit or is_server_error) and attempt < MAX_RETRIES:
104	                    # Exponential backoff with jitter
105	                    delay = min(
106	                        RETRY_BASE_SECONDS * (2 ** (attempt - 1)),
107	                        RETRY_MAX_SECONDS,
108	                    )
109	                    jitter = random.uniform(0, delay * 0.5)
110	                    total_delay = delay + jitter
111	                    logger.info(
112	                        "Fetch %s: %s, retrying in %.1fs (attempt %d/%d)",
113	                        "429" if is_rate_limit else "5xx",
114	                        url[:60], total_delay, attempt, MAX_RETRIES,
115	                    )
116	                    await asyncio.sleep(total_delay)
117	                else:
118	                    if attempt >= MAX_RETRIES:
119	                        logger.warning(
120	                            "Fetch failed after %d attempts: %s — %s",
121	                            MAX_RETRIES, url[:80], str(e)[:200],
122	                        )
123	                    raise
124	
125	    return None
126	
127	
128	async def rate_limited_fetch_batch(
129	    urls: list[str],
130	    fetch_fn: Callable[..., Any],
131	    *args: Any,
132	    **kwargs: Any,
133	) -> dict[str, Any]:
134	    """Fetch multiple URLs with rate limiting.
135	
136	    Returns dict of url -> result. Failed fetches map to None.
137	    """
138	    tasks = {
139	        url: asyncio.create_task(
140	            rate_limited_fetch(url, fetch_fn, *args, **kwargs)
141	        )
142	        for url in urls
143	    }
144	
145	    results: dict[str, Any] = {}
146	    for url, task in tasks.items():
147	        try:
148	            results[url] = await task
149	        except Exception:
150	            results[url] = None
151	
152	    succeeded = sum(1 for v in results.values() if v is not None)
153	    logger.info(
154	        "Batch fetch: %d/%d succeeded (concurrency=%d)",
155	        succeeded, len(urls), FETCH_CONCURRENCY,
156	    )
157	    return results
158

1	"""P0-1 forbidden-source-by-IDENTITY pre-composition scope gate (I-deepfix-001).
2	
3	The blocked-reference deny-list (``blocked_reference_registry``) removes an operator-PROHIBITED
4	source at fetch and at the corpus/selection seams. This module is the LAST-line, claim-level
5	backstop: given claim units that each carry their supporting sources, it REDACTS any claim
6	whose supporting source has a forbidden IDENTITY (DOI / DOAJ id / PII / title+author) —
7	**REGARDLESS of the D8 entailment verdict** on that claim.
8	
9	Why regardless of D8: the leak this closes is a blocked mirror that was span-verified and
10	counted as VERIFIED independent SUPPORT in the corroboration / necessity ledgers. A claim can
11	be perfectly entailment-SUPPORTED and still be non-compliant if its support is an operator
12	do-not-view source. Compliance is a prohibition, NOT a faithfulness call — so the gate never
13	looks at the entailment / verdict field. This STRENGTHENS the faithfulness engine (it drops
14	MORE unfaithful/forbidden text); it never relaxes it.
15	
16	Fail-LOUD: every redaction is logged AND written to a disclosed-redaction telemetry file —
17	never a silent drop. Empty registry (no appendix / kill-switch OFF) => the ORIGINAL claims
18	object is returned unchanged (byte-identical no-op). Pure / no network; never raises.
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import json
24	import logging
25	from typing import Any, Mapping
26	
27	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.blocked_reference_registry import (
28	    BlockedRegistry,
29	    is_blocked_source,
30	)
31	
32	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.forbidden_identity_gate")
33	
34	# The keys under which a claim unit may carry its supporting sources (dict OR object).
35	_SUPPORT_KEYS = (
36	    "supporting_sources",
37	    "sources",
38	    "support",
39	    "supports",
40	    "citations",
41	    "basket",
42	    "members",
43	    "evidence",
44	)
45	_CLAIM_ID_KEYS = ("claim_id", "id", "claim_hash", "clm_hash", "num")
46	
47	
48	def _claim_field(claim: "Any", *names: str) -> "Any":
49	    if isinstance(claim, Mapping):
50	        for n in names:
51	            if n in claim and claim.get(n) is not None:
52	                return claim.get(n)
53	        return None
54	    for n in names:
55	        v = getattr(claim, n, None)
56	        if v is not None:
57	            return v
58	    return None
59	
60	
61	def _claim_supporting_sources(claim: "Any") -> list[Any]:
62	    """Every supporting-source unit a claim carries, across the known field shapes."""
63	    out: list[Any] = []
64	    for key in _SUPPORT_KEYS:
65	        val = _claim_field(claim, key)
66	        if val is None:
67	            continue
68	        if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)):
69	            out.extend(val)
70	        else:
71	            out.append(val)
72	    return out
73	
74	
75	def _claim_id(claim: "Any", index: int) -> str:
76	    cid = _claim_field(claim, *_CLAIM_ID_KEYS)
77	    return str(cid) if cid is not None else f"claim_{index}"
78	
79	
80	def scope_gate_redact_claims(
81	    claims: "list[Any] | None",
82	    registry: "BlockedRegistry | None",
83	    *,
84	    log: "Any" = None,
85	    run_dir: "Any" = None,
86	    label: str = "",
87	) -> "tuple[list[Any], list[dict[str, str]]]":
88	    """Redact every claim whose supporting source has a forbidden identity (P0-1 (c)).
89	
90	    Returns ``(kept_claims, redacted_records)``. The verdict / entailment field of a claim is
91	    NEVER consulted — a forbidden support redacts the claim even when it is D8-SUPPORTED.
92	
93	    Empty/None registry => the SAME ``claims`` object is returned + ``[]`` (byte-identical
94	    no-op, no telemetry written). Fail-LOUD on every redaction; never raises."""
95	    if claims is None:
96	        return ([], [])
97	    if registry is None or getattr(registry, "is_empty", True):
98	        return (claims, [])
99	
100	    kept: list[Any] = []
101	    redacted: list[dict[str, str]] = []
102	    for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
103	        hit_reason = ""
104	        blocked_url = ""
105	        try:
106	            for src in _claim_supporting_sources(claim):
107	                _hit, _reason = is_blocked_source(src, registry)
108	                if _hit:
109	                    hit_reason = _reason
110	                    _u = src.get("url") if isinstance(src, Mapping) else getattr(src, "url", "")
111	                    blocked_url = str(_u or "")
112	                    break
113	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never let a bad claim abort the gate
114	            logger.warning("[forbidden_identity_gate] claim scan skipped: %s", exc)
115	        if hit_reason:
116	            redacted.append(
117	                {
118	                    "claim_id": _claim_id(claim, idx),
119	                    "blocked_source_url": blocked_url[:300],
120	                    "reason": f"forbidden_identity:{hit_reason}"[:300],
121	                }
122	            )
123	        else:
124	            kept.append(claim)
125	
126	    if redacted:
127	        _seam = f" [{label}]" if label else ""
128	        if log is not None:
129	            log(
130	                f"[forbidden_identity_gate]{_seam} REDACTED {len(redacted)} claim(s) "
131	                f"({len(claims)} -> {len(kept)}) whose supporting source is on the operator "
132	                "do-not-view deny-list [regardless of D8 verdict]"
133	            )
134	        if run_dir is not None:
135	            _fname = (
136	                "scope_gate_redacted.json"
137	                if not label
138	                else f"scope_gate_redacted_{label}.json"
139	            )
140	            try:
141	                (run_dir / _fname).write_text(
142	                    json.dumps(redacted, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
143	                    encoding="utf-8",
144	                )
145	            except Exception as _wx:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry best-effort
146	                if log is not None:
147	                    log(
148	                        f"[forbidden_identity_gate]{_seam} redaction-telemetry write "
149	                        f"skipped: {_wx}"
150	                    )
151	
152	    return (kept, redacted)
153

content.text: These files look clean and well-named. Now let's look at frame_fetcher.py and evidence_selector.py.

content.content:
1	"""M-56 (2026-04-23): V30 deterministic frame fetcher.
2	
3	V30 Report Contract Architecture Layer 2b. Replaces Serper/S2
4	non-deterministic keyword retrieval for known-identifier entities
5	with three deterministic, free, never-paywalled APIs:
6	
7	  1. CrossRef `/works/{doi}` — metadata (title, authors, abstract, year)
8	  2. Unpaywall `/v2/{doi}`   — OA PDF URL if an OA version exists
9	  3. PubMed EFetch (pmid)    — deterministic abstract XML when
10	                                full-text paywalled.
11	
12	V28/V29 showed that keyword retrieval via Serper/S2 was the primary
13	cause of V30 Defect A (non-determinism). Same M-48 variant queries
14	landed different primary sets across runs. M-56 bypasses keyword
15	retrieval entirely for known-DOI entities — given the same DOI the
16	retriever emits a byte-identical FrameRow.
17	
18	## Layered architecture
19	
20	The module has four distinct layers. Tests exercise them separately
21	to isolate network dependency:
22	
23	  Layer 1: Pure data types (FrameRow, ProvenanceClass, RetrievalAttempt).
24	  Layer 2: Pure parsers (_parse_crossref_response, _parse_unpaywall_response,
25	           _parse_pubmed_xml). Given raw API response, return structured
26	           fields.
27	  Layer 3: Network callers (_call_crossref, _call_unpaywall, _call_pubmed)
28	           with bounded retry + fixed timeout + status-code-to-outcome
29	           mapping.
30	  Layer 4: Orchestrator (fetch_frame_entity) which dispatches by
31	           primary_identifier and composes FrameRow.
32	
33	## Dependency injection for testability
34	
35	`fetch_frame_entity(binding, *, client=None, ...)` accepts an
36	optional httpx.Client. Production callers pass None (module creates
37	one per call); tests pass a fake client that returns canned
38	responses. No test needs monkey-patching of module globals.
39	
40	## Codex V30 plan pass-1 revision #4 (M-60 manifest metadata)
41	
42	Every fetch attempt — success or failure — is logged into
43	FrameRow.retrieval_attempts: list of RetrievalAttempt(source, url,
44	status, duration_ms, outcome). M-60 manifest rendering consumes this
45	log to explain exactly what the pipeline tried for every slot that
46	ended up in frame_gap_unrecoverable state.
47	
48	## Determinism contract
49	
50	Given the same (binding, same upstream API responses), the same
51	FrameRow is emitted. Specifically:
52	  - No wall-clock reads other than duration measurement (which is
53	    logged but does not affect the FrameRow payload proper).
54	  - No randomness in retry backoff (fixed 1s/2s/4s schedule).
55	  - Sort order within parsed fields is explicit.
56	  - direct_quote is deterministic given inputs.
57	"""
58	from __future__ import annotations
59	
60	import logging
61	import os
62	import time
63	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
64	from enum import Enum
65	from typing import Any
66	from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
67	
68	import httpx
69	
70	from ..nodes.frame_compiler import EvidenceBinding
71	from src.tools.core_client import fetch_core_oa_fulltext
72	
73	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
74	
75	
76	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77	# Layer 1 — Pure data types
78	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
79	class ProvenanceClass(str, Enum):
80	    """Provenance of the content captured in FrameRow.direct_quote.
81	
82	    open_access: full OA text retrieved (preferred; richest for M-58
83	      slot-fill).
84	    abstract_only: abstract text available (from CrossRef or PubMed);
85	      full-text paywalled.
86	    metadata_only: title/authors/year known but no abstract text.
87	      Can still surface structured citation but M-58 will emit
88	      `not_extractable` for most required_fields.
89	    frame_gap_unrecoverable: all three sources failed; no locator
90	      resolved anything. M-60 will emit explicit gap sentence.
91	    human_curated: content supplied by M-61 operator via the
92	      hybrid-completion Path B. PERMANENT marker — downstream
93	      rendering MUST surface the human-curated flag in every
94	      citation + Methods disclosure. M-61 supplies the
95	      StructuredProvenance audit evidence separately via
96	      FrameRow.human_curated_provenance.
97	    """
98	
99	    OPEN_ACCESS = "open_access"
100	    ABSTRACT_ONLY = "abstract_only"
101	    METADATA_ONLY = "metadata_only"
102	    FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE = "frame_gap_unrecoverable"
103	    HUMAN_CURATED = "human_curated"
104	
105	
106	@dataclass(frozen=True)
107	class RetrievalAttempt:
108	    """One network attempt log entry — EXACTLY ONE PER HTTP REQUEST
109	    (not per source). Codex M-56 audit Blocker 2: retry chains must
110	    be fully visible so M-60 manifest can show exactly what was
111	    tried.
112	
113	    Deterministic fields only — `duration_ms` lives in
114	    RetrievalTiming so FrameRow payload equality is meaningful.
115	    """
116	
117	    source: str           # "crossref" | "unpaywall" | "pubmed"
118	    url: str              # full requested URL including query params
119	    attempt_index: int    # 1-based per-source counter (1, 2, 3 = retry 1, 2, 3)
120	    http_status: int | None  # None on network-level failure
121	    outcome: str          # "success" | "not_found" | "error:<short>"
122	                          # | "retryable_http_<code>" | "retryable_network:<cls>"
123	
124	
125	@dataclass(frozen=True)
126	class RetrievalTiming:
127	    """Per-attempt timing, separated from RetrievalAttempt so
128	    FrameRow.retrieval_attempts stays byte-deterministic across
129	    runs. Codex M-56 audit Blocker 1."""
130	
131	    source: str
132	    attempt_index: int
133	    duration_ms: int
134	
135	
136	@dataclass(frozen=True)
137	class FrameRow:
138	    """Output of M-56 frame_fetcher. One row per required entity.
139	
140	    Maps to existing POLARIS evidence shape (entity_id, direct_quote,
141	    url) so M-57/M-58/M-59 can consume it alongside non-frame
142	    evidence rows.
143	    """
144	
145	    entity_id: str
146	    entity_type: str
147	    rendering_slot: str
148	    provenance_class: ProvenanceClass
149	    direct_quote: str           # empty when gap
150	    quote_source: str           # "crossref_abstract" | "openalex_abstract"
151	                                # | "pubmed_abstract" | "oa_full_text"
152	                                # | "core_oa_fulltext" (legal CORE OA fetch,
153	                                #   I-faith-002) | "url_pattern_fetch"
154	                                # | "url_pattern_placeholder" | "none"
155	    # Resolved locators (may be empty for gap rows)
156	    doi: str | None
157	    pmid: str | None            # string form to handle both int/str inputs
158	    oa_pdf_url: str | None
159	    url: str | None             # for regulatory url_pattern entities
160	    # Metadata where available
161	    title: str | None
162	    authors: tuple[str, ...]
163	    journal: str | None
164	    year: int | None
165	    # Failure metadata (only populated on gap)
166	    failure_reason: str | None
167	    # Audit log — deterministic payload: one RetrievalAttempt per
168	    # HTTP request (retry chain fully expanded). Codex M-56 audit
169	    # Blocker 2.
170	    retrieval_attempts: tuple[RetrievalAttempt, ...] = field(
171	        default_factory=tuple
172	    )
173	    # Per-attempt timing, non-deterministic (wall-clock-derived).
174	    # Lives outside the determinism-comparable payload per Codex
175	    # M-56 audit Blocker 1. Correlated to retrieval_attempts via
176	    # (source, attempt_index).
177	    retrieval_timings: tuple[RetrievalTiming, ...] = field(
178	        default_factory=tuple
179	    )
180	    # M-61 structured provenance for human-curated rows. None for
181	    # rows with provenance_class != HUMAN_CURATED. Dict shape —
182	    # NOT a typed StructuredProvenance reference — to avoid
183	    # circular import between frame_fetcher and
184	    # human_gap_completion. Callers can round-trip via
185	    # StructuredProvenance.from_dict when needed. Codex M-61
186	    # audit Blocker 3 fix.
187	    human_curated_provenance: dict[str, str] | None = None
188	
189	    def is_gap(self) -> bool:
190	        return self.provenance_class == ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE
191	
192	
193	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
194	# Config
195	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196	_CROSSREF_BASE = "https://api.crossref.org/works/"
197	_UNPAYWALL_BASE = "https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/"
198	_PUBMED_EFETCH_BASE = (
199	    "https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi"
200	)
201	# OpenAlex /works/{doi} — issue #1033 abstract fallback. When CrossRef
202	# carries no abstract and the OA full-text PDF is paywalled (403),
203	# OpenAlex's abstract_inverted_index reliably holds the abstract for
204	# indexed journal DOIs. DOI-driven; deterministic.
205	_OPENALEX_WORK_BASE = "https://api.openalex.org/works/"
206	_OPENALEX_FRAME_FALLBACK_ENABLED = (
207	    os.getenv("PG_OPENALEX_FRAME_FALLBACK", "1").strip().lower()
208	    not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
209	)
210	# Semantic Scholar Graph API /paper/DOI:{doi} — M3b (I-deepfix-001) 3rd deterministic
211	# abstract source. For a closed-access primary whose CrossRef/OpenAlex abstract is a
212	# degenerate fragment (or a single transient throttle leaves it empty), S2 carries a
213	# full abstract for most indexed DOIs. DOI-driven; deterministic; abstract is the honest
214	# reachable ceiling for a paywalled primary (NO full-text, NO Sci-Hub).
215	_S2_WORK_BASE = "https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/DOI:"
216	# Minimum chars for an OA full-text fetch to count as REAL full text
217	# (issue #1034). Paywalled-PDF stubs (e.g. aeaweb via Jina ~540 chars)
218	# fall below this -> they must not block the abstract fallbacks and
219	# must lose to a real abstract (e.g. OpenAlex 1331 chars).
220	_OA_FULLTEXT_MIN_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PG_OA_FULLTEXT_MIN_CHARS", "1200"))
221	# Prefer the clean, deterministic abstract (CrossRef/OpenAlex/PubMed) over a
222	# scraped OA "full text" for frame-contract grounding (#1034). Ground-truthed:
223	# paywalled-journal OA fetches are NON-DETERMINISTIC (Sci-Hub HTML one call,
224	# Jina landing-page markdown the next, clean CrossRef abstract a third) and
225	# noisy, while the abstract is clean and stable — and contract fields
226	# (thesis/mechanism/effect) are abstract-level claims. Default OFF preserves
227	# the M-66b-T clinical full-text path (multi-field trial rosters live in
228	# tables, not abstracts); run_gate_b sets it ON for the benchmark.
229	_FRAME_PREFER_ABSTRACT = (
230	    os.getenv("PG_FRAME_PREFER_ABSTRACT", "0").strip().lower()
231	    in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
232	)
233	# Entity types whose contract fields live in full-text TABLES (clinical trial
234	# 9-field rosters etc.) KEEP the OA full-text path even under prefer-abstract,
235	# so Gate-B gold-rubric coverage for clinical questions is preserved (dual-audit
236	# #1034 P1). Narrative entity types (economic_report, ...) prefer the clean
237	# abstract AND skip the scrape entirely (no non-deterministic fetch, no Sci-Hub
238	# request).
239	_FULLTEXT_ENTITY_TYPES = frozenset(
240	    t.strip().lower()
241	    for t in os.getenv(
242	        "PG_FRAME_FULLTEXT_ENTITY_TYPES",
243	        "pivotal_trial,clinical_trial,rct,systematic_review,meta_analysis",
244	    ).split(",")
245	    if t.strip()
246	)
247	
248	def _frame_multi_abstract_enabled() -> bool:
249	    """M3b (I-deepfix-001) gather-all-then-pick-richest toggle (default ON).
250	
251	    When ON, the deterministic abstract sources (OpenAlex, and Semantic Scholar
252	    below) are consulted for a DOI EVEN WHEN CrossRef/PubMed already returned an
253	    abstract, so the RICHEST abstract wins via ``_pick_richest_abstract`` instead
254	    of a degenerate first-source fragment short-circuiting the gather. Read at
255	    CALL TIME so a test/operator can flip ``PG_FRAME_MULTI_ABSTRACT`` after import
256	    (same pattern as ``_core_enabled``). OFF restores the legacy
257	    ``not abstract_crossref and not abstract_pubmed`` short-circuit byte-identically.
258	    """
259	    return os.getenv("PG_FRAME_MULTI_ABSTRACT", "1").strip().lower() not in (
260	        "0", "false", "no", "off",
261	    )

298	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
299	# Layer 2 — Pure parsers
300	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
301	def _parse_crossref_response(data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
302	    """Extract relevant fields from CrossRef /works/{doi} JSON.
303	
304	    Returns dict with keys: title, authors, journal, year, abstract,
305	    doi (normalized). Values may be None/empty when not present in
306	    the response."""
307	    msg = data.get("message", {}) if isinstance(data, dict) else {}
308	    title_list = msg.get("title") or []
309	    title = title_list[0].strip() if title_list else None
310	
311	    authors_raw = msg.get("author") or []
312	    authors: list[str] = []
313	    for a in authors_raw:
314	        if not isinstance(a, dict):
315	            continue
316	        given = (a.get("given") or "").strip()
317	        family = (a.get("family") or "").strip()
318	        if family and given:
319	            authors.append(f"{family} {given[0]}".strip())
320	        elif family:
321	            authors.append(family)
322	
323	    container = msg.get("container-title") or []
324	    journal = container[0].strip() if container else None
325	
326	    year = None
327	    for key in ("published-print", "published-online", "issued", "created"):
328	        dp = msg.get(key) or {}
329	        parts = dp.get("date-parts") or []
330	        if parts and parts[0]:
331	            try:
332	                year = int(parts[0][0])
333	                break
334	            except (ValueError, TypeError):
335	                continue
336	
337	    abstract = msg.get("abstract") or None
338	    if isinstance(abstract, str):
339	        # CrossRef abstracts often carry JATS XML tags; strip the
340	        # outer <jats:p>...</jats:p> but keep text content.
341	        abstract = _strip_jats_tags(abstract)
342	
343	    doi = (msg.get("DOI") or "").strip() or None
344	
345	    return {
346	        "title": title,
347	        "authors": tuple(authors),
348	        "journal": journal,
349	        "year": year,
350	        "abstract": abstract,
351	        "doi": doi,
352	    }
353

355	def _parse_unpaywall_response(data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
356	    """Extract OA status + best OA PDF URL from Unpaywall response."""
357	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
358	        return {"is_oa": False, "oa_pdf_url": None, "oa_html_url": None}
359	
360	    is_oa = bool(data.get("is_oa"))
361	    best = data.get("best_oa_location") or {}
362	    oa_pdf_url = (
363	        best.get("url_for_pdf") if isinstance(best, dict) else None
364	    ) or None
365	    oa_html_url = (
366	        best.get("url_for_landing_page") if isinstance(best, dict) else None
367	    ) or None
368	
369	    return {
370	        "is_oa": is_oa,
371	        "oa_pdf_url": oa_pdf_url,
372	        "oa_html_url": oa_html_url,
373	    }
