376	def _parse_pubmed_xml(xml_text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
377	    """Extract abstract text + metadata from PubMed EFetch XML.
378	
379	    Handles the standard <PubmedArticleSet>/<PubmedArticle>/<MedlineCitation>
380	    structure. When the XML is malformed or empty, returns an empty
381	    dict (caller treats as not_found)."""
382	    if not isinstance(xml_text, str) or not xml_text.strip():
383	        return {}
384	    try:
385	        root = ET.fromstring(xml_text)
386	    except ET.ParseError:
387	        return {}
388	
389	    # Abstract is under Article/Abstract/AbstractText (may be multiple)
390	    abstract_parts: list[str] = []
391	    for at in root.iter("AbstractText"):
392	        label = at.get("Label")
393	        text = (at.text or "").strip()
394	        if label and text:
395	            abstract_parts.append(f"{label}: {text}")
396	        elif text:
397	            abstract_parts.append(text)
398	    abstract = " ".join(abstract_parts) if abstract_parts else None
399	
400	    title_elt = next(root.iter("ArticleTitle"), None)
401	    title = (title_elt.text or "").strip() if title_elt is not None else None
402	
403	    journal_elt = next(root.iter("Title"), None)  # Journal/Title
404	    journal = (journal_elt.text or "").strip() if journal_elt is not None else None
405	
406	    year = None
407	    year_elt = next(root.iter("Year"), None)
408	    if year_elt is not None and year_elt.text:
409	        try:
410	            year = int(year_elt.text.strip())
411	        except ValueError:
412	            year = None
413	
414	    authors: list[str] = []
415	    for a in root.iter("Author"):
416	        last = (a.findtext("LastName") or "").strip()
417	        init = (a.findtext("Initials") or "").strip()
418	        if last and init:
419	            authors.append(f"{last} {init[0]}")
420	        elif last:
421	            authors.append(last)
422	
423	    # V30 Phase-2 sweep run-1 root cause: stale/wrong PMID in the
424	    # contract YAML (e.g. PMID 34010531 bound to surpass_2_primary
425	    # actually points at the SPRINT blood-pressure trial, not the
426	    # Frias tirzepatide paper). The extractor passed anti-fabrication
427	    # because SPRINT prose WAS verbatim in the abstract we fetched.
428	    # Defense: pull PubMed's own DOI (`<ELocationID EIdType="doi">`)
429	    # so the caller can cross-check against the bound DOI and reject
430	    # mismatches rather than render wrong content.
431	    pubmed_doi: str | None = None
432	    for el in root.iter("ELocationID"):
433	        if el.get("EIdType") == "doi" and el.text:
434	            pubmed_doi = el.text.strip().lower()
435	            break
436	    # PMID itself (for round-trip consistency checks).
437	    pmid_elt = next(root.iter("PMID"), None)
438	    pubmed_pmid = (
439	        pmid_elt.text.strip() if pmid_elt is not None and pmid_elt.text
440	        else None
441	    )
442	
443	    return {
444	        "title": title,
445	        "authors": tuple(authors),
446	        "journal": journal,
447	        "year": year,
448	        "abstract": abstract,
449	        "doi": pubmed_doi,
450	        "pmid": pubmed_pmid,
451	    }
452	
453	
454	def _reconstruct_inverted_abstract(inverted_index: Any) -> str | None:
455	    """Reconstruct a plain-text abstract from an OpenAlex
456	    `abstract_inverted_index` ({word: [pos, ...]}). Deterministic:
457	    positions are unique within a work, so sort-by-position is a
458	    total order -> same input yields byte-identical text.
459	
460	    Kept local (mirrors the proven reconstruction in
461	    agents/searcher.py) so frame_fetcher stays free of the
462	    non-deterministic keyword-retrieval stack per its module
463	    determinism contract."""
464	    if not isinstance(inverted_index, dict) or not inverted_index:
465	        return None
466	    word_positions: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
467	    for word, positions in inverted_index.items():
468	        if not isinstance(positions, (list, tuple)):
469	            continue
470	        for pos in positions:
471	            if isinstance(pos, int):
472	                word_positions.append((pos, word))
473	    if not word_positions:
474	        return None
475	    word_positions.sort()
476	    text = " ".join(w for _, w in word_positions).strip()
477	    return text or None
478	
479	
480	def _parse_openalex_response(data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
481	    """Extract abstract + metadata from an OpenAlex /works/{doi}
482	    JSON object (issue #1033). Abstract is reconstructed from
483	    abstract_inverted_index. Returns dict with keys: title, authors,
484	    journal, year, abstract, doi (normalized, prefix-stripped,
485	    lowercased). Values may be None/empty when absent."""
486	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
487	        return {}
488	    abstract = _reconstruct_inverted_abstract(
489	        data.get("abstract_inverted_index")
490	    )
491	
492	    title = data.get("title") or data.get("display_name") or None
493	    if isinstance(title, str):
494	        title = title.strip() or None
495	
496	    authors: list[str] = []
497	    for a in (data.get("authorships") or []):
498	        if not isinstance(a, dict):
499	            continue
500	        au = a.get("author")

500	        au = a.get("author")
501	        name = (au.get("display_name") or "").strip() if isinstance(au, dict) else ""
502	        if name:
503	            authors.append(name)
504	
505	    journal = None
506	    pl = data.get("primary_location")
507	    if isinstance(pl, dict):
508	        src = pl.get("source")
509	        if isinstance(src, dict):
510	            journal = (src.get("display_name") or "").strip() or None
511	
512	    year = data.get("publication_year")
513	    if not isinstance(year, int):
514	        year = None
515	
516	    doi_raw = data.get("doi") or ""
517	    doi_norm = doi_raw.lower().strip() if isinstance(doi_raw, str) else ""
518	    for prefix in ("https://doi.org/", "http://doi.org/", "doi:"):
519	        if doi_norm.startswith(prefix):
520	            doi_norm = doi_norm[len(prefix):]
521	            break
522	    doi_norm = doi_norm.rstrip("/") or None
523	
524	    return {
525	        "title": title,
526	        "authors": tuple(authors),
527	        "journal": journal,
528	        "year": year,
529	        "abstract": abstract,
530	        "doi": doi_norm,
531	    }
532	
533	
534	def _strip_jats_tags(text: str) -> str:
535	    """Very conservative JATS XML stripping for CrossRef abstracts.
536	    Keeps text content, drops tags.
537	
538	    CrossRef abstracts frequently use the `<jats:p>` etc. namespace
539	    prefix without declaring the xmlns. We wrap the text in a root
540	    element that declares `xmlns:jats` so ElementTree can parse it.
541	    """
542	    # Wrap with a root that declares the JATS namespace so prefixed
543	    # tags parse. Several common JATS prefixes seen in the wild.
544	    wrapped = (
545	        '<root xmlns:jats="http://jats.nlm.nih.gov" '
546	        'xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">'
547	        f'{text}</root>'
548	    )
549	    try:
550	        root = ET.fromstring(wrapped)
551	        return " ".join(
552	            s.strip() for s in root.itertext() if s and s.strip()
553	        )
554	    except ET.ParseError:
555	        # Last-resort: strip crude <...> tags via a single pass.
556	        # Safe on CrossRef content (no user-controlled bytes).
557	        out_chars: list[str] = []
558	        depth = 0
559	        for ch in text:
560	            if ch == "<":
561	                depth += 1
562	                continue
563	            if ch == ">":
564	                depth = max(0, depth - 1)
565	                continue
566	            if depth == 0:
567	                out_chars.append(ch)
568	        return "".join(out_chars).strip()
569

571	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
572	# Layer 3 — Network callers
573	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
574	def _build_full_url(
575	    base: str, params: dict[str, Any] | None,
576	) -> str:
577	    """Compose a URL string including query params so logs carry
578	    the exact HTTP line. Codex M-56 audit Blocker 2: PubMed
579	    attempts must show id/rettype/retmode, not just base endpoint."""
580	    if not params:
581	        return base
582	    # Deterministic param ordering — sorted by key.
583	    q = "&".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in sorted(params.items()))
584	    sep = "&" if "?" in base else "?"
585	    return f"{base}{sep}{q}"
586	
587	
588	def _request_with_retry(
589	    client: httpx.Client,
590	    method: str,
591	    source: str,
592	    url: str,
593	    *,
594	    headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,
595	    params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
596	) -> tuple[
597	    httpx.Response | None,
598	    str,
599	    list[RetrievalAttempt],
600	    list[RetrievalTiming],
601	]:
602	    """Deterministic retry with fixed backoff. Emits ONE
603	    RetrievalAttempt per HTTP request (Codex M-56 audit Blocker 2)
604	    with per-attempt timing in a separate RetrievalTiming list
605	    (Blocker 1: non-deterministic wall-clock out of payload).
606	
607	    Returns (response_or_None, final_outcome, attempts, timings).
608	    final_outcome ∈ {"success", "not_found", "error:timeout",
609	    "error:http_<code>", "error:network:<short>", "error:exhausted:<cls>"}.
610	    """
611	    headers = {**(headers or {}), "User-Agent": _POLARIS_UA}
612	    full_url = _build_full_url(url, params)
613	    attempts: list[RetrievalAttempt] = []
614	    timings: list[RetrievalTiming] = []
615	    last_exc: Exception | None = None
616	    for attempt_idx in range(1, _MAX_RETRIES + 1):
617	        t0 = time.monotonic()
618	        try:
619	            if method == "GET":
620	                r = client.get(url, headers=headers, params=params)
621	            else:
622	                raise ValueError(f"unsupported method {method!r}")
623	        except (httpx.TimeoutException, httpx.ConnectError) as exc:
624	            elapsed = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)
625	            last_exc = exc
626	            outcome = (
627	                f"retryable_network:{type(exc).__name__}"
628	                if attempt_idx < _MAX_RETRIES
629	                else f"error:{type(exc).__name__}"
630	            )
631	            attempts.append(RetrievalAttempt(
632	                source=source, url=full_url,
633	                attempt_index=attempt_idx,
634	                http_status=None, outcome=outcome,
635	            ))
636	            timings.append(RetrievalTiming(
637	                source=source, attempt_index=attempt_idx,
638	                duration_ms=elapsed,
639	            ))
640	            if attempt_idx < _MAX_RETRIES:
641	                time.sleep(_BACKOFF_SECONDS[attempt_idx - 1])
642	                continue
643	            return (None, outcome, attempts, timings)
644	        except Exception as exc:  # network / TLS / unexpected
645	            elapsed = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)
646	            last_exc = exc
647	            outcome = f"error:network:{type(exc).__name__}"
648	            attempts.append(RetrievalAttempt(
649	                source=source, url=full_url,
650	                attempt_index=attempt_idx,
651	                http_status=None, outcome=outcome,
652	            ))
653	            timings.append(RetrievalTiming(
654	                source=source, attempt_index=attempt_idx,
655	                duration_ms=elapsed,
656	            ))
657	            return (None, outcome, attempts, timings)
658	
659	        elapsed = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)
660	
661	        if r.status_code == 200:
662	            attempts.append(RetrievalAttempt(
663	                source=source, url=full_url,
664	                attempt_index=attempt_idx,
665	                http_status=200, outcome="success",
666	            ))
667	            timings.append(RetrievalTiming(
668	                source=source, attempt_index=attempt_idx,
669	                duration_ms=elapsed,
670	            ))
671	            return (r, "success", attempts, timings)
672	
673	        if r.status_code == 404:
674	            attempts.append(RetrievalAttempt(
675	                source=source, url=full_url,
676	                attempt_index=attempt_idx,
677	                http_status=404, outcome="not_found",
678	            ))
679	            timings.append(RetrievalTiming(
680	                source=source, attempt_index=attempt_idx,
681	                duration_ms=elapsed,
682	            ))
683	            return (r, "not_found", attempts, timings)
684	
685	        if r.status_code in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
686	            # Retryable. Log attempt, maybe continue.
687	            if attempt_idx < _MAX_RETRIES:
688	                attempts.append(RetrievalAttempt(
689	                    source=source, url=full_url,
690	                    attempt_index=attempt_idx,
691	                    http_status=r.status_code,
692	                    outcome=f"retryable_http_{r.status_code}",
693	                ))
694	                timings.append(RetrievalTiming(
695	                    source=source, attempt_index=attempt_idx,
696	                    duration_ms=elapsed,
697	                ))
698	                time.sleep(_BACKOFF_SECONDS[attempt_idx - 1])
699	                continue
700	            # Final retry failed.
701	            outcome = f"error:http_{r.status_code}"
702	            attempts.append(RetrievalAttempt(
703	                source=source, url=full_url,
704	                attempt_index=attempt_idx,
705	                http_status=r.status_code, outcome=outcome,
706	            ))
707	            timings.append(RetrievalTiming(
708	                source=source, attempt_index=attempt_idx,
709	                duration_ms=elapsed,
710	            ))
711	            return (r, outcome, attempts, timings)
712	
713	        # Non-retryable (400, 401, 403, etc.)
714	        outcome = f"error:http_{r.status_code}"
715	        attempts.append(RetrievalAttempt(
716	            source=source, url=full_url,
717	            attempt_index=attempt_idx,
718	            http_status=r.status_code, outcome=outcome,
719	        ))
720	        timings.append(RetrievalTiming(
721	            source=source, attempt_index=attempt_idx,
722	            duration_ms=elapsed,
723	        ))
724	        return (r, outcome, attempts, timings)
725	
726	    # Exhausted retries
727	    outcome = (
728	        f"error:exhausted:{type(last_exc).__name__ if last_exc else 'unknown'}"
729	    )
730	    return (None, outcome, attempts, timings)
731	
732	
733	def _call_crossref(
734	    client: httpx.Client, doi: str
735	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any] | None, list[RetrievalAttempt], list[RetrievalTiming]]:
736	    url = _CROSSREF_BASE + _urlsafe_doi(doi)
737	    r, outcome, attempts, timings = _request_with_retry(
738	        client, "GET", "crossref", url,
739	    )
740	    if outcome != "success" or r is None:
741	        return None, attempts, timings
742	    try:
743	        data = r.json()
744	    except ValueError:
745	        # Replace the final success attempt with an invalid_json
746	        # failure record so the log is honest about the outcome.
747	        last = attempts[-1]
748	        attempts[-1] = RetrievalAttempt(
749	            source=last.source, url=last.url,
750	            attempt_index=last.attempt_index,
751	            http_status=last.http_status,
752	            outcome="error:invalid_json",
753	        )
754	        return None, attempts, timings
755	    return data, attempts, timings

759	    client: httpx.Client, doi: str, *, email: str | None = None
760	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any] | None, list[RetrievalAttempt], list[RetrievalTiming]]:
761	    email = email or os.getenv("PG_UNPAYWALL_EMAIL", "polaris@example.org")
762	    url = _UNPAYWALL_BASE + _urlsafe_doi(doi)
763	    r, outcome, attempts, timings = _request_with_retry(
764	        client, "GET", "unpaywall", url,
765	        params={"email": email},
766	    )
767	    if outcome != "success" or r is None:
768	        return None, attempts, timings
769	    try:
770	        data = r.json()
771	    except ValueError:
772	        last = attempts[-1]
773	        attempts[-1] = RetrievalAttempt(
774	            source=last.source, url=last.url,
775	            attempt_index=last.attempt_index,
776	            http_status=last.http_status,
777	            outcome="error:invalid_json",
778	        )
779	        return None, attempts, timings
780	    return data, attempts, timings

783	def _call_pubmed(
784	    client: httpx.Client, pmid: str
785	) -> tuple[str | None, list[RetrievalAttempt], list[RetrievalTiming]]:
786	    url = _PUBMED_EFETCH_BASE
787	    params = {
788	        "db": "pubmed",
789	        "id": str(pmid),
790	        "rettype": "abstract",
791	        "retmode": "xml",
792	    }
793	    r, outcome, attempts, timings = _request_with_retry(
794	        client, "GET", "pubmed", url, params=params,
795	    )
796	    if outcome != "success" or r is None:
797	        return None, attempts, timings
798	    body = r.text or ""
799	    if not body.strip():
800	        # PubMed returns 200 empty for unknown PMIDs. Replace the
801	        # success record with not_found so the log matches reality.
802	        last = attempts[-1]
803	        attempts[-1] = RetrievalAttempt(
804	            source=last.source, url=last.url,
805	            attempt_index=last.attempt_index,
806	            http_status=last.http_status,
807	            outcome="not_found",
808	        )
809	        return None, attempts, timings
810	    return body, attempts, timings

813	def _call_openalex(
814	    client: httpx.Client, doi: str
815	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any] | None, list[RetrievalAttempt], list[RetrievalTiming]]:
816	    """Deterministic OpenAlex /works/{doi} fetch (issue #1033).
817	
818	    Abstract fallback for journal DOIs where CrossRef returned no
819	    abstract and the OA full-text PDF was paywalled (403). Uses the
820	    same retry/attempt-logging discipline as the other callers so
821	    M-60 manifest telemetry shows the OpenAlex attempt."""
822	    email = os.getenv("PG_UNPAYWALL_EMAIL", "polaris@example.org")
823	    url = _OPENALEX_WORK_BASE + "https://doi.org/" + _urlsafe_doi(doi)
824	    r, outcome, attempts, timings = _request_with_retry(
825	        client, "GET", "openalex", url, params={"mailto": email},
826	    )
827	    if outcome != "success" or r is None:
828	        return None, attempts, timings
829	    try:
830	        data = r.json()
831	    except ValueError:
832	        last = attempts[-1]
833	        attempts[-1] = RetrievalAttempt(
834	            source=last.source, url=last.url,
835	            attempt_index=last.attempt_index,
836	            http_status=last.http_status,
837	            outcome="error:invalid_json",
838	        )
839	        return None, attempts, timings
840	    return data, attempts, timings

843	def _call_s2(
844	    client: httpx.Client, doi: str
845	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any] | None, list[RetrievalAttempt], list[RetrievalTiming]]:
846	    """Deterministic Semantic Scholar Graph API /paper/DOI:{doi} fetch — M3b
847	    (I-deepfix-001) 3rd abstract source.
848	
849	    Mirrors ``_call_openalex``: same ``_request_with_retry`` discipline (one
850	    RetrievalAttempt per HTTP request, fixed backoff, status->outcome mapping)
851	    so M-60 manifest telemetry shows the S2 attempt. An optional
852	    ``SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY`` (already an env in the codebase) is sent as the
853	    ``x-api-key`` header for rate-limit headroom; unauthenticated still works
854	    (S2 throttles, which the 429 backoff already handles)."""
855	    headers: dict[str, str] = {}
856	    key = os.getenv("SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY", "").strip()
857	    if key:
858	        headers["x-api-key"] = key
859	    url = _S2_WORK_BASE + _urlsafe_doi(doi)
860	    r, outcome, attempts, timings = _request_with_retry(
861	        client, "GET", "s2", url,
862	        params={"fields": "title,abstract,year,venue,externalIds"},
863	        headers=headers,
864	    )
865	    if outcome != "success" or r is None:
866	        return None, attempts, timings
867	    try:
868	        data = r.json()
869	    except ValueError:
870	        last = attempts[-1]
871	        attempts[-1] = RetrievalAttempt(
872	            source=last.source, url=last.url,
873	            attempt_index=last.attempt_index,
874	            http_status=last.http_status,
875	            outcome="error:invalid_json",
876	        )
877	        return None, attempts, timings
878	    return data, attempts, timings

881	def _parse_s2_response(data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
882	    """Extract abstract + metadata from a Semantic Scholar Graph API
883	    /paper/DOI:{doi} JSON object (M3b). Returns dict with keys: title, authors,
884	    journal, year, abstract, doi (normalized, prefix-stripped, lowercased).
885	    Values may be None/empty when absent. DOI is read from ``externalIds.DOI``
886	    so the caller's DOI-consistency guard can reject a wrong-paper response
887	    (mirrors the OpenAlex/PubMed guards)."""
888	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
889	        return {}
890	
891	    abstract = data.get("abstract")
892	    if isinstance(abstract, str):
893	        abstract = abstract.strip() or None
894	    else:
895	        abstract = None
896	
897	    title = data.get("title")
898	    if isinstance(title, str):
899	        title = title.strip() or None
900	    else:
901	        title = None
902	
903	    journal = data.get("venue")
904	    if isinstance(journal, str):
905	        journal = journal.strip() or None
906	    else:
907	        journal = None
908	
909	    year = data.get("year")
910	    if not isinstance(year, int):
911	        year = None
912	
913	    doi_norm: str | None = None
914	    ext = data.get("externalIds")
915	    if isinstance(ext, dict):
916	        doi_raw = ext.get("DOI") or ext.get("doi") or ""
917	        if isinstance(doi_raw, str):
918	            doi_norm = doi_raw.lower().strip()
919	            for prefix in ("https://doi.org/", "http://doi.org/", "doi:"):
920	                if doi_norm.startswith(prefix):
921	                    doi_norm = doi_norm[len(prefix):]
922	                    break
923	            doi_norm = doi_norm.rstrip("/") or None
924	
925	    return {
926	        "title": title,
927	        "authors": (),
928	        "journal": journal,
929	        "year": year,
930	        "abstract": abstract,
931	        "doi": doi_norm,
932	    }

935	def _looks_like_html_junk(text: str) -> bool:
936	    """True when a fetched 'full text' is actually raw HTML markup or a
937	    Sci-Hub wrapper page rather than clean extracted prose (#1034
938	    follow-up). Ground-truthed: aeaweb paywalled econ papers (e.g.
939	    Acemoglu 10.1257/jep.33.2.3) come back as a ~25K-char Sci-Hub HTML
940	    viewer page that passes any length threshold but is useless for M-58
941	    extraction. Inspects the head of the content for HTML structural
942	    markers or Sci-Hub branding (case-insensitive)."""
943	    head = text[:600].lower()
944	    return (
945	        "<!doctype" in head
946	        or "<html" in head
947	        or "<head>" in head
948	        or "<head " in head
949	        or "<body" in head
950	        or "sci-hub" in head
951	    )
952

954	def _is_usable_full_text(text: str | None) -> bool:
955	    """A fetched OA full text is usable as the rich `direct_quote` source
956	    only if it is SUBSTANTIAL (>= _OA_FULLTEXT_MIN_CHARS) AND clean prose
957	    (not HTML / Sci-Hub junk). Otherwise the abstract fallbacks
958	    (CrossRef / OpenAlex / PubMed) must take over (#1034)."""
959	    return bool(
960	        text
961	        and len(text) >= _OA_FULLTEXT_MIN_CHARS
962	        and not _looks_like_html_junk(text)
963	    )

965	
966	def _pick_richest_abstract(
967	    *,
968	    crossref: str | None,
969	    openalex: str | None,
970	    pubmed: str | None,
971	    s2: str | None = None,
972	    partial_full_text: str | None = None,
973	) -> tuple[str, str]:
974	    """Choose the RICHEST (longest) available abstract text + its
975	    quote_source label. Candidates in priority order CrossRef >
976	    OpenAlex > PubMed > Semantic Scholar (M3b) > thin-OA-full-text
977	    partial; the LONGEST wins, ties break toward the higher-priority
978	    source (deterministic — strictly-greater comparison while
979	    iterating priority order). ``s2`` defaults to None so existing
980	    keyword callers are byte-identical.
981	
982	    A thin oa_full_text stub (paywalled-PDF Jina result, ~540 chars)
983	    is admitted only as the last-priority `partial_full_text`
984	    candidate, so a real abstract (e.g. OpenAlex 1331 chars) overrides
985	    it rather than being blocked by it (issue #1034). Returns
986	    ("", "none") when every candidate is empty."""
987	    candidates: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
988	    if crossref:
989	        candidates.append((crossref, "crossref_abstract"))
990	    if openalex:
991	        candidates.append((openalex, "openalex_abstract"))
992	    if pubmed:
993	        candidates.append((pubmed, "pubmed_abstract"))
994	    if s2:
995	        candidates.append((s2, "s2_abstract"))
996	    # A thin OA full-text stub is a TRUE last resort: admit it ONLY when
997	    # no real abstract resolved. Per §-1.1 clinical-safety (dual-audit
998	    # finding #1034), a paywall junk stub must never become the extracted
999	    # span when a real abstract exists — even if the stub is longer.
1000	    if partial_full_text and not candidates:
1001	        candidates.append((partial_full_text, "oa_full_text_partial"))
1002	    best: tuple[str, str] | None = None
1003	    for text, src in candidates:
1004	        if best is None or len(text) > len(best[0]):
1005	            best = (text, src)
1006	    return best if best is not None else ("", "none")

1009	def _urlsafe_doi(doi: str) -> str:
1010	    """CrossRef and Unpaywall accept DOIs verbatim; percent-encode
1011	    any slashes? The standard is to pass the raw DOI. Both APIs
1012	    accept both forms. We keep it raw — no encoding needed for the
1013	    typical DOI characters (10.XXXX/YYYY)."""
1014	    return doi.strip()

1017	# V30 Phase-2 M-66b content-fetch helper (Codex pass-3 CONDITIONAL-
1018	# no-blockers approved). Wraps POLARIS's existing AccessBypass
1019	# stack (Crawl4AI + Jina Reader + Firecrawl concurrent) used
1020	# elsewhere for content extraction. Used by M-56 for:
1021	#
1022	#   - M-66b-R: url_pattern-primary regulatory entities (FDA, EMA,
1023	#     NICE, HC landing pages) where no DOI/PMID exists.
1024	#   - M-66b-T: OA PDF/HTML full-text fetch when Unpaywall
1025	#     surfaced an OA URL — upgrades direct_quote from abstract
1026	#     to full text so M-58 can extract 9-field SURPASS rosters.
1027	#
1028	# Returns `(content_str, final_url_str)` on success, `("", "")` on
1029	# failure. Caller decides provenance class + attempt logging.
1030	# Content is truncated at `_M66_CONTENT_CAP` chars for prompt-
1031	# budget predictability.
1032	_M66_CONTENT_CAP = 25000
1033	
1034	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1035	# A1 (iarch006 RC1) — fetch-INGESTION shell detector.
1036	#
1037	# The url-pattern / OA-locator fetch layer previously accepted ANY
1038	# non-empty body as bound evidence (`return content[:_M66_CONTENT_CAP]`).
1039	# In the Q90 epic-failure run every mandated legal section was wired to
1040	# ONE web page that returned HTTP 200 but whose captured text was page
1041	# FURNITURE — an Archive.org JS wrapper, a CourtListener docket index
1042	# ("Filing fee $402.00") instead of the verdict, a literal "Page not
1043	# found" NTSB body. No real prose to ground on, so the section honestly
1044	# printed an empty gap stub even though real case-law content sat in the
1045	# pool unbound.
1046	#
1047	# This detector keys ONLY on FETCH-INTEGRITY (chrome / soft-404 / docket
1048	# index / JS-wrapper / content-starvation), NEVER on topicality. It must
1049	# NOT become a generic relevance hard-drop on real-but-junk-host content
1050	# — that would be the §-1.3-banned FILTER. A detected shell is routed to
1051	# the EXISTING METADATA_ONLY / not_extractable branch (a gap/recovery
1052	# signal), NOT a new hard-drop and NOT bound evidence.
1053	#
1054	# Minimum chars of recovered main content for a fetched body to count as
1055	# real prose (env-overridable, LAW VI). Sits at the same order of
1056	# magnitude as live_retriever.is_content_starved's 200-char useful-text
1057	# floor; the fetch layer wants a slightly higher bar because a bound
1058	# frame-entity quote feeds slot prose, not a corpus weight.
1059	_MIN_MAINCONTENT_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PG_MIN_MAINCONTENT_CHARS", "250"))
1060	
1061	# Above this fraction of lines being pure link/nav markers a body is a
1062	# navigation index / docket listing, not article prose (env, LAW VI).
1063	_MAX_LINK_DENSITY = float(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_MAX_LINK_DENSITY", "0.6"))
1064	
1065	# A body shorter than this is treated as a single unit for the
1066	# boilerplate / non-assertional whole-unit check (a real article body is
1067	# far longer; a stub / docket index / error page is short). Env, LAW VI.
1068	_SHELL_WHOLE_UNIT_MAX_CHARS = int(
1069	    os.getenv("PG_FETCH_SHELL_WHOLE_UNIT_MAX_CHARS", "1200")
1070	)
1071	
1072	
1073	def _link_density(text: str) -> float:
1074	    """Fraction of non-blank lines that are pure link / nav / bullet
1075	    markers (markdown `[...](...)`, bare URLs, or `* `/`- ` list chrome
1076	    with little prose). A docket index or a JS-wrapper landing page is
1077	    almost entirely such lines; a real article is almost none.
1078	
1079	    Pure fetch-integrity signal — measures STRUCTURE, never topic."""
1080	    lines = [ln.strip() for ln in text.splitlines() if ln.strip()]
1081	    if not lines:
1082	        return 1.0
1083	    import re as _re
1084	
1085	    link_like = 0
1086	    for ln in lines:
1087	        # A markdown link line, a bare-URL line, or a short nav/list-glue
1088	        # line dominated by a link token.
1089	        if _re.match(r"^[*\-•]?\s*\[[^\]]*\]\([^)]*\)\s*$", ln):
1090	            link_like += 1
1091	        elif _re.match(r"^https?://\S+$", ln):
1092	            link_like += 1
1093	        elif "](" in ln and len(_re.sub(r"\[[^\]]*\]\([^)]*\)", "", ln).strip()) < 24:
1094	            link_like += 1
1095	    return link_like / len(lines)
1096

1098	def _is_fetch_shell(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
1099	    """A1 — return ``(True, reason)`` when a fetched body is a fetch-layer
1100	    SHELL (page furniture / soft-404 / docket index / JS-wrapper /
1101	    content-starved) rather than real article prose to ground on.
1102	
1103	    Keys ONLY on fetch-integrity. NEVER inspects topicality — a real
1104	    article about an unrelated subject is NOT a shell. The detector
1105	    REUSES the existing markers:
1106	      - `_looks_like_html_junk` (raw-HTML / Sci-Hub wrapper) → recover
1107	        the main text via the guarded `safe_trafilatura_extract`
1108	        (favor_precision) before measuring, so a real article that the
1109	        backend returned as raw HTML is NOT mis-flagged.
1110	      - `is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional` (access_bypass) — the GATE-layer
1111	        chrome / bare-DOI / table-row / soft-404 marker, now applied at the
1112	        FETCH layer (the exact layer gap A1 names) on a short whole body.
1113	      - a `_MIN_MAINCONTENT_CHARS` floor and a `_MAX_LINK_DENSITY` cap.
1114	
1115	    The empty-string return is the caller's existing "no usable content"
1116	    signal — it routes to the METADATA_ONLY / not_extractable branch."""
1117	    if not content:
1118	        return True, "empty_body"
1119	
1120	    body = content
1121	    # If the body is raw HTML markup (Sci-Hub viewer / JS-wrapper), recover
1122	    # the main text via the ONE guarded trafilatura entrypoint before
1123	    # measuring — a real article wrapped in markup must survive.
1124	    if _looks_like_html_junk(body):
1125	        try:
1126	            from src.tools.access_bypass import safe_trafilatura_extract
1127	
1128	            recovered = safe_trafilatura_extract(
1129	                body, favor_precision=True, include_links=False
1130	            )
1131	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — recovery is best-effort.
1132	            recovered = None
1133	        if recovered and len(recovered.strip()) >= _MIN_MAINCONTENT_CHARS:
1134	            # Real prose was hidden under the markup — not a shell.
1135	            return False, ""
1136	        # No real main content recovered from the markup → shell.
1137	        return True, "js_wrapper_no_maincontent"
1138	
1139	    stripped = body.strip()
1140	
1141	    # Content-starvation floor (fetch-integrity, not topicality).
1142	    if len(stripped) < _MIN_MAINCONTENT_CHARS:
1143	        # A short body that IS a known chrome / soft-404 / docket-row
1144	        # stub is a shell; reuse the gate-layer marker at the fetch layer.
1145	        try:
1146	            from src.tools.access_bypass import is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional
1147	
1148	            if is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional(stripped):
1149	                return True, "boilerplate_or_error_stub"
1150	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1151	            pass
1152	        return True, "content_starved"
1153	
1154	    # A short-ish body that is wholly non-assertional chrome / a docket
1155	    # index / a soft-404 (the gate-layer marker, now at fetch layer).
1156	    if len(stripped) <= _SHELL_WHOLE_UNIT_MAX_CHARS:
1157	        try:
1158	            from src.tools.access_bypass import is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional
1159	
1160	            if is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional(stripped):
1161	                return True, "boilerplate_or_error_stub"
1162	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1163	            pass
1164	
1165	    # I-fetchclean-001 A4: an any-length whole-body fetch-shell (bot-wall /
1166	    # security-verification interstitial / crawler HTTP-error wrapper) is a failed
1167	    # fetch, not an index page — the link-density test below does not see it. Reuse
1168	    # the SINGLE-SOURCE cited-span shell detector (LAW V; same package) so the SAME
1169	    # bot-wall vocabulary that guards the cited-span gate also guards this seam. Local
1170	    # import keeps this leaf's import-time deps unchanged; the detector is pure.
1171	    try:
1172	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import shell_detector
1173	
1174	        if shell_detector.is_cited_span_shell(stripped):
1175	            return True, "bot_wall_shell_vocab"
1176	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — detector fault must never break fetch-shell check
1177	        pass
1178	
1179	    # Navigation-index / docket-listing structure (high link density) —
1180	    # a fetch-integrity STRUCTURE signal, never a topic signal.
1181	    if _link_density(stripped) >= _MAX_LINK_DENSITY:
1182	        return True, "link_density_index_page"
1183	
1184	    return False, ""

1187	def _fetch_url_pattern(url: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
1188	    """Fetch content at `url` via AccessBypass. Returns
1189	    `(content, final_url)` or `("", "")` on failure.
1190	
1191	    Deterministic-test seam: this function is the mockable
1192	    boundary for M-66b-R / M-66b-T tests (stub via
1193	    `monkeypatch.setattr("...frame_fetcher._fetch_url_pattern",
1194	    lambda u: ("stubbed content", u))`).
1195	
1196	    AccessBypass.fetch_with_bypass is async; M-56 is sync. The
1197	    helper wraps the async call in asyncio.run(). If an event
1198	    loop is already running in the caller's thread (live server
1199	    context), we fall back to a fresh thread + new loop so this

1200	    helper is safe to invoke from any caller context.
1201	    """
1202	    if not url:
1203	        return "", ""
1204	    try:
1205	        import asyncio as _asyncio
1206	        from src.tools.access_bypass import AccessBypass
1207	
1208	        async def _run() -> tuple[str, str]:
1209	            ab = AccessBypass()
1210	            result = await ab.fetch_with_bypass(url)
1211	            if result is None or not getattr(result, "success", False):
1212	                return "", ""
1213	            content = getattr(result, "content", "") or ""
1214	            final_url = getattr(result, "url", "") or url
1215	            method = (getattr(result, "access_method", "") or "").lower()
1216	            # Never use pirate-source (Sci-Hub) content in a research /
1217	            # clinical product — legal + provenance (#1034 dual-audit P1).
1218	            # Rejects BOTH the Sci-Hub HTML viewer page AND clean Sci-Hub
1219	            # PDF text (which _looks_like_html_junk cannot detect).
1220	            if "scihub" in method or "sci-hub" in method:
1221	                return "", ""
1222	            if not content:
1223	                return "", ""
1224	            # I-beatboth-010 (#1288) FIX-A: strip Jina/Crawl4AI reader chrome
1225	            # (Title:/URL Source:/Published Time:/Number of Pages:/Markdown
1226	            # Content: preamble) BEFORE the shell check + evidence binding, so the
1227	            # fetched body returned from this path is clean. Input hygiene only;
1228	            # faithfulness gates untouched. An all-chrome body cleans to empty and
1229	            # is then correctly routed to the existing METADATA_ONLY gap branch.
1230	            from src.tools.access_bypass import clean_fetch_body
1231	            content = clean_fetch_body(content).cleaned_text
1232	            if not content:
1233	                return "", ""
1234	            # A1 (iarch006 RC1): reject a fetch-layer SHELL (page furniture
1235	            # / soft-404 / docket index / JS-wrapper / content-starved)
1236	            # BEFORE it is bound as evidence. Keys on fetch-integrity only,
1237	            # never topicality. Returning ("", "") routes the caller to the
1238	            # EXISTING METADATA_ONLY / not_extractable branch — a gap/
1239	            # recovery signal, not bound evidence and not a new hard-drop.
1240	            is_shell, shell_reason = _is_fetch_shell(content)
1241	            if is_shell:
1242	                logger.info(
1243	                    "[frame_fetcher] A1 fetch-shell rejected url=%s "
1244	                    "reason=%s len=%d → METADATA_ONLY gap/recovery",
1245	                    final_url or url, shell_reason, len(content),
1246	                )
1247	                return "", ""
1248	            return content[:_M66_CONTENT_CAP], final_url
1249	
1250	        try:
1251	            # Probe for a running loop. asyncio.get_running_loop
1252	            # raises RuntimeError when no loop is running (normal
1253	            # sync context); the no-warning way to ask.
1254	            _asyncio.get_running_loop()
1255	            # A loop IS running — spin a fresh one in a worker
1256	            # thread so we don't collide with it (e.g. live-server
1257	            # FastAPI context).
1258	            import concurrent.futures as _cf
1259	
1260	            def _thread_run() -> tuple[str, str]:
1261	                return _asyncio.run(_run())
1262	
1263	            with _cf.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as ex:
1264	                return ex.submit(_thread_run).result()
1265	        except RuntimeError:
1266	            # No running loop — asyncio.run will create one.
1267	            return _asyncio.run(_run())
1268	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1269	        # AccessBypass raised — don't propagate; M-56 treats as
1270	        # failed fetch and continues with whatever it has.
1271	        return "", ""
1272

1277	def fetch_frame_entity(
1278	    binding: EvidenceBinding,
1279	    *,
1280	    client: httpx.Client | None = None,
1281	) -> FrameRow:
1282	    """Deterministic frame-entity retriever.
1283	
1284	    Given a compiled EvidenceBinding (from M-55), emit one FrameRow
1285	    with provenance_class, direct_quote, metadata, and retrieval
1286	    attempt log.
1287	
1288	    Args:
1289	        binding: M-55 compiled binding with primary_identifier +
1290	            secondary_identifiers.
1291	        client: optional httpx.Client for DI (testing). When None,
1292	            a fresh client is created per call with _DEFAULT_TIMEOUT.
1293	
1294	    Returns:
1295	        FrameRow — always non-None. A FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE row is
1296	        still a valid return — callers inspect `is_gap()` or
1297	        `provenance_class` to branch.
1298	
1299	    Deterministic per design — same (binding, upstream API state) →
1300	    byte-identical FrameRow. No randomness, no wall-clock in payload.
1301	    """
1302	    owns_client = client is None
1303	    if client is None:
1304	        client = httpx.Client(timeout=_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT)
1305	    try:
1306	        return _fetch_frame_entity_inner(binding, client)
1307	    finally:
1308	        if owns_client:
1309	            client.close()

1312	def _fetch_frame_entity_inner(
1313	    binding: EvidenceBinding,
1314	    client: httpx.Client,
1315	) -> FrameRow:
1316	    """Core dispatch: pick strategy by primary_identifier prefix."""
1317	    identifiers = _collect_identifiers(binding)
1318	
1319	    # URL-pattern-primary entities (regulatory): V30 Phase-2 M-66b-R
1320	    # lifts these from METADATA_ONLY to OPEN_ACCESS when content can
1321	    # be fetched via the POLARIS AccessBypass helper. Codex pass-3
1322	    # CONDITIONAL-no-blockers approved this scope.
1323	    if binding.primary_identifier.startswith("url:") and not (
1324	        identifiers.get("doi") or identifiers.get("pmid")
1325	    ):
1326	        url = identifiers["url"]
1327	        fetched_content, fetched_url = _fetch_url_pattern(url)
1328	        # I-wire-013 (#1327): repair PDF/HTML line-wrap hyphens in the stored,

1797	def _collect_identifiers(binding: EvidenceBinding) -> dict[str, str]:
1798	    """Flatten primary + secondary identifiers into a {kind: value}
1799	    dict. Kinds: 'doi', 'pmid', 'url', 'anchor'."""
1800	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
1801	    for ident in (binding.primary_identifier, *binding.secondary_identifiers):
1802	        if ":" not in ident:
1803	            continue
1804	        kind, value = ident.split(":", 1)
1805	        out.setdefault(kind, value)
1806	    return out
1807

1809	def _summarize_failure(attempts: list[RetrievalAttempt]) -> str:
1810	    """Compose a short failure_reason string from the attempt log.
1811	    Consumed by M-60 manifest rendering. Summarizes the FINAL
1812	    outcome per source (aggregates retry noise, while the full
1813	    chain remains visible via FrameRow.retrieval_attempts)."""
1814	    if not attempts:
1815	        return "no retrieval attempted (no resolvable identifier)"
1816	    # Collapse per-source by taking the last attempt for each
1817	    # source (which carries the terminal outcome).
1818	    final_by_source: dict[str, RetrievalAttempt] = {}
1819	    for a in attempts:
1820	        final_by_source[a.source] = a
1821	    summaries = [
1822	        f"{a.source}={a.outcome}({a.http_status})"
1823	        for a in final_by_source.values()
1824	    ]
1825	    return "all sources failed: " + "; ".join(summaries)

content.content:
1	"""
2	Evidence selector — BUG-M-201 fix (deep-dive R6).
3	
4	Pre-fix, `run_one_query` passed `retrieval.evidence_rows[:PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN]`
5	to the generator — raw retrieval order with no tier awareness. Gates
6	reasoned over the full pool; generation reasoned over a smaller,
7	order-biased subset. Real artifacts showed 20 corpus sources but
8	only 4 evidence rows surviving to generation.
9	
10	Post-fix, `select_evidence_for_generation()` produces a deterministic,
11	tier-balanced, relevance-ranked subset. The result is emitted to
12	`manifest.json.evidence_selection` so downstream consumers can see
13	exactly what the generator saw.
14	"""
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import logging
18	import math
19	import os
20	import re
21	from collections import Counter, defaultdict
22	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, replace
23	from typing import Any
24	
25	
26	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	
29	# Tier priority for within-pool ranking. Lower tier number = higher priority.
30	_TIER_PRIORITY: dict[str, int] = {
31	    "T1": 1, "T2": 2, "T3": 3, "T4": 4,
32	    "T5": 5, "T6": 6, "T7": 7, "UNKNOWN": 8,
33	}
34	
35	# I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989): default relevance floor for `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`.
36	_DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR = 0.30
37	
38	# BUG-14 (GH #1262, §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER): default min embeddable chars below
39	# which a row is treated as a degenerate / stub fetch (UNKNOWN-relevance, NOT
40	# off-topic) and so floor-EXEMPTED rather than hard-dropped on its near-zero cosine.
41	# Overridable via `PG_SELECT_DEGENERATE_MIN_CHARS` (LAW VI).
42	_DEFAULT_SELECT_DEGENERATE_MIN_CHARS = 24
43	
44	
45	def parse_relevance_floor(
46	    raw: str | None, *, default: float = _DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR,
47	) -> float:
48	    """Parse + validate ``PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`` (I-meta-005 Phase 5 #989).
49	
50	    Range is (0.0, 1.0]. Empty/None -> ``default``. An unparseable float OR an
51	    out-of-range value raises ``ValueError`` (FAIL LOUD) — a missing/garbage floor
52	    must never silently send an unbounded, unfiltered pool to the generator.
53	    """
54	    if raw is None or not str(raw).strip():
55	        value = default
56	    else:
57	        try:
58	            value = float(str(raw).strip())
59	        except ValueError as exc:
60	            raise ValueError(
61	                "PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR must be a float in (0.0, 1.0]; got "
62	                f"{raw!r}"
63	            ) from exc
64	    if not (0.0 < value <= 1.0):
65	        raise ValueError(
66	            f"PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR out of range (0.0, 1.0]: {value}"
67	        )
68	    return value
69	
70	
71	# ── I-arch-004 F24 (#1255): generator-facing evidence cap default ────────────
72	# THE BUG: every direct entry point (run_honest_sweep_r3, run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus,
73	# run_live_honest_cycle) read ``int(os.getenv("PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN", "20"))`` — a hardcoded
74	# default of 20. The Gate-B cert slate overrides it to 1500, so the starvation is LATENT on the
75	# certification path; but a DIRECT / bypass / flags-OFF caller silently capped the generator at
76	# 20 evidence rows regardless of how large the corpus was. A 20-evidence generation off a
77	# multi-hundred-source corpus is a starved generation (CLAUDE.md §-1.3: a silent thinner).
78	#
79	# THE FIX (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, not a bigger silent cap): when the env is UNSET, default
80	# to the FULL pool — i.e. NO cap (the selector already keeps everything when ``pool_size <=
81	# max_rows``, see the ``len(scored) <= max_rows`` keep-all branch). When the env IS set, honor it
82	# (LAW VI) but emit a LOUD WARNING whenever the cap actually BINDS (cap < pool_size) so a direct
83	# caller is never SILENTLY truncated. Truncation is always observable; it is never silent.
84	_MAX_EV_TO_GEN_ENV = "PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN"
85	
86	
87	def resolve_max_ev_to_gen(pool_size: int) -> int:
88	    """Resolve the generator-facing evidence cap for a corpus of ``pool_size`` rows.
89	
90	    Returns the effective ``max_rows`` to hand the tier-balanced selector:
91	
92	    * Env UNSET  -> ``pool_size`` (full-corpus floor; the selector keeps every row, NO silent
93	      20-cap). A non-positive/empty corpus returns 0 (the selector's own
94	      ``max_rows <= 0`` short-circuit handles it).
95	    * Env SET    -> the parsed integer (LAW VI operator override). If that cap is smaller than the
96	      corpus (it BINDS), log a LOUD WARNING naming the drop count, so a direct caller can SEE the
97	      truncation. A non-binding or absurd (<=0) value still defers to the selector's own guards.
98	
99	    This is a CAPABILITY FLOOR, not a thinner: the default path delivers the full pool; any
100	    reduction is operator-chosen AND announced.
101	    """
102	    safe_pool = max(0, int(pool_size))
103	    raw = os.getenv(_MAX_EV_TO_GEN_ENV)
104	    if raw is None or not str(raw).strip():
105	        # Full-corpus floor: feed every available row to the selector (keep-all).
106	        return safe_pool
107	    try:
108	        cap = int(str(raw).strip())
109	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
110	        _LOGGER.warning(
111	            "[evidence_selector] F24: %s=%r is not an integer — defaulting to the "
112	            "full-corpus floor (%d rows, no cap) rather than a silent starve.",
113	            _MAX_EV_TO_GEN_ENV, raw, safe_pool,
114	        )
115	        return safe_pool
116	    if 0 < cap < safe_pool:
117	        _LOGGER.warning(
118	            "[evidence_selector] F24: %s=%d BINDS on a %d-row corpus — the generator will see "
119	            "only %d of %d rows (%d dropped). This is an operator-set cap, NOT a silent default; "
120	            "unset %s to feed the full corpus.",
121	            _MAX_EV_TO_GEN_ENV, cap, safe_pool, cap, safe_pool, safe_pool - cap,
122	            _MAX_EV_TO_GEN_ENV,
123	        )
124	    return cap
125

127	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
128	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "is", "are", "was", "were",
129	    "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for", "with", "by", "from", "as",
130	    "that", "this", "these", "those", "it", "its", "be", "been",
131	    "what", "which", "who", "how", "why", "when", "where",
132	})

135	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
136	    """Lowercase content-word tokens, stopword-filtered, 3+ chars."""
137	    toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{2,}", text.lower())
138	    return {t for t in toks if t not in _STOPWORDS}

141	@dataclass
142	class EvidenceSelection:
143	    """Result of select_evidence_for_generation."""
144	    selected_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]
145	    full_counts: dict[str, int]        # tier -> count in FULL pool
146	    selected_counts: dict[str, int]    # tier -> count in SELECTED subset
147	    dropped_count: int
148	    selection_strategy: str            # stable identifier for telemetry
149	    notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
150	
151	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
152	        return {
153	            "evidence_total": (
154	                sum(self.full_counts.values())
155	            ),
156	            "evidence_selected": len(self.selected_rows),
157	            "selection_strategy": self.selection_strategy,
158	            "full_tier_counts": dict(self.full_counts),
159	            "selected_tier_counts": dict(self.selected_counts),
160	            "dropped_count": self.dropped_count,
161	            "notes": list(self.notes),
162	        }

165	def _row_tier(row: dict[str, Any], url_to_tier: dict[str, str]) -> str:
166	    """Resolve tier for an evidence row via URL join to classified sources.
167	    Falls back to row's own `tier` field, then UNKNOWN."""
168	    url = row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or ""
169	    if url in url_to_tier:
170	        return url_to_tier[url]
171	    tier = row.get("tier") or "UNKNOWN"
172	    return str(tier)

175	# M-41d (2026-04-21): jurisdictional-diversity floor for T3 regulatory
176	# selection. V24 had 0 Health Canada entries in its bibliography

178	# prompt rule, but the evidence selector tier-quota gave T3 only 12
179	# slots that all went to FDA/EMA/NICE since they scored higher on
180	# lexical relevance than a Canadian monograph). M-41d adds a floor:
181	# within the T3 quota, reserve one slot for each present jurisdiction
182	# whose evidence appears in the pool.
183	#
184	# Jurisdiction detection is by URL host suffix. A row maps to exactly
185	# one jurisdiction or None. No drug-specific tokens; the host list
186	# mirrors the regulatory_anchors template schema across domains.
187	_M41D_JURISDICTION_HOSTS: list[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]]] = [
188	    # M-41d pass-2 (Codex audit medium #3): removed bare "europa.eu"
189	    # from EMA — it was too broad (collapsed any EU-domain source
190	    # into EMA). Kept "ema.europa.eu" which is the specific EMA

194	    ("NICE", ("nice.org.uk",)),

199	    # health-products.canada.ca, pdf.hres.ca via suffix match.
200	    ("HC",   ("canada.ca", "hres.ca", "hc-sc.gc.ca", "cda-amc.ca",
201	              "hpfb-dgpsa.ca")),
202	    ("TGA",  ("tga.gov.au",)),
203	    ("PMDA", ("pmda.go.jp",)),
204	    ("WHO",  ("who.int",)),

209	# M-42d (2026-04-22): Health Canada quota expansion. V25 baseline had
210	# 1 HC entry in the bibliography (M-41d reserved 1 slot per present
211	# jurisdiction). V26 aims for >=2 HC entries covering >=2 distinct
212	# topics (e.g. monograph + recall/advisory). The expansion is a
213	# quota-bounded 2nd reservation that ONLY fires after every present
214	# jurisdiction has its 1st reservation, so FDA/EMA/NICE/MHRA each
215	# keep their baseline 1 slot regardless of HC's 2nd. Relevance-fill
216	# still runs afterwards so FDA/EMA/NICE can exceed 1 slot naturally.
217	#
218	# Env override `PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA` (default 2). Setting to 1 restores
219	# exact M-41d behavior (no HC expansion).
220	_M42D_HC_QUOTA_DEFAULT = 2
221	_M42D_HC_JURISDICTION_CODE = "HC"

223	
224	def _m42d_hc_quota() -> int:
225	    """Return the Health Canada quota (1..N) for the T3 selector floor.
226	
227	    M-42d (2026-04-22): configurable via `PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA` env var.
228	    Defaults to 2. Values <1 clamp to 1 (legacy M-41d behavior).
229	    Invalid strings fall back to default.
230	    """
231	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_M41D_HC_QUOTA", "")
232	    if not raw:
233	        return _M42D_HC_QUOTA_DEFAULT
234	    try:
235	        v = int(raw)
236	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
237	        return _M42D_HC_QUOTA_DEFAULT
238	    return max(1, v)

241	def _row_jurisdiction(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str | None:
242	    """Return the regulatory jurisdiction code a row belongs to, or
243	    None if the URL is not from a recognized regulatory host.
244	
245	    M-41d pass-2 (Codex audit medium #3): uses proper host-suffix
246	    matching instead of substring `in`. Pre-pass-2 `h in url` would
247	    classify `https://not-fda.gov.example/path` as FDA because the
248	    literal string `fda.gov` appears in the path. Now we extract the
249	    actual host component from the URL and require it to either
250	    equal one of the listed hosts or end with `.{host}` (proper
251	    subdomain). Plain substring-in-path no longer matches.
252	    """
253	    url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").lower()
254	    if not url:
255	        return None
256	    # Extract host: scheme://host/path → host. Be permissive about
257	    # missing schemes; in that case treat the first path segment as
258	    # host if it looks like a hostname.
259	    try:
260	        from urllib.parse import urlparse
261	        parsed = urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"http://{url}")
262	        host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()
263	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
264	        return None
265	    if not host:
266	        return None
267	    for code, hosts in _M41D_JURISDICTION_HOSTS:
268	        for h in hosts:
269	            # Proper suffix match: host == h OR host ends with ".h".
270	            if host == h or host.endswith("." + h):
271	                return code
272	    return None

277	# SURPASS-3 Ludvik, SURMOUNT-1 Jastreboff as first-class biblio
278	# entries even though M-35 anchor queries surfaced them into the
279	# corpus. The tier-balanced selector gave T1 proportional slots
280	# allocated by relevance, letting meta-analyses and review papers
281	# outscore primary trials on lexical relevance.

283	# M-42e adds a T1 named-trial-primary floor: for each anchor trial
284	# configured in `per_query_primary_trial_anchors`, if the T1 pool
285	# contains a matching primary paper (title regex), reserve 1 slot
286	# before filling T1 by relevance. Capped at 6 (prevents displacing
287	# T2 meta-analysis allocation below V25 baseline).

291	# Diabetes Care, Nat Med). Used alongside title regex to detect
292	# primary-trial rows in T1 pool.
293	_M42E_PRIMARY_DOI_PREFIXES = (

310	    "post hoc",
311	    "post-hoc",
312	    "posthoc",
313	    "subgroup analysis",

351	# mechanism-section selector floor. Token patterns mirror the M-40
352	# outline trigger vocabulary. A row is "mechanism-flagged" when any
353	# of these appear in title OR statement OR direct_quote.
354	_M42C_MECHANISM_TOKENS = (
355	    "mechanism",
356	    "pharmacokinetic",
357	    "pharmacodynamic",
358	    "receptor",
359	    "half-life",
360	    "half life",
361	    "bioavailability",
362	    "metabolism",
363	    "agonist",
364	    "antagonist",
365	    "binding",
366	    "signaling",
367	    "signalling",  # British spelling
368	    "pathway",

371	    "isotope",     # tracer / isotope labeling
372	    "affinity",    # receptor affinity
373	    "biomarker",
374	    "glucagon",    # incretin mechanism
