379	
380	def _row_title_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
381	    """M-48 pass-2 (2026-04-22, Codex blocker fix): shared accessor for
382	    a row's title/title-like text.
383	
384	    Live evidence rows from `run_live_retrieval` populate `statement`
385	    with `cand.title[:300]` — NOT `title`. Pre-pass-2 accessors only
386	    read `row["title"]`, silently returning `""` for every live row.
387	    This broke M-42e primary detection AND M-48 population-scope
388	    labeling AND the preflight coverage check on real sweep data
389	    (tests passed only because fixtures used `title`).
390	
391	    Precedence: explicit `title` > `statement` (live-retriever form) >
392	    `source_title` (alternative schema seen in some retrievers) > "".
393	    Returns a plain string (never None)."""
394	    for key in ("title", "statement", "source_title"):
395	        v = row.get(key)
396	        if isinstance(v, str) and v:
397	            return v
398	    return ""
399	
400

400	
401	def _m42c_row_is_mechanism_rich(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
402	    """True if the row contains mechanism-of-action vocabulary in
403	    title, statement, or direct_quote. Case-insensitive substring
404	    match against `_M42C_MECHANISM_TOKENS`."""
405	    fields = [
406	        _row_title_text(row),
407	        str(row.get("statement") or ""),
408	        str(row.get("direct_quote") or ""),
409	    ]
410	    combined = " ".join(fields).lower()
411	    return any(tok in combined for tok in _M42C_MECHANISM_TOKENS)
412	
413	
414	# M-42c floor: number of T1/T2 slots to reserve for mechanism rows
415	# when the corpus contains enough mechanism-flagged content. The
416	# floor fires only when the pool has >=4 mechanism rows (matches the
417	# M-40 outline-trigger threshold). Slots are taken from T1 first,
418	# then T2; the floor does NOT expand those tier quotas.
419	_M42C_MECHANISM_FLOOR_MIN_POOL_ROWS = 4
420	_M42C_MECHANISM_FLOOR_SLOTS = 3  # reserve 3 slots across T1+T2
421	
422	
423	def _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(
424	    row: dict[str, Any],
425	    anchor: str,
426	) -> bool:
427	    """True if `row` appears to be the primary publication for the
428	    named-trial `anchor` (e.g. 'SURPASS-2', 'SURMOUNT-1').
429	
430	    Detection (M-42e pass-2):
431	      1. Anchor appears in row title (required).
432	      2. Row URL/DOI matches a known primary-publication prefix OR
433	         sits on a primary-publication host.
434	      3. Row title does NOT match any non-primary-analysis pattern
435	         (post hoc, subgroup, secondary, exploratory, pooled,
436	         meta-analysis, substudy, etc.). Even on a primary host, a
437	         title declaring itself as a post hoc or subgroup analysis
438	         is NOT the primary publication; usually it's a follow-up
439	         analysis published in the same or a companion journal.
440	
441	    All three conditions required to prevent tagging analyses as
442	    primaries.
443	    """
444	    if not anchor:
445	        return False
446	    # M-48 pass-2 (Codex blocker fix): live retriever populates
447	    # `statement` not `title`. Use shared accessor.
448	    title = _row_title_text(row).lower()
449	    url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").lower()
450	    anchor_l = anchor.lower()
451	    # (1) Title must contain the anchor (possibly with colon or
452	    # parenthesis separator).
453	    if anchor_l not in title:
454	        return False
455	    # (3) Title must NOT contain a non-primary-analysis marker.
456	    for pat in _M42E_NON_PRIMARY_TITLE_PATTERNS:
457	        if pat in title:
458	            return False
459	    # (2) URL must look like a primary publication — DOI prefix OR
460	    # host suggesting peer-reviewed journal.
461	    if any(p in url for p in _M42E_PRIMARY_DOI_PREFIXES):
462	        return True
463	    primary_hosts = (
464	        "nejm.org", "thelancet.com", "jamanetwork.com",
465	        "nature.com", "diabetesjournals.org",
466	    )
467	    for h in primary_hosts:
468	        if h in url:
469	            return True
470	    return False
471	
472	
473	def _row_relevance(
474	    row: dict[str, Any],
475	    question_tokens: set[str],
476	    protocol_tokens: set[str],
477	) -> float:
478	    """Deterministic lexical relevance score in [0, 1].
479	
480	    Overlap between the union of question+protocol tokens and the
481	    evidence's statement+direct_quote content tokens. Jaccard-ish but
482	    normalized by the question side so longer evidence doesn't dominate.
483	    """
484	    text = " ".join(str(row.get(k, "") or "") for k in ("statement", "direct_quote"))
485	    ev_toks = _content_tokens(text)
486	    anchors = question_tokens | protocol_tokens
487	    if not anchors:
488	        return 0.0
489	    overlap = ev_toks & anchors
490	    return len(overlap) / max(1, len(anchors))
491	
492	
493	# ── I-perm-011 (#1205): max-over-subqueries relevance (default OFF) ──────────
494	# `_row_relevance` normalizes overlap by the WHOLE question+protocol token set.
495	# That denominator scales with question LENGTH: a ~73-content-token multi-part
496	# research question makes the 0.30 floor demand >=22 exact-word matches, so an
497	# excellent on-topic top-tier paper whose domain vocabulary (Fusobacterium,
498	# butyrate, tumorigenesis) doesn't lexically overlap the question's exact words
499	# (predominant, mitigate, retard) is dropped (drb_76: 597->53; 74 on-topic T1
500	# shed). The run already decomposes the question into focused sub-queries
501	# (q1d + STORM). Scoring each row against the BEST-MATCHING sub-query (each with
502	# a SMALL denominator) lets a row matching ONE facet clear the floor instead of
503	# being diluted by the other ~60 paragraph tokens.
504	#
505	# This is MONOTONIC-UP: it returns max(whole_question_score, best_subquery_score)
506	# so a row NEVER scores lower than today. Flag-ON therefore keeps a SUPERSET of
507	# flag-OFF rows — it can only OPEN the throttle, never tighten it.
508	#
509	# Flag `PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR` (default OFF). OFF, or no sub-query token sets,
510	# => the score equals `_row_relevance` exactly (byte-identical selection).
511	
512	
513	def _subquery_floor_enabled() -> bool:
514	    """Kill-switch `PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR`. Returns True under the redesign
515	    default (I-arch-011 B16) OR the semantic scorer OR an explicit ON value.
516	
517	    B1 (b1b10 redesign): the semantic scorer (`PG_RELEVANCE_SCORER=semantic_v2`)
518	    implies the sub-query floor for the LEXICAL FALLBACK path — it is MONOTONIC-UP
519	    (max over per-sub-query scores, each with a small denominator) so it can only
520	    OPEN the floor, never tighten it, mitigating the long-question denominator if
521	    the embedder is unavailable and selection degrades to the lexical scorer.
522	
523	    I-arch-011 (B16, §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER): the whole-question denominator in
524	    `_row_relevance` (len(overlap)/len(question+protocol tokens)) makes the floor
525	    TIGHTER as the question gets longer — a 3-part research question demands ~22
526	    exact-word matches per source, so the lexical `selection_relevance` stamp (used
527	    as the ORDERING input downstream and as the legacy-path filter) buries on-topic
528	    sources that match ONE facet. Under the credibility redesign (the §-1.3 default,
529	    `PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN` unset/on) the per-sub-query MAX is the correct
530	    relevance normalization: it is MONOTONIC-UP (`_row_relevance_facet` returns
531	    `max(whole_question, best_subquery)`, clamped to 1.0) so a row's score can only
532	    RISE — the floor only ever OPENS, NEVER tightens. Tying it to the redesign flag
533	    (rather than flipping the bare default) keeps it consistent with how this gate
534	    already honors `_semantic_scorer_enabled()`, and the lift stays CONFINED to the
535	    relevance-floor path (the caller passes empty sub-query sets on the tier-balanced
536	    TRUNCATING path, where a score lift could reorder/displace a kept row).
537	
538	    Explicit `PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR=0` (off/false/no) forces it OFF even under the
539	    redesign — the legacy whole-question denominator — for audit/reversal."""
540	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR", "").strip().lower()
541	    if raw in ("0", "false", "no", "off"):
542	        return False  # explicit OFF wins (audit/reversal)
543	    if raw != "":
544	        # Any explicit non-OFF value => ON (the prior "not in off-set => True" rule).
545	        return True
546	    # Unset: honor the semantic scorer OR the credibility-redesign default (§-1.3 B16).
547	    return _semantic_scorer_enabled() or _credibility_redesign_enabled()
548	
549	
550	def _subquery_token_sets(sub_queries: list[str] | None) -> list[set[str]]:
551	    """Per-sub-query content-token sets, dropping empties. Returns [] when the
552	    feature is disabled OR no usable sub-queries are supplied — the empty list
553	    makes `_row_relevance_facet` fall back to the whole-question score."""
554	    if not sub_queries or not _subquery_floor_enabled():
555	        return []
556	    sets: list[set[str]] = []
557	    for sq in sub_queries:
558	        toks = _content_tokens(str(sq or ""))
559	        if toks:
560	            sets.append(toks)
561	    return sets
562	
563	
564	def _row_relevance_facet(
565	    row: dict[str, Any],
566	    question_tokens: set[str],
567	    protocol_tokens: set[str],
568	    subquery_token_sets: list[set[str]],
569	) -> float:
570	    """Relevance score that takes the MAX of the whole-question score and the
571	    best per-sub-query score. `subquery_token_sets` empty => identical to
572	    `_row_relevance` (the only caller-visible difference is the max-up lift when
573	    the flag is on AND sub-queries are present). Result clamped to [0, 1]."""
574	    base = _row_relevance(row, question_tokens, protocol_tokens)
575	    if not subquery_token_sets:
576	        return base
577	    text = " ".join(
578	        str(row.get(k, "") or "") for k in ("statement", "direct_quote")
579	    )
580	    ev_toks = _content_tokens(text)
581	    best = base
582	    for subq_toks in subquery_token_sets:
583	        denom = len(subq_toks)
584	        if denom <= 0:
585	            continue
586	        score = len(ev_toks & subq_toks) / denom
587	        if score > best:
588	            best = score
589	    return min(1.0, best)
590	
591	
592	# ── B1 (b1b10 redesign, 2026-06-14): SEMANTIC relevance scorer ───────────────
593	# THE BUG (claude_plan.md B1 / DUAL_AGREED_PLAN.md): `_row_relevance` =
594	# len(overlap)/max(1,len(anchors)) — lexical word-overlap divided by QUESTION
595	# LENGTH. The denominator scales with question length, so a ~73-token multi-part
596	# research question makes a 0.30 floor demand >=22 exact-word matches; an on-topic
597	# T1 paper whose domain vocab (Fusobacterium, butyrate) doesn't lexically match
598	# the question's words (predominant, mitigate) is dropped (the live 236/589 loss;
599	# the code comment at `_row_relevance` documents the same drb_76 collapse). No
600	# single float is safe on that scorer — the SCORER is wrong.
601	#
602	# THE FIX: embedding-cosine relevance (synonym/paraphrase aware) reusing the
603	# ALREADY-LOADED embedder from `prefetch_offtopic_filter` (no new model cost).
604	# Relevance stays a FILTER (topical, orthogonal axis); credibility/authority/
605	# retrieval_weight stay the WEIGHT in the sort. This is the relevance HALF of
606	# §-1.3 (the one axis "weight don't filter" does NOT govern — off-topic is
607	# useless at any weight); the faithfulness engine is untouched.
608	#
609	# GATING: `PG_RELEVANCE_SCORER` (default "lexical"). Only "semantic_v2" activates
610	# the embedding scorer AND the restored relevance filter. Unset / any other value
611	# => byte-identical lexical behavior (the `_row_relevance_facet` path).
612	
613	
614	def _relevance_scorer_mode() -> str:
615	    """Read `PG_RELEVANCE_SCORER` (default "lexical"). "semantic_v2" => the
616	    embedding-cosine scorer + restored relevance filter; anything else (incl.
617	    unset) => the legacy lexical `_row_relevance_facet` scorer, byte-identical."""
618	    return os.environ.get("PG_RELEVANCE_SCORER", "lexical").strip().lower()
619	
620	
621	def _semantic_scorer_enabled() -> bool:
622	    """True iff `PG_RELEVANCE_SCORER == semantic_v2`. Default OFF => byte-identical
623	    lexical selection (no embedder load, no filter change)."""
624	    return _relevance_scorer_mode() == "semantic_v2"
625	
626	
627	def _relevance_drop_ledger_enabled() -> bool:
628	    """Kill-switch `PG_RELEVANCE_DROP_LEDGER` (default ON under semantic_v2). When
629	    ON, every below-floor relevance drop is logged with its cosine score + url so
630	    the operator can audit the filter. Telemetry-only — never changes the keep
631	    set. Default-ON is harmless when the semantic scorer is OFF (no drops are made
632	    by the semantic filter on the legacy path)."""
633	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_RELEVANCE_DROP_LEDGER", "1").strip().lower()
634	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
635	
636	
637	# Module-level cache: `prefetch_offtopic_filter._load_embedder()` constructs a
638	# fresh embedder per call (it does NOT use the singleton), so cache the handle here
639	# to reuse the already-resident weights across rows / sections.
640	#
641	# I-deepfix-001 P0-2 RETRYABLE CACHE (Codex iter-1): the prior sentinel cached
642	# `False` ("tried, unavailable") on ANY first failure — which permanently DARKENED
643	# the embedder for the whole run when the very first load hit a TRANSIENT failure
644	# (e.g. a GPU OOM / race while the heavy models co-resident). That is the deepfix
645	# darkness this fix prevents. Now we distinguish:
646	#   * STRUCTURAL failure (the import of `_load_embedder` itself raises — the module
647	#     / symbol does not exist): cache `False` — retrying cannot help.
648	#   * TRANSIENT failure (`_load_embedder()` returns None — it swallows its own
649	#     load exception, which may be a recoverable OOM/race): do NOT cache `False`;
650	#     return None so the NEXT section retries the load (one bad load doesn't dark
651	#     the whole run).
652	# The transient retry is BOUNDED (§8.4 resource discipline): `_load_embedder()`
653	# reads a ~16GB model from disk before it can OOM, so an unbounded per-section retry
654	# of a genuinely-persistent failure would thrash CPU/RAM every section. After
655	# `PG_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_MAX_LOAD_RETRIES` consecutive transient None returns we
656	# cache `False` and stop retrying. Sentinels are read with explicit `is None` /
657	# `is False` identity (never truthiness) so a real handle is never misread.
658	_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE: Any = None
659	_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_TRANSIENT_FAILS: int = 0
660	
661	# I-deepfix-001 P1-1 (#1344): W7 selection-reranker (Qwen3-Reranker-4B) STRUCTURAL
662	# load-failure signal, read by `winner_firing_gate` so W7 is actually hard-gated.
663	# Tri-state, with the SAME semantics as the embedder cache sentinel:
664	#   * None  => no W7 rerank attempted yet (load state not determined) — NOT dark.
665	#   * True  => the LAZY IMPORT / MODEL LOAD raised structurally on a real attempt
666	#              (the wiring is wrong) — dark. Persists across queries in a sweep so
667	#              query N's structural load failure is read by query N+1's gate (the
668	#              gate fires BEFORE selection on each query, so same-query W7 is only
669	#              caught when an earlier selection — e.g. a finding-dedup/CRAG pass —
670	#              already attempted the load).
671	#   * False => the import+load SUCCEEDED on a real attempt (the model fired). A
672	#              transient SCORING/permutation failure does NOT flip this to True
673	#              (the load itself worked; the winner DID load) — only an import/load
674	#              failure marks structural-dark, never a single transient encode
675	#              exception (winner_firing_gate docstring).
676	_W7_RERANKER_LOAD_FAILED: "bool | None" = None
677	
678	
679	def _semantic_embedder_max_load_retries() -> int:
680	    """Bound on consecutive TRANSIENT embedder-load failures before we give up and
681	    cache `False` (§8.4 — avoid re-reading the ~16GB model every section on a
682	    genuinely-persistent failure). Env `PG_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_MAX_LOAD_RETRIES`
683	    (default 3). Clamped to >= 1 so at least one retry is always allowed."""
684	    try:
685	        raw = int(os.environ.get("PG_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_MAX_LOAD_RETRIES", "3"))
686	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
687	        raw = 3
688	    return max(1, raw)
689	
690	
691	def _get_semantic_embedder() -> Any:
692	    """Return the cached embedder (reusing `prefetch_offtopic_filter`'s loader so
693	    we share the same weights / model cost). Returns the embedder, or None if it
694	    could not be loaded — the caller MUST handle None with a LOUD fallback to the
695	    lexical scorer (LAW II: no silent degrade).
696	
697	    Retryable per I-deepfix-001 P0-2: a STRUCTURAL import failure caches `False`
698	    (never retried); a TRANSIENT `_load_embedder()` None return is retried on the
699	    next call up to `PG_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_MAX_LOAD_RETRIES`, after which it caches
700	    `False` to stop thrashing the heavy load."""
701	    global _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE, _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_TRANSIENT_FAILS
702	    # Already resolved (a real handle OR a structural-False) — return it as-is.
703	    if _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE is not None:
704	        return None if _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE is False else _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE
705	    try:
706	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.prefetch_offtopic_filter import (
707	            _load_embedder,
708	        )
709	    except Exception as exc:  # STRUCTURAL: import-path failure — retrying cannot help.
710	        _LOGGER.warning(
711	            "[select] semantic relevance: embedder import failed (%s) — caching "
712	            "unavailable (STRUCTURAL, no retry); the caller will fall back LOUDLY "
713	            "to the lexical scorer.",
714	            str(exc)[:200],
715	        )
716	        _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE = False
717	        return None
718	    loaded = _load_embedder()
719	    if loaded is not None:
720	        _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE = loaded
721	        _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_TRANSIENT_FAILS = 0
722	        return loaded
723	    # TRANSIENT: _load_embedder() returned None (it swallowed its own load
724	    # exception). Retry on the next call until the bound, then cache False.
725	    _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_TRANSIENT_FAILS += 1
726	    max_retries = _semantic_embedder_max_load_retries()
727	    if _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_TRANSIENT_FAILS >= max_retries:
728	        _LOGGER.warning(
729	            "[select] semantic relevance: embedder load returned None %d times "
730	            "(>= PG_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_MAX_LOAD_RETRIES=%d) — caching unavailable "
731	            "to stop thrashing the heavy load; the caller falls back LOUDLY to the "
732	            "lexical scorer.",
733	            _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_TRANSIENT_FAILS, max_retries,
734	        )
735	        _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE = False
736	    else:
737	        _LOGGER.warning(
738	            "[select] semantic relevance: embedder load returned None "
739	            "(transient attempt %d of %d) — will RETRY on the next section; the "
740	            "caller falls back LOUDLY to the lexical scorer for now.",
741	            _SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_TRANSIENT_FAILS, max_retries,
742	        )
743	    return None
744	
745	
746	def _row_embed_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
747	    """The text embedded for a row's relevance — statement + direct_quote, the
748	    SAME fields the lexical scorer reads (`_row_relevance`), so the two scorers
749	    rank the same content surface."""
750	    return " ".join(
751	        str(row.get(k, "") or "") for k in ("statement", "direct_quote")
752	    )
753	
754	
755	def _semantic_relevance_scores(
756	    research_question: str,
757	    sub_queries: list[str] | None,
758	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
759	) -> dict[int, float] | None:
760	    """Batch embedding-cosine relevance for every row.
761	
762	    Returns ``{row_index -> max cosine over {research_question} ∪ {sub_queries}}``
763	    clamped to [0, 1], or ``None`` if the embedder is unavailable / scoring fails
764	    (the caller then falls back LOUDLY to the lexical scorer — never a silent
765	    keep-all, LAW II).
766	
767	    The MAX over the question AND each sub-query is the cosine-space analogue of
768	    `_row_relevance_facet`'s max-over-subqueries: a row matching ONE focused facet
769	    clears the floor instead of being diluted. Because the score is cosine (not a
770	    fraction over the whole-question token set), the long-question denominator that
771	    buried on-topic papers is gone at the root — but we still take the per-subquery
772	    max so a multi-facet question routes each row to its best-matching facet. All
773	    anchors are embedded in the SAME cosine space (no scale-mixing — the silent bug
774	    `_row_relevance_facet` would have if base became cosine while subquery stayed
775	    lexical).
776	    """
777	    embedder = _get_semantic_embedder()
778	    if embedder is None:
779	        return None
780	    anchors: list[str] = []
781	    q = (research_question or "").strip()
782	    if q:
783	        anchors.append(q)
784	    for sq in sub_queries or []:
785	        s = str(sq or "").strip()
786	        if s:
787	            anchors.append(s)
788	    if not anchors:
789	        # No usable anchor text — cannot score semantically; fall back loudly.
790	        _LOGGER.warning(
791	            "[select] semantic relevance: empty research_question AND no "
792	            "sub-queries — falling back to the lexical scorer."
793	        )
794	        return None
795	    row_texts = [_row_embed_text(row) for row in evidence_rows]
796	    try:
797	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.prefetch_offtopic_filter import (
798	            _similarity_scores,
799	        )

800	        # One embed pass per anchor over ALL row texts (batched inside
801	        # `_similarity_scores`); take the per-row MAX across anchors. Rows are
802	        # embedded once per anchor (anchors are few: 1 question + a handful of
803	        # sub-queries), NEVER once per row.
804	        per_anchor_raw = [
805	            _similarity_scores(embedder, anchor, row_texts) for anchor in anchors
806	        ]
807	    except Exception as exc:
808	        _LOGGER.warning(
809	            "[select] semantic relevance scoring failed (%s) — falling back to "
810	            "the lexical scorer.",
811	            str(exc)[:200],
812	        )
813	        return None
814	    # I-deepfix-001 B1 (Codex wave-1 P0): `_similarity_scores` now returns None on a
815	    # scorer/infra failure (no embedder interface / zero-norm query / encode raise),
816	    # NOT all-zeros. Drop the failed anchors; if EVERY anchor failed the scorer is
817	    # unavailable -> return None so B1/B4 FALL BACK LOUDLY to the lexical scorer
818	    # (keep candidates) instead of mass-dropping the corpus on an embedder error.
819	    per_anchor = [s for s in per_anchor_raw if s is not None]
820	    if not per_anchor:
821	        _LOGGER.warning(
822	            "[select] semantic relevance: scorer returned no usable scores for any "
823	            "anchor (infra failure) — falling back to the lexical scorer."
824	        )
825	        return None
826	    scores: dict[int, float] = {}
827	    for i in range(len(evidence_rows)):
828	        best = 0.0
829	        for sims in per_anchor:
830	            if i < len(sims):
831	                v = sims[i]
832	                if v > best:
833	                    best = v
834	        # Clamp to [0, 1]: cosine can be negative (off-topic); a negative score is
835	        # floored to 0.0 so it reads as "no relevance" and a row with no embeddable
836	        # text (empty statement+quote) scores 0.0 rather than being laundered up.
837	        scores[i] = min(1.0, max(0.0, float(best)))
838	    return scores
839	
840	
841	# ── #955 (S2, 2026-05-30): within-tier-band recency tiebreaker ──────────────
842	# Semantic Scholar `year` is fetched (live_retriever.py) and lands on the row
843	# (row["year"] or row["metadata"]["year"]) but the selector never used it, so a
844	# 2014 review and a 2025 pivotal RCT in the same tier competed only on
845	# tier + lexical relevance. This adds recency as a SOFT tiebreaker: relevance
846	# stays primary (banded into widths of epsilon); within the SAME tier AND SAME
847	# relevance band, a higher year sorts first. It NEVER crosses tiers, floor
848	# priority classes, or higher relevance bands (band is monotonic in score), and
849	# it NEVER hard-drops a row (only reorders within a band).
850	#
851	# Codex brief-gate APPROVE (#955) P2 note, documented here as required: because
852	# same-tier/same-band rows are treated as near-ties, recency CAN change WHICH
853	# same-band row fills a tier quota slot — a same-band OLDER row may lose its
854	# slot to a same-band NEWER one. It can never cost a MORE-relevant (higher-band)
855	# row its slot. That is the intended "soft floor, never hard-drop the more
856	# relevant" semantics. Default epsilon 0.05 per Codex ruling (0.0 = exact-tie
857	# only, too weak for the 2014-vs-2025 near-tie case).
858	_RECENCY_MIN_YEAR = 1900
859	_RECENCY_MAX_YEAR = 2100
860	_RECENCY_EPSILON_DEFAULT = 0.05
861	
862	
863	# ── I-deepfix-001 B10(d) (#1352): user date-window enforcement at selection ────
864	# A user date constraint ("academic research published before June 2023") must
865	# (1) DEMOTE out-of-window sources as a DISCLOSED soft floor — never hard-drop
866	# per §-1.3 (the source stays in the corpus + an exclusion record is written),
867	# and (2) INVERT/DISABLE the recency tiebreak when a max-date is present, because
868	# "newer first" directly FIGHTS a max-date ceiling (the B10 smoking gun). All
869	# fail-OPEN: an undated row (no resolvable publication year) is NEVER demoted.
870	_ENV_DATE_DEMOTE_WEIGHT = "PG_SELECT_DATE_WINDOW_WEIGHT"
871	_DEFAULT_DATE_DEMOTE_WEIGHT = 0.25
872	
873	
874	def _date_demote_weight() -> float:
875	    """The ranking multiplier applied to an out-of-window source (LAW VI). Clamp
876	    to (0, 1) — a demoted source is KEPT at low weight, never zero/dropped."""
877	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_DATE_DEMOTE_WEIGHT, "").strip()
878	    if not raw:
879	        return _DEFAULT_DATE_DEMOTE_WEIGHT
880	    try:
881	        val = float(raw)
882	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
883	        return _DEFAULT_DATE_DEMOTE_WEIGHT
884	    # (0, 1): never 0 (that is a drop) and never >=1 (that is no demotion).
885	    return min(max(val, 0.01), 0.99)
886	
887	
888	def _protocol_date_window(
889	    protocol: dict[str, Any] | None,
890	) -> tuple[int | None, int | None]:
891	    """Resolve the (start_year, end_year) window from ``protocol['date_range']``.
892	
893	    Accepts the serialized ``{"start": ISO|null, "end": ISO|null}`` shape OR a
894	    (start, end) tuple. Parses the leading 4-digit year from each bound. Returns
895	    (None, None) when no window is set — the caller then leaves recency + weights
896	    byte-identical (fail-open)."""
897	    if not protocol:
898	        return (None, None)
899	    dr = protocol.get("date_range")
900	    start_raw = end_raw = None
901	    if isinstance(dr, dict):
902	        start_raw, end_raw = dr.get("start"), dr.get("end")
903	    elif isinstance(dr, (list, tuple)) and len(dr) == 2:
904	        start_raw, end_raw = dr[0], dr[1]
905	
906	    def _year(v: Any) -> int | None:
907	        if v is None:
908	            return None
909	        m = re.match(r"\s*(\d{4})", str(v))
910	        if not m:
911	            return None
912	        y = int(m.group(1))
913	        return y if _RECENCY_MIN_YEAR <= y <= _RECENCY_MAX_YEAR else None
914	
915	    return (_year(start_raw), _year(end_raw))
916	
917	
918	def _row_out_of_window(
919	    row: dict[str, Any], start_year: int | None, end_year: int | None,
920	) -> bool:
921	    """True iff the row's EXPLICIT publication year falls OUTSIDE [start, end].
922	
923	    FAIL-OPEN: a row with no resolvable year (``_row_year`` is None) is NEVER
924	    out-of-window — an undated source is kept at full weight, never demoted."""
925	    y = _row_year(row)
926	    if y is None:
927	        return False
928	    if start_year is not None and y < start_year:
929	        return True
930	    if end_year is not None and y > end_year:
931	        return True
932	    return False
933	
934	
935	# ── I-deepfix-001 Codex wave-2 P1: MONTH-precision date window + guaranteed tail ──
936	# A year-only window let post-June-2023 rows survive a "before June 2023" ceiling,
937	# and the multiplicative demote weight did not guarantee out-of-window rows sort
938	# last (a high-score row could outrank an in-window one, and rerank washed it out).
939	# These helpers add MONTH-precision detection (year-floor guaranteed, month
940	# best-effort) + a stable post-rerank tail partition. Month index is 0-based
941	# (year*12 + month-1) so ``idx // 12 == year`` for year-only comparison.
942	def _ym_window_bounds(
943	    protocol: dict[str, Any] | None,
944	) -> tuple[int | None, int | None]:
945	    """(start_idx, end_idx) month indices for the date window, or (None, None).
946	
947	    Reads protocol['date_range'] ISO bounds 'YYYY', 'YYYY-MM', or 'YYYY-MM-DD'.
948	    A year-only START defaults to month 1, a year-only END ceiling to month 12, so
949	    a whole-year bound behaves exactly as the prior year comparison."""
950	    if not protocol:
951	        return (None, None)
952	    dr = protocol.get("date_range")
953	    start_raw = end_raw = None
954	    if isinstance(dr, dict):
955	        start_raw, end_raw = dr.get("start"), dr.get("end")
956	    elif isinstance(dr, (list, tuple)) and len(dr) == 2:
957	        start_raw, end_raw = dr[0], dr[1]
958	
959	    def _idx(v: Any, default_month: int) -> int | None:
960	        if v is None:
961	            return None
962	        m = re.match(r"\s*(\d{4})(?:-(\d{1,2}))?", str(v))
963	        if not m:
964	            return None
965	        y = int(m.group(1))
966	        if not (_RECENCY_MIN_YEAR <= y <= _RECENCY_MAX_YEAR):
967	            return None
968	        mo = int(m.group(2)) if m.group(2) else default_month
969	        mo = min(12, max(1, mo))
970	        return y * 12 + (mo - 1)
971	
972	    return (_idx(start_raw, 1), _idx(end_raw, 12))
973	
974	
975	def _row_pub_ym(row: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[int, int | None] | None:
976	    """(year, month|None) from the row's pub_date/publication_date ISO, else
977	    (year, None) from the row's year, else None. No network."""
978	    for key in ("pub_date", "publication_date"):
979	        val = row.get(key)
980	        if val is None:
981	            meta = row.get("metadata")
982	            if isinstance(meta, dict):
983	                val = meta.get(key)
984	        if val:
985	            m = re.match(r"\s*(\d{4})-(\d{1,2})", str(val))
986	            if m:
987	                y, mo = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
988	                if _RECENCY_MIN_YEAR <= y <= _RECENCY_MAX_YEAR and 1 <= mo <= 12:
989	                    return (y, mo)
990	            m2 = re.match(r"\s*(\d{4})", str(val))
991	            if m2:
992	                y = int(m2.group(1))
993	                if _RECENCY_MIN_YEAR <= y <= _RECENCY_MAX_YEAR:
994	                    return (y, None)
995	    y = _row_year(row)
996	    return (y, None) if y is not None else None
997	
998	
999	def _row_out_of_window_ym(
1000	    row: dict[str, Any], start_idx: int | None, end_idx: int | None,
1001	) -> bool:
1002	    """Month-precision out-of-window test. FAIL-OPEN: an undated row is NEVER
1003	    out-of-window; a row known only to the YEAR is out only when its year is
1004	    strictly past the boundary year (a boundary-year row whose month is unknown
1005	    is KEPT — never demote what we cannot prove out-of-window)."""
1006	    pub = _row_pub_ym(row)
1007	    if pub is None:
1008	        return False
1009	    y, mo = pub
1010	    if mo is not None:
1011	        idx = y * 12 + (mo - 1)
1012	        if start_idx is not None and idx < start_idx:
1013	            return True
1014	        if end_idx is not None and idx > end_idx:
1015	            return True
1016	        return False
1017	    if start_idx is not None and y < (start_idx // 12):
1018	        return True
1019	    if end_idx is not None and y > (end_idx // 12):
1020	        return True
1021	    return False
1022

1024	def _partition_out_of_window_last(
1025	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], oow_urls: set[str],
1026	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
1027	    """Stable partition: in-window rows first (order preserved), out-of-window rows
1028	    moved to the TAIL (order preserved). KEEPS every row (§-1.3 — demote, never
1029	    drop). Returns the SAME object when there is nothing out-of-window (byte-
1030	    identical no-window path). This is the GUARANTEE that out-of-window sorts last
1031	    regardless of score or a downstream rerank (Codex wave-2 P1)."""
1032	    if not oow_urls:
1033	        return rows
1034	
1035	    def _u(r: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
1036	        return str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "")
1037	
1038	    out_w = [r for r in rows if _u(r) in oow_urls]
1039	    if not out_w:
1040	        return rows
1041	    in_w = [r for r in rows if _u(r) not in oow_urls]
1042	    return in_w + out_w
1043	
1044	
1045	def _partition_out_of_scope_last(
1046	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], out_urls: set[str],
1047	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
1048	    """I-scope-001 generalization of ``_partition_out_of_window_last`` to the UNION of
1049	    out-of-window (timeline) + out-of-scope (source-type/jurisdiction) urls. Same stable-
1050	    partition, keep-all contract; returns the SAME object when ``out_urls`` is empty
1051	    (byte-identical no-constraint path)."""
1052	    return _partition_out_of_window_last(rows, out_urls)

1074	def _row_year(row: dict[str, Any]) -> int | None:
1075	    """Publication year from `row['year']` or `row['metadata']['year']`.
1076	    Returns None if absent / non-numeric / outside [1900, 2100]. No network."""
1077	    val = row.get("year")
1078	    if val is None:
1079	        meta = row.get("metadata")
1080	        if isinstance(meta, dict):
1081	            val = meta.get("year")
1082	    if val is None:
1083	        return None
1084	    try:
1085	        year = int(val)
1086	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1087	        return None
1088	    if year < _RECENCY_MIN_YEAR or year > _RECENCY_MAX_YEAR:
1089	        return None
1090	    return year

1093	def _relevance_band(score: float, epsilon: float) -> float:
1094	    """Bucket a relevance score into a band. Higher score → higher band.
1095	    epsilon <= 0 → exact score (only exact ties share a band)."""
1096	    if epsilon <= 0:
1097	        return score
1098	    return math.floor(score / epsilon)

1101	def _year_sort_value(row: dict[str, Any]) -> int:
1102	    """Ascending-sort value for 'newer first': negated year; a missing year
1103	    maps below any real year so undated rows sort LAST within a band (but are
1104	    never excluded)."""
1105	    year = _row_year(row)
1106	    return -(year if year is not None else _RECENCY_MIN_YEAR - 1)

1109	def _relevance_recency_key(
1110	    item: tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]],
1111	    enabled: bool,
1112	    epsilon: float,
1113	) -> tuple:
1114	    """Sort fragment ranking by relevance then (soft) recency, or by raw
1115	    relevance when disabled. Callers append the original index for full
1116	    determinism. Disabled → `(-score,)`, byte-identical to the prior key."""
1117	    _idx, score, _tier, row = item
1118	    if not enabled:
1119	        return (-score,)
1120	    # Within a relevance band, newer year first; then EXACT score as the
1121	    # sub-tiebreaker. Because the band is monotonic in score and exact score
1122	    # breaks within-band ties, an all-undated corpus reproduces the prior
1123	    # pure-(-score) order EXACTLY — recency only reorders DATED same-band rows.
1124	    return (-_relevance_band(score, epsilon), _year_sort_value(row), -score)

1127	def _recency_telemetry_note(
1128	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1129	    epsilon: float,
1130	) -> str:
1131	    """Note emitted ONLY when the recency tiebreaker is enabled."""
1132	    dated = sum(1 for s in scored if _row_year(s[3]) is not None)
1133	    return (
1134	        f"recency_tiebreak enabled epsilon={epsilon} "
1135	        f"dated={dated}/{len(scored)}"
1136	    )

1139	# ── #956 (S2, 2026-05-30): source-diversity passes ──────────────────────────
1140	# Tier quota != topical diversity: 20 T1 RCTs all on ONE sub-topic satisfy the
1141	# T1 quota while starving the other sub-topics, tanking per-sub-topic coverage.
1142	# Two SOFT post-selection passes operate ONLY on post-floor slack and ALWAYS
1143	# preserve tier minimums (Codex brief-gate P2). Because the selected set sits
1144	# exactly at the tier quotas, every quota-preserving swap is SAME-TIER: a
1145	# diversity swap replaces a same-tier NON-reserved relevance-fill row with a
1146	# same-tier pool row, so no tier ever drops below quota and no floor-reserved
1147	# (M-42e/M-51/M-42c/M-42d) or sub-query-reserved row is ever evicted. Both
1148	# passes run on the truncating main path only (short-pool keeps every row, so
1149	# diversity is already maximal there). Codex rulings: k=1, cap_frac=0.5,
1150	# reservation BEFORE domain cap.
1151	_SUBQUERY_K_DEFAULT = 1
1152	_DOMAIN_CAP_FRAC_DEFAULT = 0.5

1179	# ── I-perm-003 (#1197): corpus-size-scaled evidence-selection budget ─────────
1180	# The generator-facing cap is a FIXED `max_rows` (PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN, default
1181	# 20 — #1070/#1078). At 1000-URL retrieval the fixed cap truncates the pool to a
1182	# constant slice regardless of how much best-ranked evidence the corpus actually
1183	# holds. This PREVENTATIVE knob scales the selection budget WITH the pool size so
1184	# a larger corpus feeds proportionally more BEST-ranked rows (the existing
1185	# tier-balanced + relevance/recency truncation already picks best-ranked, never
1186	# first-N — this only raises the budget it operates under).
1187	#
1188	# HONEST SCOPE: on the beatboth8 corpus the selector drops 0 sources (the ~90%
1189	# loss is UPSTREAM extraction, owned by I-perm-007), so this changes nothing
1190	# there. It is a forward guard for when the upstream pool is genuinely large.
1191	#
1192	# DEFAULT OFF. When `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE` is unset/0/false/no/off the helper
1193	# returns `base_max_rows` UNCHANGED and `select_evidence_for_generation` is
1194	# byte-identical to the prior behaviour (no reassignment, no telemetry).
1195	#
1196	# FLOOR semantics: effective = max(base_max_rows, ceil(pool_size * frac)),
1197	# optionally clamped to a ceiling. The `max(...)` guarantees scaling NEVER drops
1198	# the budget below the operator/code cap (a small pool with a low frac keeps the
1199	# existing cap — never a regression).

1200	_SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT = 0.30
1201	# 0 = no ceiling (scale unbounded with the pool). A positive value clamps the
1202	# scaled budget so an enormous pool can't blow past an operator-set ceiling.
1203	_SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT = 0
1204	
1205	
1206	def _selection_scale_enabled() -> bool:
1207	    """Flag `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE` (default OFF). ON only on explicit
1208	    truthy ('1'/'true'/'yes'/'on'). OFF → byte-identical prior selection AND
1209	    no scaling telemetry. Inverse default of `_env_flag_on` ON-by-default."""
1210	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE", "0").strip().lower()
1211	    return raw in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
1212	
1213	
1214	def _selection_scale_frac() -> float:
1215	    """Budget-per-pool-row fraction `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC`
1216	    (default 0.30). Non-positive / unparseable → default (FAIL SOFT to a sane
1217	    positive fraction; a 0 frac would make scaling a no-op via the floor)."""
1218	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC", "").strip()
1219	    if not raw:
1220	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT
1221	    try:
1222	        frac = float(raw)
1223	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1224	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT
1225	    # Reject non-finite (inf/nan): a positive `inf` parses but would OverflowError in
1226	    # `math.ceil(pool_size * frac)` (Codex iter-1 P2). FAIL SOFT to the sane default.
1227	    return frac if (frac > 0 and math.isfinite(frac)) else _SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT
1228	
1229	
1230	def _selection_scale_ceiling() -> int:
1231	    """Optional absolute ceiling `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING`
1232	    (default 0 = no ceiling). Clamps the scaled budget so an enormous pool can't
1233	    overshoot an operator cap. Values <= 0 / unparseable → no ceiling."""
1234	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING", "").strip()
1235	    if not raw:
1236	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT
1237	    try:
1238	        ceiling = int(raw)
1239	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1240	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT
1241	    return ceiling if ceiling > 0 else _SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT
1242

1244	def _scaled_max_rows(pool_size: int, base_max_rows: int) -> tuple[int, str | None]:
1245	    """Corpus-size-scaled selection budget (I-perm-003, default OFF).
1246	
1247	    Returns ``(effective_max_rows, note)``. When the flag is OFF, returns
1248	    ``(base_max_rows, None)`` — the caller MUST treat that as a byte-identical
1249	    no-op (no telemetry note appended). When ON, returns the FLOOR-guarded scaled
1250	    budget ``max(base_max_rows, ceil(pool_size * frac))`` (optionally clamped to a
1251	    ceiling) plus a single telemetry string for the selection notes.
1252	    """
1253	    if not _selection_scale_enabled():
1254	        return base_max_rows, None
1255	    frac = _selection_scale_frac()
1256	    ceiling = _selection_scale_ceiling()
1257	    scaled = math.ceil(pool_size * frac)
1258	    effective = max(base_max_rows, scaled)
1259	    clamped = False
1260	    if ceiling > 0 and effective > ceiling:
1261	        # Never below the base cap even when the ceiling is < base (floor wins).
1262	        effective = max(base_max_rows, ceiling)
1263	        clamped = True
1264	    note = (
1265	        f"selection_scale pool={pool_size} frac={frac} "
1266	        f"base_max_rows={base_max_rows} scaled={scaled} "
1267	        f"effective={effective} ceiling={ceiling or 'none'}"
1268	        f"{' clamped' if clamped else ''}"
1269	    )
1270	    return effective, note

1278	def _row_domain(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
1279	    """Registrable-ish domain (last two host labels) for the per-domain cap."""
1280	    url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").lower()
1281	    if not url:
1282	        return ""
1283	    try:
1284	        from urllib.parse import urlparse
1285	        host = (urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"http://{url}").hostname or "").lower()
1286	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
1287	        return ""
1288	    parts = host.split(".")
1289	    return ".".join(parts[-2:]) if len(parts) >= 2 else host

1292	def _priority_sort_key(
1293	    item: tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]],
1294	    rec_enabled: bool,
1295	    rec_eps: float,
1296	) -> tuple:
1297	    """Within-tier priority for diversity swap decisions — same ordering the
1298	    selector uses (banded relevance + #955 recency, then original index).
1299	    Ascending = best first; max(...) of this key = worst (lowest priority)."""
1300	    return (*_relevance_recency_key(item, rec_enabled, rec_eps), item[0])

1303	def _reserve_subqueries(
1304	    selected: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1305	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1306	    protected_ids: set[int],
1307	    k: int,
1308	    rec_enabled: bool,
1309	    rec_eps: float,
1310	) -> tuple[int, set[int]]:
1311	    """Same-tier swap so each sub-query origin present in a tier's pool reaches
1312	    >= k selected rows in that tier, evicting only NON-protected slack rows whose
1313	    origin is OVER-represented (count > k). Returns (swaps, brought_in_ids)."""
1314	    selected_ids = {id(it) for it in selected}
1315	    pool_by_tier: dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)
1316	    for it in scored:
1317	        if id(it) not in selected_ids:
1318	            pool_by_tier[it[2]].append(it)
1319	    brought_in: set[int] = set()
1320	    swaps = 0
1321	    for tier in {it[2] for it in selected}:
1322	        sel_pos = [i for i, it in enumerate(selected) if it[2] == tier]
1323	        origin_count = Counter(_row_query_origin(selected[i][3]) for i in sel_pos)
1324	        cand_by_origin: dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)
1325	        for it in pool_by_tier.get(tier, []):
1326	            cand_by_origin[_row_query_origin(it[3])].append(it)
1327	        missing = sorted(
1328	            [o for o in cand_by_origin if origin_count.get(o, 0) < k],
1329	            key=lambda o: (-max(c[1] for c in cand_by_origin[o]), o),
1330	        )
1331	        for origin in missing:
1332	            cands = sorted(
1333	                cand_by_origin[origin],
1334	                key=lambda c: _priority_sort_key(c, rec_enabled, rec_eps),
1335	            )
1336	            ci = 0
1337	            while origin_count.get(origin, 0) < k and ci < len(cands):
1338	                evictable = [
1339	                    i for i in sel_pos
1340	                    if id(selected[i]) not in protected_ids
1341	                    and id(selected[i]) not in brought_in
1342	                    and origin_count[_row_query_origin(selected[i][3])] > k
1343	                    and _row_query_origin(selected[i][3]) != origin
1344	                ]
1345	                if not evictable:
1346	                    break
1347	                evict_i = max(
1348	                    evictable,
1349	                    key=lambda i: _priority_sort_key(selected[i], rec_enabled, rec_eps),
1350	                )
1351	                bring = cands[ci]
1352	                ci += 1
1353	                old_origin = _row_query_origin(selected[evict_i][3])
1354	                selected[evict_i] = bring
1355	                brought_in.add(id(bring))
1356	                origin_count[old_origin] -= 1
1357	                origin_count[origin] = origin_count.get(origin, 0) + 1
1358	                swaps += 1
1359	    return swaps, brought_in

1362	# ── I-perm-023 (#1215): diversity-aware selection (constrained-greedy) ────────
1363	# A default-OFF forward guard that diversifies the SELECTED set on the coverage
1364	# axes the existing floor stack does NOT already cover. Entity custody is owned by
1365	# the M-42e/M-51 anchor-primary floors, mechanism by the M-42c floor, the 1-per
1366	# present-jurisdiction reservation by M-41d — so this pass adds SAFETY-CATEGORY +
1367	# EVIDENCE-CLASS coverage (plus jurisdiction diversity BEYOND the M-41d 1-per floor)
1368	# via the SAME post-floor same-tier swap mechanism as the #956 passes. It NEVER
1369	# touches a floor / quota / protected row, so floor parity is guaranteed BY
1370	# CONSTRUCTION. Swaps are COVERAGE-MONOTONE: a swap fires only when the incoming row
1371	# adds a NOVEL bucket AND the evicted row's every bucket stays covered by another
1372	# selected row, so distinct coverage can only INCREASE, never drop an axis.
1373	# Faithfulness (Codex design-gate iter-2): selection only changes the generator's
1374	# candidate menu; the effective generator evidence_pool starts from selected_rows
1375	# (and may add sanctioned prepends + M-52 live pulls), and strict_verify / the
1376	# 4-role evaluator / D8 re-check every sentence against the cited span unchanged —
1377	# so a swap can at worst trade one verifiable row for another, NEVER admit
1378	# unsupported prose. No-op at drb_76 scale (pool<=cap returns via the short-pool
1379	# branch before this region runs).
1380	
1381	# Safety-category taxonomy (FDA/DailyMed SPL labeling sections). Keyword -> category,
1382	# preference-only (a miss costs diversity, never faithfulness). Versioned constant.
1383	_GREEDY_SAFETY_CATEGORIES: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...] = (
1384	    ("boxed_warning", ("boxed warning", "black box warning", "black-box warning")),
1385	    ("contraindication", ("contraindication", "contraindicated")),
1386	    ("warning_precaution", ("warnings and precautions", "warning", "precaution")),
1387	    ("adverse_reaction", ("adverse reaction", "adverse event", "adverse effect",
1388	                          "side effect")),
1389	    ("drug_interaction", ("drug interaction", "drug-drug interaction")),
1390	    ("pregnancy_lactation", ("pregnancy", "lactation", "breastfeeding", "teratogen")),
1391	    ("hepatic_renal", ("hepatotoxicity", "hepatic impairment", "renal impairment",
1392	                       "nephrotox")),
1393	    ("hypoglycemia", ("hypoglycemia", "hypoglycaemia")),
1394	    ("overdose", ("overdose", "overdosage")),

1416	def _greedy_match_category(
1417	    row: dict[str, Any], taxonomy: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...],
1418	) -> str | None:
1419	    """First taxonomy category whose keyword appears in the row's title/statement/
1420	    quote (case-insensitive substring), in taxonomy order. None if no match."""
1421	    combined = " ".join((
1422	        _row_title_text(row),
1423	        str(row.get("statement") or ""),
1424	        str(row.get("direct_quote") or ""),
1425	    )).lower()
1426	    for category, tokens in taxonomy:
1427	        if any(tok in combined for tok in tokens):
1428	            return category
1429	    return None

1432	def _greedy_active_axes() -> tuple[str, ...]:
1433	    """Call-time active axis set (env override PG_GREEDY_AXES, default safety/class/
1434	    jurisdiction). Unknown axis names are ignored; empty/invalid -> the default."""
1435	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_GREEDY_AXES", "").strip()
1436	    if not raw:
1437	        return _GREEDY_AXES_DEFAULT
1438	    axes = tuple(a for a in (x.strip() for x in raw.split(",")) if a in _GREEDY_VALID_AXES)

1442	def _row_coverage_buckets(
1443	    row: dict[str, Any], axes: tuple[str, ...],
1444	) -> frozenset[tuple[str, str]]:
1445	    """The (axis, value) coverage buckets a row belongs to across the ACTIVE axes.
1446	    safety_category / evidence_class are keyword-derived; jurisdiction reuses the
1447	    existing M-41d host predicate. A row with no axis value contributes nothing."""
1448	    out: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
1449	    if "safety" in axes:
1450	        s = _greedy_match_category(row, _GREEDY_SAFETY_CATEGORIES)
1451	        if s:
1452	            out.add(("safety", s))
1453	    if "class" in axes:
1454	        c = _greedy_match_category(row, _GREEDY_EVIDENCE_CLASSES)
1455	        if c:
1456	            out.add(("class", c))
1457	    if "jurisdiction" in axes:
1458	        j = _row_jurisdiction(row)
1459	        if j:
1460	            out.add(("jurisdiction", j))
1461	    return frozenset(out)

1465	    """Call-time config for the I-perm-023 diversity pass. DEFAULT OFF (NOT
1466	    `_env_flag_on`, which defaults ON — Codex design-gate iter-2 P2.3) -> when unset
1467	    the pass never runs and selected/notes/to_dict stay byte-identical. Returns
1468	    (enabled, max_swaps)."""
1469	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SELECT_CONSTRAINED_GREEDY", "0").strip().lower()
1470	    enabled = raw in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
1471	    raw_n = os.environ.get("PG_GREEDY_MAX_SWAPS", "").strip()
1472	    try:
1473	        n = int(raw_n) if raw_n else _GREEDY_MAX_SWAPS_DEFAULT

1479	def _apply_domain_cap(
1480	    selected: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1481	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1482	    protected_ids: set[int],
1483	    cap: int,
1484	    rec_enabled: bool,
1485	    rec_eps: float,
1486	) -> tuple[int, set[int]]:
1487	    """Soft per-domain cap via same-tier swaps. An over-cap domain's NON-protected
1488	    slack rows (worst-priority first) are replaced by the best same-tier pool row
1489	    of an under-cap domain. YIELDS (stops) when no valid same-tier replacement
1490	    exists — never leaves a slot empty, drops a tier below quota, or evicts a
1491	    protected/reserved row. Returns (rows_moved, brought_in_ids) — the brought-in
1492	    ids let the caller protect them from a later pass (Codex design-gate iter-2
1493	    P2.2: the I-perm-023 greedy pass must not undo this domain-diversity pass)."""
1494	    moved = 0
1495	    brought_in: set[int] = set()
1496	    selected_ids = {id(it) for it in selected}
1497	    domain_count = Counter(_row_domain(it[3]) for it in selected)
1498	    over = [d for d, c in domain_count.items() if d and c > cap]
1499	    for dom in over:
1500	        while domain_count[dom] > cap:
1501	            evict_positions = sorted(
1502	                [i for i, it in enumerate(selected)
1503	                 if _row_domain(it[3]) == dom and id(it) not in protected_ids],
1504	                key=lambda i: _priority_sort_key(selected[i], rec_enabled, rec_eps),
1505	                reverse=True,  # worst-priority first
1506	            )
1507	            swapped = False
1508	            for ei in evict_positions:
1509	                tier = selected[ei][2]
1510	                repls = [
1511	                    it for it in scored
1512	                    if id(it) not in selected_ids
1513	                    and it[2] == tier
1514	                    and _row_domain(it[3]) != dom
1515	                    and domain_count.get(_row_domain(it[3]), 0) < cap
1516	                ]
1517	                if not repls:
1518	                    continue
1519	                best = min(repls, key=lambda it: _priority_sort_key(it, rec_enabled, rec_eps))
1520	                old = selected[ei]
1521	                selected[ei] = best
1522	                brought_in.add(id(best))
1523	                selected_ids.discard(id(old))
1524	                selected_ids.add(id(best))
1525	                domain_count[dom] -= 1
1526	                domain_count[_row_domain(best[3])] = domain_count.get(_row_domain(best[3]), 0) + 1
1527	                moved += 1
1528	                swapped = True
1529	                break
1530	            if not swapped:
1531	                break  # yield: no same-tier under-cap replacement available
1532	    return moved, brought_in

1535	def _apply_coverage_diversification(
1536	    selected: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1537	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1538	    protected_ids: set[int],
1539	    max_swaps: int,
1540	    axes: tuple[str, ...],
1541	    rec_enabled: bool,
1542	    rec_eps: float,
1543	    domain_cap: int | None = None,
1544	) -> tuple[int, dict[str, Any]]:
1545	    """I-perm-023 (#1215) constrained-greedy diversity pass. COVERAGE-MONOTONE
1546	    same-tier swaps on post-floor slack: a swap fires only when a non-selected
1547	    same-tier candidate adds a NOVEL coverage bucket AND the evicted (non-protected)
1548	    selected row's every bucket remains covered by another selected row — so the
1549	    pass can only INCREASE distinct coverage, never drop an axis or touch a floor.
1550	    When ``domain_cap`` is set (the active #956 per-domain soft cap) a swap is ALSO
1551	    rejected if it would raise the candidate's source domain ABOVE that cap (Codex
1552	    diff-gate iter-1 P1: the greedy pass must not regress the domain-diversity pass
1553	    by pulling an at-cap domain back over the cap). Deterministic (total-ordered
1554	    tie-breaks); bounded by max_swaps. Returns (swaps, telemetry)."""
1555	    selected_ids = {id(it) for it in selected}
1556	    pool_by_tier: dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)
1557	    for it in scored:
1558	        if id(it) not in selected_ids:
1559	            pool_by_tier[it[2]].append(it)
1560	    # live (axis, value) -> count of selected rows covering it
1561	    bucket_count: Counter = Counter()
1562	    for it in selected:
1563	        for b in _row_coverage_buckets(it[3], axes):
1564	            bucket_count[b] += 1
1565	    # live per-domain count (only consulted when domain_cap is enforced)
1566	    domain_count: Counter = Counter(_row_domain(it[3]) for it in selected)
1567	    brought_in: set[int] = set()
1568	    swaps = 0
1569	    while swaps < max_swaps:
1570	        best = None  # (sort_key, cand, evict_pos, tier, cand_buckets)
1571	        for tier, pool in pool_by_tier.items():
1572	            for cand in pool:
1573	                if id(cand) in brought_in:
1574	                    continue
1575	                cb = _row_coverage_buckets(cand[3], axes)
1576	                novel = sum(1 for b in cb if bucket_count.get(b, 0) == 0)
1577	                if novel == 0:
1578	                    continue
1579	                # same-tier, non-protected, not-brought-in, REDUNDANT evictable:
1580	                # every bucket it holds is covered by >=2 selected rows, so removing
1581	                # it keeps each of those buckets covered (>=1) -> coverage-monotone.
1582	                evictables = [
1583	                    i for i, it in enumerate(selected)
1584	                    if it[2] == tier
1585	                    and id(it) not in protected_ids
1586	                    and id(it) not in brought_in
1587	                    and all(bucket_count.get(b, 0) >= 2
1588	                            for b in _row_coverage_buckets(it[3], axes))
1589	                ]
1590	                # Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: respect the #956 domain cap. Admitting
1591	                # `cand` raises its domain by 1 UNLESS the evicted row is the SAME
1592	                # domain (net-zero). If cand's domain is already at/over the cap, the
1593	                # ONLY domain-feasible swap evicts a same-domain row.
1594	                if domain_cap is not None:
1595	                    cand_dom = _row_domain(cand[3])
1596	                    if domain_count.get(cand_dom, 0) >= domain_cap:
1597	                        evictables = [
1598	                            i for i in evictables
1599	                            if _row_domain(selected[i][3]) == cand_dom

1649	def _m46_short_pool_ordered_selection(
1650	    *,
1651	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
1652	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1653	    full_counts: dict[str, int],
1654	    max_rows: int,
1655	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None,
1656	) -> EvidenceSelection:
1657	    """M-46 (2026-04-22): compute floor detection + deterministic
1658	    priority ordering + telemetry for the short-pool case
1659	    (pool_size <= max_rows).
1660	
1661	    Pre-M-46: the early-exit branch returned evidence_rows as-is with
1662	    a single `pool_size<=max_rows` note and no floor telemetry. When
1663	    floors were configured, the reservations silently did not fire.
1664	
1665	    Post-M-46: all rows are kept (no truncation), but ordering is
1666	    [M-42e primaries → M-42c mechanism → M-42d HC → rest by tier
1667	    priority then -relevance then index]. Notes include the same
1668	    floor-telemetry entries seen on truncating runs so downstream
1669	    audits see consistent signals regardless of pool size.
1670	
1671	    The function is self-contained and does NOT share state with the
1672	    main branch; it re-detects primaries / mechanism / HC using the
1673	    same module-level predicates (`_m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor`,
1674	    `_m42c_row_is_mechanism_rich`, `_row_jurisdiction`).
1675	    """
1676	    notes: list[str] = [
1677	        f"pool_size<=max_rows ({len(scored)}/{max_rows})",
1678	        "m46_short_pool_ordered_selection",
1679	    ]
1680	
1681	    # --- M-42e primary detection ---
1682	    m42e_ids: set[int] = set()
1683	    m42e_anchors: list[str] = []
1684	    if primary_trial_anchors:
1685	        # Prefer T1 primaries; fall back to any-tier if no T1 match
1686	        # (matches M-42e spirit — the primary paper is the priority).
1687	        t1_scored = [s for s in scored if s[2] == "T1"]
1688	        for anchor in primary_trial_anchors:
1689	            if len(m42e_ids) >= _M42E_PRIMARY_FLOOR_CAP:
1690	                break
1691	            matched = False
1692	            for item in t1_scored:
1693	                if id(item) in m42e_ids:
1694	                    continue
1695	                if _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(item[3], anchor):
1696	                    m42e_ids.add(id(item))
1697	                    m42e_anchors.append(anchor)
1698	                    matched = True
1699	                    break
1700	            # No fallback to other tiers — same contract as main branch.
1701	            del matched  # quiet linters
1702	    if m42e_anchors:
1703	        notes.append(
1704	            f"m42e_primary_floor matched={len(m42e_anchors)} "
1705	            f"reserved={len(m42e_ids)} cap={_M42E_PRIMARY_FLOOR_CAP} "
1706	            f"anchors={m42e_anchors[:10]}"
1707	        )
1708	
1709	    # --- M-51 primary custody (short-pool path) ---
1710	    # V29 cycle 1. Short-pool path keeps ALL rows so no truncation
1711	    # trim is needed. But we still want to scan the full `scored`
1712	    # pool for anchor-matched primaries that M-42e (T1-only) may
1713	    # have missed (non-T1 primaries, or rows with tie-breaking
1714	    # mis-rankings). Any match is prioritized to class 0 in
1715	    # `_priority_class` via `m51_extra_ids`.
1716	    m51_extra_ids: set[int] = set()
1717	    m51_sp_matched_anchors: list[str] = []
1718	    if primary_trial_anchors:
1719	        def _m51_canonical_identity_sp(row: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple:
1720	            evid = row.get("evidence_id")
1721	            if isinstance(evid, str) and evid:
1722	                return ("ev", evid)
1723	            url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").lower()
1724	            title = _row_title_text(row).lower()[:200]
1725	            dq = (row.get("direct_quote") or "")[:200]
1726	            return ("key", url, title, dq)
1727	        already_canon = {
1728	            _m51_canonical_identity_sp(item[3]) for item in scored
1729	            if id(item) in m42e_ids
1730	        }
1731	        seen_anchors_sp: set[str] = set()
1732	        for anchor in primary_trial_anchors:
1733	            if anchor in seen_anchors_sp:
1734	                continue
1735	            seen_anchors_sp.add(anchor)
1736	            # Already covered by M-42e for this anchor?
1737	            if anchor in m42e_anchors:
1738	                continue
1739	            # Scan full scored pool (includes non-T1) for this anchor
1740	            for scored_item in scored:
1741	                if id(scored_item) in m42e_ids:
1742	                    continue
1743	                if id(scored_item) in m51_extra_ids:
1744	                    continue
1745	                if _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(scored_item[3], anchor):
1746	                    m51_extra_ids.add(id(scored_item))
1747	                    m51_sp_matched_anchors.append(anchor)
1748	                    break
1749	    if m51_sp_matched_anchors:
1750	        notes.append(
1751	            f"m51_anchor_primary_custody matched="
1752	            f"{len(m51_sp_matched_anchors)} "
1753	            f"inserted={len(m51_sp_matched_anchors)} "
1754	            f"cap={len({a for a in primary_trial_anchors})} "
1755	            f"anchors={m51_sp_matched_anchors[:12]}"
1756	        )
1757	
1758	    # --- M-42c mechanism detection ---
1759	    mech_pool = [s for s in scored if _m42c_row_is_mechanism_rich(s[3])]
1760	    m42c_ids: set[int] = set()
1761	    if len(mech_pool) >= _M42C_MECHANISM_FLOOR_MIN_POOL_ROWS:
1762	        mech_ranked = sorted(
1763	            mech_pool,
1764	            key=lambda s: (_TIER_PRIORITY.get(s[2], 9), -s[1], s[0]),
1765	        )
1766	        slots_left = _M42C_MECHANISM_FLOOR_SLOTS
1767	        for item in mech_ranked:
1768	            if slots_left <= 0:
1769	                break
1770	            if item[2] not in ("T1", "T2"):
1771	                continue
1772	            if id(item) in m42e_ids:
1773	                # Primary-trial slot already reserved — don't double-count.
1774	                continue
1775	            m42c_ids.add(id(item))
1776	            slots_left -= 1
1777	        notes.append(
1778	            f"m42c_mechanism_floor pool_mech_rows={len(mech_pool)} "
1779	            f"reserved={len(m42c_ids)} "
1780	            f"slots={_M42C_MECHANISM_FLOOR_SLOTS}"
1781	        )
1782	
1783	    # --- M-42d HC quota expansion detection ---
1784	    hc_rows = [s for s in scored
1785	               if s[2] == "T3"
1786	               and _row_jurisdiction(s[3]) == _M42D_HC_JURISDICTION_CODE]
1787	    hc_quota = _m42d_hc_quota()
1788	    m42d_hc_ids: set[int] = set()
1789	    m42d_hc_extras = 0
1790	    if hc_rows and hc_quota > 1:
1791	        # In short-pool mode, reserve up to `hc_quota` HC rows
1792	        # (bounded by pool) — first one mirrors the 1-per-juris pass
1793	        # in the main branch, extras are the M-42d expansion.
1794	        hc_sorted = sorted(hc_rows, key=lambda s: (-s[1], s[0]))
1795	        target = min(hc_quota, len(hc_sorted))
1796	        for item in hc_sorted[:target]:
1797	            m42d_hc_ids.add(id(item))
1798	        m42d_hc_extras = max(0, len(m42d_hc_ids) - 1)
1799	        if m42d_hc_extras > 0:
1800	            notes.append(
1801	                f"m42d_hc_quota_expand hc_pool={len(hc_rows)} "
1802	                f"reserved={len(m42d_hc_ids)} "
1803	                f"extras_added={m42d_hc_extras} quota={hc_quota}"
1804	            )
1805	
1806	    # --- Deterministic priority ordering ---
1807	    # Priority class: 0 = M-42e primary OR M-51 anchor-primary extra,
1808	    # 1 = M-42c mechanism, 2 = M-42d HC, 3 = rest. Within same class:
1809	    # by tier priority, then -score, then index.
1810	    def _priority_class(item: tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]) -> int:
1811	        iid = id(item)
1812	        if iid in m42e_ids or iid in m51_extra_ids:
1813	            return 0
1814	        if iid in m42c_ids:
1815	            return 1
1816	        if iid in m42d_hc_ids:
1817	            return 2
1818	        return 3
1819	
1820	    # #955: recency is a SOFT tiebreaker AFTER priority-class + tier (both stay
1821	    # ahead, per Codex P2) and after the banded relevance — recency only orders
1822	    # within the same class/tier/relevance-band.
1823	    _rec_enabled = _recency_enabled()
1824	    _rec_eps = _recency_epsilon()
1825	    ordered = sorted(
1826	        scored,
1827	        key=lambda item: (
1828	            _priority_class(item),
1829	            _TIER_PRIORITY.get(item[2], 9),
1830	            *_relevance_recency_key(item, _rec_enabled, _rec_eps),
1831	            item[0],
1832	        ),
1833	    )
1834	    if _rec_enabled:
1835	        notes.append(_recency_telemetry_note(scored, _rec_eps))
1836	    selected_rows = [item[3] for item in ordered]
1837	    selected_counts = dict(full_counts)
1838	
1839	    return EvidenceSelection(
1840	        selected_rows=selected_rows,
1841	        full_counts=full_counts,
1842	        selected_counts=selected_counts,
1843	        dropped_count=0,
1844	        selection_strategy="tier_balanced_v1_all_m46_ordered",
1845	        notes=notes,
1846	    )

1871	#   #1231). OFF => byte-identical keep set. Faithfulness-safe: this can only ADD an

1877	    """Kill-switch `PG_RELEVANCE_HONEST_DROP` (default ON). When ON, the
1878	    relevance-floor selection logs the ACTUAL number of rows cut by the floor.
1879	    `=0`/`false`/`off`/`no` reverts to the prior no-log behavior. Telemetry-only:
1880	    NEVER changes which rows are kept or the returned `dropped_count`."""
1881	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_RELEVANCE_HONEST_DROP", "1").strip().lower()
1882	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")

1898	    off-topic, so it must NOT be dropped on the degenerate near-zero cosine its
1899	    empty content produces (BUG-14, GH #1262, §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER). The §-1.3-
1900	    correct behavior is the DEFAULT; the flag exists only so the exemption is

1902	    (byte-identical legacy floor filter)."""

1908	    """Floor `PG_SELECT_DEGENERATE_MIN_CHARS` (default 24). The minimum number of
1909	    embeddable content characters (statement + direct_quote, the SAME surface
1910	    `_row_embed_text` feeds the cosine scorer) below which a row is treated as a
1911	    degenerate / stub fetch — its near-zero embedding cannot support an "off-topic"
1912	    conclusion (BUG-14, GH #1262). Env-driven per LAW VI; a non-positive / malformed
1913	    value falls back to the default rather than disabling the direct-content guard
1914	    (fail-safe: an UNKNOWN-relevance row must never be silently dropped)."""
1915	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SELECT_DEGENERATE_MIN_CHARS", "").strip()
1916	    try:
1917	        val = int(raw)
1918	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
1919	        return _DEFAULT_SELECT_DEGENERATE_MIN_CHARS
1920	    return val if val > 0 else _DEFAULT_SELECT_DEGENERATE_MIN_CHARS

1923	def _fetch_degenerate(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
1924	    """BUG-14 (GH #1262, §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER): True iff this row is the product
1925	    of a FAILED / STUB / content-starved / landing-page fetch, i.e. its content is
1926	    UNKNOWN-relevance rather than genuinely off-topic.
1927	
1928	    Such a row produces a DEGENERATE embedding (empty / near-empty statement+quote
1929	    → near-zero, often identical, cosine — the drb_72 smoking gun: two different
1930	    journal URLs both logged cosine 0.0431 from identical empty input). The cosine
1931	    floor then HARD-DROPS it as if "off-topic", silently losing canonical T1
1932	    sources (Autor, Frey & Osborne — 24 of 166 on drb_72). But you CANNOT conclude
1933	    "off-topic" from empty content: §-1.3 lets the floor filter genuine off-topic
1934	    REAL content, never an unknown-because-unfetched row. So such rows are kept
1935	    (floor-EXEMPT), down-weighted to sort LAST (their near-zero cosine already does
1936	    this via the existing `relevance x authority x retrieval_weight` sort), and
1937	    flagged for re-fetch / disclosure (telemetry below) — disclosure, never a
1938	    SILENT drop. Faithfulness is untouched: this NEVER admits off-topic REAL
1939	    content (a non-empty below-floor row is still dropped) and never relaxes any
1940	    verify gate.
1941	
1942	    Detection, in precedence order:
1943	      1. Reliable UPSTREAM markers set by `live_retriever`'s §-1.3 down-weight path
1944	         (`content_starved` / `landing_page` / `down_weighted` / a falsy
1945	         `full_text_capable`) or a `fetch_failed` / `*_failed` `failure_mode` /
1946	         `fetch_failed` diagnostic that reached the row.
1947	      2. DIRECT content detection as the backstop for rows whose upstream lane did
1948	         NOT stamp a marker (e.g. the redesign flag was off, or a different
1949	         retrieval path): the embeddable text (`_row_embed_text`, the SAME field
1950	         set the cosine scorer reads) is empty or shorter than
1951	         `PG_SELECT_DEGENERATE_MIN_CHARS`. Empty embeddable text is exactly the
1952	         condition that produces the degenerate cosine.
1953	    """
1954	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
1955	        return False
1956	    # 1) Trust an explicit upstream degenerate / down-weight marker when present.
1957	    if row.get("content_starved") or row.get("landing_page") or row.get(
1958	        "down_weighted"
1959	    ):
1960	        return True
1961	    if "full_text_capable" in row and not row.get("full_text_capable"):
1962	        return True
1963	    _fail = str(
1964	        row.get("failure_mode") or row.get("fetch_status") or ""
1965	    ).strip().lower()
1966	    if _fail in ("fetch_failed", "exception", "timeout", "empty_url"):
1967	        return True
1968	    if row.get("fetch_failed"):
1969	        return True
1970	    # 2) Backstop: detect the degenerate condition directly on the row's own
1971	    # content surface (the text the cosine scorer embeds). Empty / too-short =>
1972	    # degenerate embedding => its near-zero cosine is NOT evidence of off-topic.
1973	    if len(_row_embed_text(row).strip()) < _select_degenerate_min_chars():
1974	        return True
1975	    return False

2006	# ── I-scope-001 (#1244): low-cred domain denylist (gate 1, pure, no LLM) ───────
2007	# Grounded diagnosis (do not re-derive): the drb_72 breadth run cited 76 distinct
2008	# sources, 0 fabrication, but ~9 were contamination — 3 of them low-credibility
2009	# domains (facebook.com, scribd.com, en.wikipedia.org) that the tier system
2010	# mis-tiered and the relevance floor passed on shared generic words. The tier
2011	# system rates CREDIBILITY but cannot demote a junk-host article that happens to
2012	# share content words with the topic. This gate drops candidate rows whose source
2013	# netloc matches an operator-supplied denylist. It is DEFAULT-OFF: the env var
2014	# `PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS` is empty by default, so `_scope_denylist_domains()`
2015	# returns () and `_apply_scope_denylist` is a byte-identical no-op (returns the
2016	# input list unchanged). Suggested-but-NOT-hardcoded-on default list (operator
2017	# pastes it into the env when desired):
2018	#   facebook.com,scribd.com,en.wikipedia.org,blogspot,wordpress,reddit,quora,medium.com
2019	# Deliberately does NOT denylist .gov / .edu / nber / doi.org (credibility is not
2020	# journal-only; gov + working-papers + institutes are kept). Marquee /
2021	# required-entity anchors are EXEMPT (never dropped). Faithfulness-safe: selection
2022	# can only SUBTRACT a candidate before generation; strict_verify / NLI / 4-role /
2023	# provenance are unchanged, and subtraction cannot fabricate.
2024	
2025	
2026	def _scope_denylist_domains() -> tuple[str, ...]:
2027	    """Parse `PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS` (comma-separated, default empty = OFF).
2028	    Each entry is lowercased + stripped; blank entries dropped. Empty env =>
2029	    `()` => the denylist gate is a byte-identical no-op."""
2030	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS", "").strip()
2031	    if not raw:
2032	        return ()
2033	    return tuple(
2034	        entry.strip().lower()
2035	        for entry in raw.split(",")
2036	        if entry.strip()
2037	    )
2038

2040	def _row_netloc(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
2041	    """Lowercased hostname for an evidence row's source URL. Reuses the
2042	    urlparse pattern already used elsewhere in this module (lines ~197/751)."""
2043	    url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip().lower()
2044	    if not url:
2045	        return ""
2046	    from urllib.parse import urlparse
2047	    try:
2048	        host = urlparse(
2049	            url if "://" in url else f"http://{url}"
2050	        ).hostname or ""
2051	    except Exception:
2052	        host = ""
2053	    return host.lower()
2054

2056	def _netloc_matches_denylist(netloc: str, denylist: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
2057	    """True iff a netloc matches a denylist entry.
2058	
2059	    Dotted entries (e.g. `facebook.com`, `en.wikipedia.org`) match on EXACT
2060	    netloc OR a `.`+entry suffix (so `m.facebook.com` matches but
2061	    `facebook.com.evil.org` does NOT). Bare-token entries (e.g. `blogspot`,
2062	    `reddit`) match on substring-in-netloc (catches `foo.blogspot.com`)."""
2063	    if not netloc or not denylist:
2064	        return False
2065	    for entry in denylist:
2066	        if "." in entry:
2067	            if netloc == entry or netloc.endswith("." + entry):
2068	                return True
2069	        else:
2070	            if entry in netloc:
2071	                return True
2072	    return False
2073

2075	def _apply_scope_denylist(
2076	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
2077	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None,
2078	) -> tuple[list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]], int, list[str]]:
2079	    """Drop scored rows whose netloc matches `PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS`.
2080	
2081	    EXEMPT: marquee / required-entity anchors AND named-trial primary anchors
2082	    (the same exemption set as the relevance floor). Returns
2083	    `(kept_scored, n_dropped, dropped_netlocs)`. When the env var is empty the
2084	    denylist is `()` and the input is returned UNCHANGED (byte-identical no-op).
2085	    Pure — does not mutate the caller's rows."""
2086	    denylist = _scope_denylist_domains()
2087	    if not denylist:
2088	        return scored, 0, []
2089	    anchors = list(primary_trial_anchors or [])
2090	
2091	    def _is_exempt(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
2092	        if _row_is_marquee_anchor(row):
2093	            return True
2094	        return any(_m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(row, a) for a in anchors)
2095	
2096	    # I-arch-002 (#1246) P-W2scope: under the master redesign flag the denylist becomes
2097	    # a credibility-CLASS WEIGHT, not a DROP (DNA §-1.3 — social/junk hosts STAY at low
2098	    # weight; they sometimes report a real journal). The matched row is KEPT, stamped
2099	    # `scope_denylist_demoted` + a low credibility class, and surfaced per-citation.
2100	    # OFF => the exact prior `continue`-drop => byte-identical.
2101	    _cred_redesign = _credibility_redesign_enabled()
2102	    kept: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]] = []
2103	    dropped_netlocs: list[str] = []
2104	    for item in scored:
2105	        row = item[3]
2106	        netloc = _row_netloc(row)
2107	        if _netloc_matches_denylist(netloc, denylist) and not _is_exempt(row):
2108	            if _cred_redesign:
2109	                demoted = dict(row)
2110	                demoted["scope_denylist_demoted"] = True
2111	                demoted["credibility_class"] = "low_denylist"
2112	                kept.append((item[0], item[1], item[2], demoted))
2113	                continue
2114	            dropped_netlocs.append(netloc)
2115	            continue
2116	        kept.append(item)
2117	    return kept, len(dropped_netlocs), dropped_netlocs

2121	# Grounded diagnosis: 2 of the 9 drb_72 contaminants were arXiv/preprint twins of
2122	# a paper that ALSO appears as a published journal/DOI row — the citation should
2123	# prefer the published version. This gate, when ON (`PG_SCOPE_PREFER_JOURNAL`,
2124	# default OFF), groups rows by NORMALIZED title and, for any title that appears as
2125	# BOTH an arxiv.org row AND a non-arxiv journal/DOI row, drops the arXiv twin(s)
2126	# and keeps the journal row. An arXiv row with NO journal twin is NEVER dropped
2127	# (two arXiv versions with no journal twin both survive). Default OFF => no-op.
