2224	def _relevance_floor_selection(
2225	    *,
2226	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
2227	    relevance_floor: float,
2228	    full_counts: dict[str, int],
2229	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None,
2230	    semantic_mode: bool = False,
2231	    semantic_requested: bool = False,
2232	    semantic_fell_back: bool = False,
2233	    url_to_content_relevance_weight: dict[str, float] | None = None,
2234	    url_to_date_weight: dict[str, float] | None = None,
2235	    url_to_scope_weight: dict[str, float] | None = None,
2236	    url_to_must_include: set[str] | None = None,
2237	) -> EvidenceSelection:
2238	    """I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989): relevance-floor selection (no max_rows cap).
2239	
2240	    Keep EVERY row whose lexical relevance >= ``relevance_floor``, PLUS any row
2241	    matching a primary trial anchor (floor-EXEMPT — a relevant primary RCT must
2242	    never be dropped on a low lexical score). Ranked by ``relevance x authority``
2243	    (authority = the row's ``authority_score`` sidecar, default 1.0). Each kept
2244	    row is stamped with the additive ``selection_relevance`` float so
2245	    ``finding_dedup`` picks representatives on the IDENTICAL score (no recompute
2246	    drift). Pure; returns SHALLOW COPIES (never mutates the caller's rows).
2247	
2248	    P0-A6 (I-arch-007, §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER): under the redesign (the coherent
2249	    DEFAULT, PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN unset/on) relevance is a WEIGHT, never a hard
2250	    FILTER — in BOTH semantic and lexical modes the keep set is ALL scored rows, and the
2251	    ``relevance x authority x retrieval_weight`` sort pushes a below-floor (low-cosine)
2252	    row LAST so off-topic noise sinks to the bottom of the pool WITHOUT being silently
2253	    dropped. ``semantic_mode``/``semantic_requested``/``semantic_fell_back`` are
2254	    telemetry/labelling only on this path. This supersedes the prior B1 logic, which had
2255	    RESTORED a hard below-floor DROP whenever the semantic scorer was requested — a
2256	    rank-then-DROP gate that re-introduced the FILTER-AND-CAP anti-pattern §-1.3 forbids.
2257	    The ONLY hard gate is the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role), untouched.
2258	
2259	    Legacy path (explicit PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN=0): the relevance FILTER
2260	    (``score >= floor OR floor-exempt``) runs unchanged in both lexical and semantic
2261	    modes — byte-identical regression behavior.

2642	# ── I-recency-001 (#1296): flag-gated cross-encoder rerank of the SELECTED set ──
2643	# A SELF-CONTAINED, default-OFF reorder applied ONLY at the FINAL return of the
2644	# tier-balanced truncating path in `select_evidence_for_generation`. When
2645	# `PG_RERANKER_MODEL` is set (qwen3 / qwen3-reranker-4b / 1 / true / on / yes), the
2646	# ALREADY-selected rows are reordered DESC by the recency-completion reranker pick
2647	# (Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-4B via `CrossEncoderConfig.from_env`) scored with its CANONICAL
2648	# causal-LM yes/no-logit method (`qwen_reranker_scorer.score_query_document_relevance` on
2649	# (research_question, statement+direct_quote)). NOTE: a Qwen3 reranker is an
2650	# AutoModelForCausalLM — loading it via `sentence_transformers.CrossEncoder` mints a RANDOM
2651	# score head and returns ~0.5 NOISE (the #1312 bug this path fixes). The scorer module is
2652	# LAZY-imported INSIDE the ON branch (no module-level torch/transformers import, nothing
2653	# loads when OFF).
2654	#
2655	# CONTRACT — pure IDENTITY no-op when OFF (default): same rows in, same list object
2656	# semantics out (`selected_rows` returned unchanged) so the tier-balanced selection
2657	# CONTRACT (counts, dropped_count) is byte-identical. When ON it REORDERS ONLY (same
2658	# set of rows, never adds/drops one) so the contract still holds — only generator
2659	# ORDER changes. A load/scoring failure FALLS BACK LOUDLY to the original order
2660	# (LAW II: no silent capability degrade; a reorder of an already-verified-downstream
2661	# set is faithfulness-safe — the original order is itself a valid result, and
2662	# strict_verify / the 4-role evaluator re-check every sentence regardless of order).
2663	# The GPU model loads ONLY during a real run; in offline tests the lazy import is
2664	# never reached (OFF) or is mocked (ON-path tests), so this is $0 to exercise.
2665	_RERANKER_ON_VALUES = frozenset(
2666	    {"qwen3", "qwen3-reranker-4b", "1", "true", "on", "yes"}
2667	)
2668	
2669	
2670	def _reranker_selection_enabled() -> bool:
2671	    """True iff `PG_RERANKER_MODEL` is an ON value. Default/unset => OFF (identity)."""
2672	    return os.environ.get("PG_RERANKER_MODEL", "").strip().lower() in _RERANKER_ON_VALUES

2675	def _maybe_rerank_selection(
2676	    selected_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
2677	    research_question: str,
2678	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
2679	    """Flag-gated causal-LM reranker reorder of the ALREADY-selected evidence rows.
2680	
2681	    OFF (default / `PG_RERANKER_MODEL` unset): returns ``selected_rows`` UNCHANGED
2682	    with NO import of torch/transformers — a pure identity no-op.
2683	
2684	    ON: reorders the SAME rows DESC by Qwen3-Reranker relevance to
2685	    ``research_question`` (scoring each row's ``statement + direct_quote`` — the same
2686	    text surface the lexical/semantic scorers read). Reorder only: the returned list
2687	    is a permutation of the input (same rows, none added or dropped). Any
2688	    load/scoring failure FALLS BACK LOUDLY to the original order.
2689	    """
2690	    if not _reranker_selection_enabled():
2691	        return selected_rows  # identity — no model import, no spend
2692	    if len(selected_rows) <= 1:
2693	        return selected_rows  # nothing to reorder
2694	    global _W7_RERANKER_LOAD_FAILED
2695	    try:
2696	        # LAZY import — nothing (incl. torch/transformers) loads unless the flag is ON.
2697	        # The reranker WINNER (Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-4B, I-recency-001 #1296) is an
2698	        # AutoModelForCausalLM, NOT a sequence-classification model. Loading it through
2699	        # sentence_transformers.CrossEncoder mints a FRESH RANDOM classification head ->
2700	        # encoder.predict() returns ~0.5 NOISE (the winner was effectively not running;
2701	        # I-bug-reranker-noise #1312). The canonical bake-off method scores P("yes") via the
2702	        # causal-LM next-token "yes"/"no" logits — that is what qwen_reranker_scorer does.
2703	        # I-deepfix-001 P1-1 (#1344): a FAILURE of THIS lazy import is the STRUCTURAL
2704	        # W7 load failure the winner-firing gate hard-gates on — captured below in the
2705	        # `except` as `_W7_RERANKER_LOAD_FAILED=True`. A scoring/permutation failure
2706	        # later in this block is a transient (the model DID load) and must NOT flip
2707	        # the structural signal — see the per-stage handling below.
2708	        from src.config.core import CrossEncoderConfig
2709	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.qwen_reranker_scorer import (
2710	            RerankerLoadError,
2711	            score_query_document_relevance,
2712	        )
2713	
2714	        _ce_cfg = CrossEncoderConfig.from_env()
2715	        model_name = _ce_cfg.model
2716	        # Honor a configured device override (PG_*/YAML). Precedence (I-deepfix-001 P0-3):
2717	        # the NEW launch-env knob ``PG_RERANKER_DEVICE`` wins (the deepfix static 2-card
2718	        # split pins W7 to cuda:1 via this read), then the YAML/config ``device``, then the
2719	        # scorer's GPU-first auto-resolve. The "auto" config default is the SENTINEL for
2720	        # "let the scorer auto-resolve" (cuda-if-available else cpu) — pass None for it, NOT
2721	        # the literal "auto" (which would crash .to("auto")). PG_RERANKER_DEVICE is a
2722	        # LAUNCH-ENV read (not a slate force-ON value), so it does not trip SLATE-PURITY /
2723	        # NO-LOSER (those gate winner force-ON values, not device reads).
2724	        device_override = (
2725	            os.getenv("PG_RERANKER_DEVICE", "").strip()
2726	            or (_ce_cfg.device if (_ce_cfg.device and _ce_cfg.device != "auto") else None)
2727	        )
2728	        documents = [
2729	            " ".join(
2730	                str(row.get(k, "") or "")
2731	                for k in ("statement", "direct_quote")
2732	            )
2733	            for row in selected_rows
2734	        ]
2735	        # I-deepfix-001 P1-1 (#1344): the scorer raises a TYPED RerankerLoadError ONLY
2736	        # for a structural load failure (import / CUDA / from_pretrained / .to(device) /
2737	        # token-id resolution). That is the structural W7-dark signal the winner-firing
2738	        # gate hard-gates on → mark the sticky module signal True. A FORWARD-PASS
2739	        # exception (a transient encode/OOM) surfaces as a PLAIN exception and is NOT
2740	        # caught here — it propagates to the OUTER except as a transient and must NOT
2741	        # flip the sticky structural signal (winner_firing_gate: "never a single
2742	        # transient encode exception"; mirrors the embedder structural-vs-transient
2743	        # split). A SUCCESSFUL return proves the model loaded AND fired → mark not-dark.
2744	        try:
2745	            scores = score_query_document_relevance(
2746	                research_question or "",
2747	                documents,
2748	                model_id=model_name,
2749	                device=device_override,
2750	            )
2751	        except RerankerLoadError as load_exc:  # STRUCTURAL load failure only
2752	            _W7_RERANKER_LOAD_FAILED = True
2753	            _LOGGER.warning(
2754	                "[select] PG_RERANKER_MODEL set but the Qwen3-Reranker causal-LM "
2755	                "STRUCTURALLY FAILED TO LOAD (%s) — W7 dark; FELL BACK LOUDLY to the "
2756	                "tier-balanced order (no reorder). The winner-firing gate will surface "
2757	                "this. Not a silent degrade: the original order is a valid result.",
2758	                str(load_exc)[:200],
2759	            )
2760	            return selected_rows
2761	        # The model loaded and produced scores — W7 fired (not structurally dark).
2762	        _W7_RERANKER_LOAD_FAILED = False
2763	        # Stable DESC sort by score; ties keep the input (tier-balanced) order.
2764	        order = sorted(
2765	            range(len(selected_rows)),
2766	            key=lambda i: (-float(scores[i]), i),
2767	        )
2768	        reranked = [selected_rows[i] for i in order]
2769	        # Defensive invariant: the rerank is a pure permutation (no add/drop).
2770	        if len(reranked) != len(selected_rows):
2771	            _LOGGER.warning(
2772	                "[select] reranker returned %d rows for a %d-row selection — "
2773	                "FALLING BACK to the original order (reorder must be a permutation).",
2774	                len(reranked), len(selected_rows),
2775	            )
2776	            return selected_rows
2777	        return reranked
2778	    except Exception as exc:  # transient: a post-load forward/permutation/config failure
2779	        # NOTE: a STRUCTURAL load failure is handled by the inner `except RerankerLoadError`
2780	        # above and has ALREADY set `_W7_RERANKER_LOAD_FAILED=True`. This outer handler
2781	        # covers a config read (CrossEncoderConfig.from_env), a per-document FORWARD-PASS
2782	        # exception (a transient encode/OOM — the model DID load), or a permutation/sort
2783	        # hiccup AFTER the model loaded — all transients that must NOT flip the sticky
2784	        # structural signal (winner_firing_gate: "never a single transient encode
2785	        # exception"). The signal is left untouched (None on the never-loaded path, or
2786	        # its prior value), so a one-off forward-pass error cannot poison later queries.
2787	        _LOGGER.warning(
2788	            "[select] PG_RERANKER_MODEL set but the Qwen3-Reranker causal-LM rerank failed "
2789	            "(%s) — FELL BACK LOUDLY to the tier-balanced order (no reorder). This "
2790	            "is not a silent degrade: the original order is a valid result.",
2791	            str(exc)[:200],
2792	        )
2793	        return selected_rows

2796	def select_evidence_for_generation(
2797	    *,
2798	    research_question: str,
2799	    protocol: dict[str, Any] | None,

2800	    classified_sources: list[Any],
2801	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
2802	    max_rows: int,
2803	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None = None,
2804	    relevance_floor: float | None = None,
2805	    sub_queries: list[str] | None = None,
2806	) -> EvidenceSelection:
2807	    """Public entry: run the selection, then GUARANTEE the date-window ordering on
2808	    the FINAL result regardless of which internal branch produced it (Codex wave-2
2809	    P1c: the demotion/tail partition was previously wired ONLY into the
2810	    relevance-floor branch, so the tier-capped / short-pool paths — used when
2811	    relevance_floor=None, incl. the finding-dedup helper — could rank an
2812	    out-of-window source ahead of an in-window one). The finalizer is month-aware,
2813	    KEEPS every row (§-1.3 demote-not-drop), idempotent (re-running on an already-
2814	    partitioned floor-path result is a no-op), and FAIL-OPEN (never breaks
2815	    selection on a finalizer error; no window => same result object)."""
2816	    result = _select_evidence_for_generation_impl(
2817	        research_question=research_question,
2818	        protocol=protocol,
2819	        classified_sources=classified_sources,
2820	        evidence_rows=evidence_rows,
2821	        max_rows=max_rows,
2822	        primary_trial_anchors=primary_trial_anchors,
2823	        relevance_floor=relevance_floor,
2824	        sub_queries=sub_queries,
2825	    )
2826	    try:
2827	        _s_idx, _e_idx = _ym_window_bounds(protocol)
2828	        if (_s_idx is None and _e_idx is None) or not getattr(result, "selected_rows", None):
2829	            return result
2830	        _oow = {
2831	            str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "")
2832	            for r in result.selected_rows
2833	            if isinstance(r, dict) and _row_out_of_window_ym(r, _s_idx, _e_idx)
2834	        }
2835	        _oow.discard("")
2836	        # I-scope-001 P1 (iter-3): a user-INCLUDED (pinned) source that is ALSO out-of-window
2837	        # must STAY pinned in the FINAL public output — include wins over the date demote for
2838	        # the SAME source (include != prefer; §-1.3 include=boost-not-demote). The internal
2839	        # floor path already exempts the pins (`(_oow_urls | _oos_urls) - _must_include_urls`);
2840	        # this finalizer runs AFTER it, so it must apply the SAME exemption or it re-sinks the
2841	        # pinned source to the tail (un-pinning it). Derived from the SAME pure plan builder as
2842	        # the impl; fail-open (never breaks the date-window finalizer) + gated behind
2843	        # PG_SCOPE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCE => empty set => byte-identical when OFF / no include facet.
2844	        try:
2845	            from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.constraint_enforcement import (  # noqa: PLC0415
2846	                build_scope_enforcement,
2847	            )
2848	            _oow -= set(build_scope_enforcement(protocol, evidence_rows).must_include_urls)
2849	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — pin-exemption is fail-open (falls back to no exemption)
2850	            pass
2851	        if not _oow:
2852	            return result
2853	        _parted = _partition_out_of_window_last(result.selected_rows, _oow)
2854	        _already_noted = any("date-window" in str(n).lower() for n in result.notes)
2855	        if _parted == result.selected_rows and _already_noted:
2856	            return result  # floor-path already enforced + disclosed → no-op
2857	        _notes = list(result.notes)
2858	        if not _already_noted:
2859	            _notes.append(
2860	                f"B10 date-window: {len(_oow)} out-of-window source(s) sorted "
2861	                "LAST (kept — §-1.3 demote-not-drop; hard user date ceiling)."
2862	            )
2863	        return replace(result, selected_rows=_parted, notes=_notes)
2864	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — date-window finalizer is fail-open
2865	        return result

2868	def _select_evidence_for_generation_impl(
2869	    *,
2870	    research_question: str,
2871	    protocol: dict[str, Any] | None,
2872	    classified_sources: list[Any],
2873	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
2874	    max_rows: int,
2875	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None = None,
2876	    relevance_floor: float | None = None,
2877	    sub_queries: list[str] | None = None,
2878	) -> EvidenceSelection:
2879	    """Pick up to max_rows evidence rows, tier-balanced + relevance-ranked.
2880	
2881	    Strategy (deterministic, stable):
2882	      1. Join evidence rows to classified sources by URL → tier per row.
2883	      2. Compute full_tier_counts.
2884	      3. Score each row by lexical relevance (question+protocol anchors
2885	         vs statement+direct_quote content words).
2886	      4. Sort within each tier by (-relevance, original_index) for
2887	         deterministic top-N per tier.
2888	      5. Allocate slots proportionally to full_tier_counts but with
2889	         floors for high-value tiers that are present (each T1-T3 tier
2890	         present gets at least min(1, available)).
2891	      6. Fill remaining slots globally by (tier_priority, -relevance,
2892	         original_index).
2893	
2894	    Args:
2895	        research_question: the user's raw question.
2896	        protocol: scope protocol dict (uses population/intervention/
2897	            comparator/outcome/research_question if present).
2898	        classified_sources: list of objects with .url and .tier (or
2899	            dict-like with those keys). Used for URL→tier join.
2900	        evidence_rows: full evidence row pool.
2901	        max_rows: target selection size.
2902	
2903	    Returns:
2904	        EvidenceSelection with selected_rows + telemetry.
2905	    """
2906	    # I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989) — Codex diff-gate P2: in relevance-floor mode the
2907	    # `max_rows` cap is REPLACED by the floor, so a legacy `max_rows <= 0` must NOT
2908	    # empty the ON-mode pool. The empty-corpus guard still applies in both modes.
2909	    if (max_rows <= 0 and relevance_floor is None) or not evidence_rows:
2910	        return EvidenceSelection(
2911	            selected_rows=[], full_counts={}, selected_counts={},
2912	            dropped_count=0, selection_strategy="tier_balanced_v1",
2913	            notes=["no evidence rows or max_rows <= 0"],
2914	        )
2915	
2916	    # Build URL → tier map from classified_sources (whatever shape).
2917	    url_to_tier: dict[str, str] = {}
2918	    # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311) P1-2 keystone: also join the per-source CONTENT-
2919	    # RELEVANCE WEIGHT carried on each classified source (CorpusSource.
2920	    # content_relevance_weight, default 1.0 = W2 OFF / full weight). This is the
2921	    # ACTUAL evidence-selection seam — the W2 weight was previously orphaned
2922	    # (set on CorpusSource + used only to reorder classified_sources, but NEVER
2923	    # read by row scoring), so demoted junk still entered evidence_for_gen at
2924	    # full selection weight. We surface the weight here and MULTIPLY it into the
2925	    # ranking sort (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER: a low-relevance passage is DEMOTED
2926	    # to the bottom of the pool, NEVER hard-dropped; retention stays 1.0). When
2927	    # W2 is OFF every weight is 1.0 so the map is all-default and the sort key is
2928	    # byte-identical.
2929	    url_to_content_relevance_weight: dict[str, float] = {}
2930	    for src in classified_sources or []:
2931	        url = getattr(src, "url", None) or (
2932	            src.get("url") if isinstance(src, dict) else None
2933	        )
2934	        tier = getattr(src, "tier", None) or (
2935	            src.get("tier") if isinstance(src, dict) else None
2936	        )
2937	        if url and tier:
2938	            url_to_tier[str(url)] = str(tier)
2939	        if url is not None:
2940	            _crw = getattr(src, "content_relevance_weight", None)
2941	            if _crw is None and isinstance(src, dict):
2942	                _crw = src.get("content_relevance_weight")
2943	            if _crw is not None:
2944	                try:
2945	                    _crwf = float(_crw)
2946	                except (TypeError, ValueError):
2947	                    _crwf = 1.0
2948	                # Clamp to (0, 1]: a demoted source is KEPT at low weight, never
2949	                # zero/dropped (§-1.3). Only record a non-default weight so an
2950	                # all-default (W2-OFF) map stays empty => byte-identical sort.
2951	                if 0.0 < _crwf < 1.0:
2952	                    url_to_content_relevance_weight[str(url)] = _crwf
2953	
2954	    # I-deepfix-001 B10(d) (#1352): build the per-URL DATE-WINDOW demotion map from
2955	    # the user date constraint in protocol['date_range'] (populated by B10(a) intake
2956	    # extraction). An out-of-window source (explicit publication year outside the
2957	    # window) is DEMOTED (weight < 1.0) so it sorts LAST in the selected set, and an
2958	    # exclusion record is written to telemetry — it is KEPT in the corpus, NEVER
2959	    # hard-dropped (§-1.3). Undated rows are never in the map (fail-open). When no
2960	    # window is set the map is empty => sort byte-identical. `_date_window_disable_
2961	    # recency` flips recency OFF when a MAX-date is present (newer-first fights the
2962	    # ceiling — the direct B10 bug).
2963	    _date_start_year, _date_end_year = _protocol_date_window(protocol)
2964	    # MONTH-precision bounds (Codex wave-2 P1): enforce a sub-year ceiling when the
2965	    # window carries a month; year-only bounds reduce to the prior year comparison.
2966	    _date_start_idx, _date_end_idx = _ym_window_bounds(protocol)
2967	    url_to_date_weight: dict[str, float] = {}
2968	    _oow_urls: set[str] = set()
2969	    _date_excluded_records: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
2970	    _date_window_disable_recency = _date_end_idx is not None
2971	    if _date_start_idx is not None or _date_end_idx is not None:
2972	        _dw = _date_demote_weight()
2973	        for _r in evidence_rows:
2974	            if not isinstance(_r, dict):
2975	                continue
2976	            if _row_out_of_window_ym(_r, _date_start_idx, _date_end_idx):
2977	                _u = str(_r.get("source_url") or _r.get("url") or "")
2978	                if _u:
2979	                    url_to_date_weight[_u] = _dw
2980	                    _oow_urls.add(_u)
2981	                _pub = _row_pub_ym(_r)
2982	                _date_excluded_records.append({
2983	                    "source_url": _u,
2984	                    "year": _row_year(_r),
2985	                    "pub_ym": (f"{_pub[0]:04d}-{_pub[1]:02d}"
2986	                               if _pub and _pub[1] else
2987	                               (str(_pub[0]) if _pub else None)),
2988	                    "window": [_date_start_year, _date_end_year],
2989	                    "action": "demoted_out_of_window_kept",
2990	                    "weight": _dw,
2991	                })
2992	        if _date_excluded_records:
2993	            _LOGGER.info(
2994	                "[select] B10 date-window [%s..%s]: DEMOTED %d out-of-window "
2995	                "source(s) (kept at weight=%.2f, sorted last — §-1.3 disclose, "
2996	                "NOT dropped); recency_tiebreak disabled=%s",
2997	                _date_start_year, _date_end_year, len(_date_excluded_records),
2998	                _date_demote_weight(), _date_window_disable_recency,
2999	            )
3000	
3001	    # I-scope-001: build the per-URL SCOPE-WEIGHT demotion map — the GENERALIZATION of the
3002	    # date-window WEIGHT seam above to arbitrary user-stated source-type / jurisdiction
3003	    # facets. Gated PG_SCOPE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCE (default OFF) inside build_scope_enforcement
3004	    # => an empty plan => `url_to_scope_weight` empty + `_oos_urls` empty => the sort key +
3005	    # tail partition are BYTE-IDENTICAL. A prefer/weight-demoted out-of-scope source sinks
3006	    # LAST while staying KEPT (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER). The HARD grounding mask lives at the
3007	    # billed-set seam (run_honest_sweep_r3 ~13063), NOT here.
3008	    url_to_scope_weight: dict[str, float] = {}
3009	    _oos_urls: set[str] = set()
3010	    _must_include_urls: set[str] = set()
3011	    try:
3012	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.constraint_enforcement import (  # noqa: PLC0415
3013	            build_scope_enforcement,
3014	        )
3015	        _scope_plan = build_scope_enforcement(protocol, evidence_rows)
3016	        url_to_scope_weight = dict(_scope_plan.url_to_scope_weight)
3017	        _oos_urls = set(_scope_plan.out_of_scope_urls)
3018	        # I-scope-001 P1 fix: the user-INCLUDED URLs (op='include' / named-include). These
3019	        # get an ADDITIVE PIN into the SELECTED set (front of the order, floor-exempt) —
3020	        # they NEVER demote a non-matching source (include != prefer). Empty when no
3021	        # include facet OR PG_SCOPE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCE OFF => byte-identical selection.
3022	        _must_include_urls = set(_scope_plan.must_include_urls)
3023	        if url_to_scope_weight:
3024	            _LOGGER.info(
3025	                "[select] I-scope-001: DEMOTED %d out-of-scope source(s) "
3026	                "(kept, sorted last — §-1.3 disclose, NOT dropped)",
3027	                len(url_to_scope_weight),
3028	            )
3029	        if _must_include_urls:
3030	            _LOGGER.info(
3031	                "[select] I-scope-001: PINNED %d user-included source(s) into the "
3032	                "selected grounding set (additive boost — non-matching NOT demoted)",
3033	                len(_must_include_urls),
3034	            )
3035	    except Exception as _scope_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - fail-open: scope never aborts selection
3036	        _LOGGER.warning("[select] scope enforcement skipped (%s)", _scope_exc)
3037	        url_to_scope_weight = {}
3038	        _oos_urls = set()
3039	        _must_include_urls = set()
3040	
3041	    # Compute relevance tokens from research question + protocol anchors.
3042	    question_tokens = _content_tokens(research_question or "")

content.text:
`_relevance_honest_drop_enabled` — this is a real functional term used consistently as a flag name throughout (matching the env var `PG_RELEVANCE_HONEST_DROP`), describing accurate/non-laundered telemetry vs. a marketing adjective. It's borderline but used as a genuine technical distinction (honest vs laundered counts) — I'll flag it as KEEP-NOTED since "honest" reads informally in an external review even though it's a real distinguishing concept here.

Let's continue reading remaining chunks of the file for other issues (variable/function names not caught by the regex, e.g., odd abbreviations).
